39:3:15: And Jesus answering said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Suffer it to be so now: for thus it becometh us to fulfil all righteousness.</font> Then he suffered him.
39:4:4: But he answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">It is written, Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of God.</font>
39:4:7: Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">It is written again, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.</font>
39:4:10: Then saith Jesus unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Get thee hence, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.</font>
39:4:17: From that time Jesus began to preach, and to say, <font color="#CC0000">Repent: for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.</font>
39:4:19: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men.</font>
39:5:3: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are the poor in spirit: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.</font>
39:5:4: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted.</font>
39:5:5: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are the meek: for they shall inherit the earth.</font>
39:5:6: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness: for they shall be filled.</font>
39:5:7: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are the merciful: for they shall obtain mercy.</font>
39:5:8: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.</font>
39:5:9: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.</font>
39:5:10: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness' sake: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.</font>
39:5:11: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are ye, when men shall revile you, and persecute you, and shall say all manner of evil against you falsely, for my sake.</font>
39:5:12: <font color="#CC0000">Rejoice, and be exceeding glad: for great is your reward in heaven: for so persecuted they the prophets which were before you.</font>
39:5:13: <font color="#CC0000">Ye are the salt of the earth: but if the salt have lost his savour, wherewith shall it be salted? it is thenceforth good for nothing, but to be cast out, and to be trodden under foot of men.</font>
39:5:14: <font color="#CC0000">Ye are the light of the world. A city that is set on an hill cannot be hid.</font>
39:5:15: <font color="#CC0000">Neither do men light a candle, and put it under a bushel, but on a candlestick; and it giveth light unto all that are in the house.</font>
39:5:16: <font color="#CC0000">Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father which is in heaven.</font>
39:5:17: <font color="#CC0000">Think not that I am come to destroy the law, or the prophets: I am not come to destroy, but to fulfil.</font>
39:5:18: <font color="#CC0000">For verily I say unto you, Till heaven and earth pass, one jot or one tittle shall in no wise pass from the law, till all be fulfilled.</font>
39:5:19: <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever therefore shall break one of these least commandments, and shall teach men so, he shall be called the least in the kingdom of heaven: but whosoever shall do and teach them, the same shall be called great in the kingdom of heaven.</font>
39:5:20: <font color="#CC0000">For I say unto you, That except your righteousness shall exceed the righteousness of the scribes and Pharisees, ye shall in no case enter into the kingdom of heaven.</font>
39:5:21: <font color="#CC0000">Ye have heard that it was said by them of old time, Thou shalt not kill; and whosoever shall kill shall be in danger of the judgment:</font>
39:5:22: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, That whosoever is angry with his brother without a cause shall be in danger of the judgment: and whosoever shall say to his brother, Raca, shall be in danger of the council: but whosoever shall say, Thou fool, shall be in danger of hell fire.</font>
39:5:23: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore if thou bring thy gift to the altar, and there rememberest that thy brother hath ought against thee;</font>
39:5:24: <font color="#CC0000">Leave there thy gift before the altar, and go thy way; first be reconciled to thy brother, and then come and offer thy gift.</font>
39:5:25: <font color="#CC0000">Agree with thine adversary quickly, whiles thou art in the way with him; lest at any time the adversary deliver thee to the judge, and the judge deliver thee to the officer, and thou be cast into prison.</font>
39:5:26: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto thee, Thou shalt by no means come out thence, till thou hast paid the uttermost farthing.</font>
39:5:27: <font color="#CC0000">Ye have heard that it was said by them of old time, Thou shalt not commit adultery:</font>
39:5:28: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, That whosoever looketh on a woman to lust after her hath committed adultery with her already in his heart.</font>
39:5:29: <font color="#CC0000">And if thy right eye offend thee, pluck it out, and cast it from thee: for it is profitable for thee that one of thy members should perish, and not that thy whole body should be cast into hell.</font>
39:5:30: <font color="#CC0000">And if thy right hand offend thee, cut it off, and cast it from thee: for it is profitable for thee that one of thy members should perish, and not that thy whole body should be cast into hell.</font>
39:5:31: <font color="#CC0000">It hath been said, Whosoever shall put away his wife, let him give her a writing of divorcement:</font>
39:5:32: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, That whosoever shall put away his wife, saving for the cause of fornication, causeth her to commit adultery: and whosoever shall marry her that is divorced committeth adultery.</font>
39:5:33: <font color="#CC0000">Again, ye have heard that it hath been said by them of old time, Thou shalt not forswear thyself, but shalt perform unto the Lord thine oaths:</font>
39:5:34: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, Swear not at all; neither by heaven; for it is God's throne:</font>
39:5:35: <font color="#CC0000">Nor by the earth; for it is his footstool: neither by Jerusalem; for it is the city of the great King.</font>
39:5:36: <font color="#CC0000">Neither shalt thou swear by thy head, because thou canst not make one hair white or black.</font>
39:5:37: <font color="#CC0000">But let your communication be, Yea, yea; Nay, nay: for whatsoever is more than these cometh of evil.</font>
39:5:38: <font color="#CC0000">Ye have heard that it hath been said, An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth:</font>
39:5:39: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, That ye resist not evil: but whosoever shall smite thee on thy right cheek, turn to him the other also.</font>
39:5:40: <font color="#CC0000">And if any man will sue thee at the law, and take away thy coat, let him have thy cloke also.</font>
39:5:41: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever shall compel thee to go a mile, go with him twain.</font>
39:5:42: <font color="#CC0000">Give to him that asketh thee, and from him that would borrow of thee turn not thou away.</font>
39:5:43: <font color="#CC0000">Ye have heard that it hath been said, Thou shalt love thy neighbour, and hate thine enemy.</font>
39:5:44: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, Love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you, and pray for them which despitefully use you, and persecute you;</font>
39:5:45: <font color="#CC0000">That ye may be the children of your Father which is in heaven: for he maketh his sun to rise on the evil and on the good, and sendeth rain on the just and on the unjust.</font>
39:5:46: <font color="#CC0000">For if ye love them which love you, what reward have ye? do not even the publicans the same?</font>
39:5:47: <font color="#CC0000">And if ye salute your brethren only, what do ye more than others? do not even the publicans so?</font>
39:5:48: <font color="#CC0000">Be ye therefore perfect, even as your Father which is in heaven is perfect.</font>
39:6:1: <font color="#CC0000">Take heed that ye do not your alms before men, to be seen of them: otherwise ye have no reward of your Father which is in heaven.</font>
39:6:2: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore when thou doest thine alms, do not sound a trumpet before thee, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, that they may have glory of men. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward.</font>
39:6:3: <font color="#CC0000">But when thou doest alms, let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doeth:</font>
39:6:4: <font color="#CC0000">That thine alms may be in secret: and thy Father which seeth in secret himself shall reward thee openly.</font>
39:6:5: <font color="#CC0000">And when thou prayest, thou shalt not be as the hypocrites are: for they love to pray standing in the synagogues and in the corners of the streets, that they may be seen of men. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward.</font>
39:6:6: <font color="#CC0000">But thou, when thou prayest, enter into thy closet, and when thou hast shut thy door, pray to thy Father which is in secret; and thy Father which seeth in secret shall reward thee openly.</font>
39:6:7: <font color="#CC0000">But when ye pray, use not vain repetitions, as the heathen do: for they think that they shall be heard for their much speaking.</font>
39:6:8: <font color="#CC0000">Be not ye therefore like unto them: for your Father knoweth what things ye have need of, before ye ask him.</font>
39:6:9: <font color="#CC0000">After this manner therefore pray ye: Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name.</font>
39:6:10: <font color="#CC0000">Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done in earth, as it is in heaven.</font>
39:6:11: <font color="#CC0000">Give us this day our daily bread.</font>
39:6:12: <font color="#CC0000">And forgive us our debts, as we forgive our debtors.</font>
39:6:13: <font color="#CC0000">And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil: For thine is the kingdom, and the power, and the glory, for ever. Amen.</font>
39:6:14: <font color="#CC0000">For if ye forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you:</font>
39:6:15: <font color="#CC0000">But if ye forgive not men their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.</font>
39:6:16: <font color="#CC0000">Moreover when ye fast, be not, as the hypocrites, of a sad countenance: for they disfigure their faces, that they may appear unto men to fast. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward.</font>
39:6:17: <font color="#CC0000">But thou, when thou fastest, anoint thine head, and wash thy face;</font>
39:6:18: <font color="#CC0000">That thou appear not unto men to fast, but unto thy Father which is in secret: and thy Father, which seeth in secret, shall reward thee openly.</font>
39:6:19: <font color="#CC0000">Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon earth, where moth and rust doth corrupt, and where thieves break through and steal:</font>
39:6:20: <font color="#CC0000">But lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust doth corrupt, and where thieves do not break through nor steal:</font>
39:6:21: <font color="#CC0000">For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.</font>
39:6:22: <font color="#CC0000">The light of the body is the eye: if therefore thine eye be single, thy whole body shall be full of light.</font>
39:6:23: <font color="#CC0000">But if thine eye be evil, thy whole body shall be full of darkness. If therefore the light that is in thee be darkness, how great is that darkness!</font>
39:6:24: <font color="#CC0000">No man can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and mammon.</font>
39:6:25: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore I say unto you, Take no thought for your life, what ye shall eat, or what ye shall drink; nor yet for your body, what ye shall put on. Is not the life more than meat, and the body than raiment?</font>
39:6:26: <font color="#CC0000">Behold the fowls of the air: for they sow not, neither do they reap, nor gather into barns; yet your heavenly Father feedeth them. Are ye not much better than they?</font>
39:6:27: <font color="#CC0000">Which of you by taking thought can add one cubit unto his stature?</font>
39:6:28: <font color="#CC0000">And why take ye thought for raiment? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow; they toil not, neither do they spin:</font>
39:6:29: <font color="#CC0000">And yet I say unto you, That even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.</font>
39:6:30: <font color="#CC0000">Wherefore, if God so clothe the grass of the field, which to day is, and to morrow is cast into the oven, shall he not much more clothe you, O ye of little faith?</font>
39:6:31: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore take no thought, saying, What shall we eat? or, What shall we drink? or, Wherewithal shall we be clothed?</font>
39:6:32: <font color="#CC0000">(For after all these things do the Gentiles seek:) for your heavenly Father knoweth that ye have need of all these things.</font>
39:6:33: <font color="#CC0000">But seek ye first the kingdom of God, and his righteousness; and all these things shall be added unto you.</font>
39:6:34: <font color="#CC0000">Take therefore no thought for the morrow: for the morrow shall take thought for the things of itself. Sufficient unto the day is the evil thereof.</font>
39:7:1: <font color="#CC0000">Judge not, that ye be not judged.</font>
39:7:2: <font color="#CC0000">For with what judgment ye judge, ye shall be judged: and with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you again.</font>
39:7:3: <font color="#CC0000">And why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brother's eye, but considerest not the beam that is in thine own eye?</font>
39:7:4: <font color="#CC0000">Or how wilt thou say to thy brother, Let me pull out the mote out of thine eye; and, behold, a beam is in thine own eye?</font>
39:7:5: <font color="#CC0000">Thou hypocrite, first cast out the beam out of thine own eye; and then shalt thou see clearly to cast out the mote out of thy brother's eye.</font>
39:7:6: <font color="#CC0000">Give not that which is holy unto the dogs, neither cast ye your pearls before swine, lest they trample them under their feet, and turn again and rend you.</font>
39:7:7: <font color="#CC0000">Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you:</font>
39:7:8: <font color="#CC0000">For every one that asketh receiveth; and he that seeketh findeth; and to him that knocketh it shall be opened.</font>
39:7:9: <font color="#CC0000">Or what man is there of you, whom if his son ask bread, will he give him a stone?</font>
39:7:10: <font color="#CC0000">Or if he ask a fish, will he give him a serpent?</font>
39:7:11: <font color="#CC0000">If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children, how much more shall your Father which is in heaven give good things to them that ask him?</font>
39:7:12: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore all things whatsoever ye would that men should do to you, do ye even so to them: for this is the law and the prophets.</font>
39:7:13: <font color="#CC0000">Enter ye in at the strait gate: for wide is the gate, and broad is the way, that leadeth to destruction, and many there be which go in thereat:</font>
39:7:14: <font color="#CC0000">Because strait is the gate, and narrow is the way, which leadeth unto life, and few there be that find it.</font>
39:7:15: <font color="#CC0000">Beware of false prophets, which come to you in sheep's clothing, but inwardly they are ravening wolves.</font>
39:7:16: <font color="#CC0000">Ye shall know them by their fruits. Do men gather grapes of thorns, or figs of thistles?</font>
39:7:17: <font color="#CC0000">Even so every good tree bringeth forth good fruit; but a corrupt tree bringeth forth evil fruit.</font>
39:7:18: <font color="#CC0000">A good tree cannot bring forth evil fruit, neither can a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.</font>
39:7:19: <font color="#CC0000">Every tree that bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down, and cast into the fire.</font>
39:7:20: <font color="#CC0000">Wherefore by their fruits ye shall know them.</font>
39:7:21: <font color="#CC0000">Not every one that saith unto me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdom of heaven; but he that doeth the will of my Father which is in heaven.</font>
39:7:22: <font color="#CC0000">Many will say to me in that day, Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied in thy name? and in thy name have cast out devils? and in thy name done many wonderful works?</font>
39:7:23: <font color="#CC0000">And then will I profess unto them, I never knew you: depart from me, ye that work iniquity.</font>
39:7:24: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore whosoever heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them, I will liken him unto a wise man, which built his house upon a rock:</font>
39:7:25: <font color="#CC0000">And the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell not: for it was founded upon a rock.</font>
39:7:26: <font color="#CC0000">And every one that heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them not, shall be likened unto a foolish man, which built his house upon the sand:</font>
39:7:27: <font color="#CC0000">And the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell: and great was the fall of it.</font>
39:8:3: And Jesus put forth his hand, and touched him, saying, <font color="#CC0000">I will; be thou clean.</font> And immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
39:8:4: And Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">See thou tell no man; but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.</font>
39:8:7: And Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">I will come and heal him.</font>
39:8:10: When Jesus heard it, he marvelled, and said to them that followed, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel.</font>
39:8:11: <font color="#CC0000">And I say unto you, That many shall come from the east and west, and shall sit down with Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, in the kingdom of heaven.</font>
39:8:12: <font color="#CC0000">But the children of the kingdom shall be cast out into outer darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.</font>
39:8:13: And Jesus said unto the centurion, <font color="#CC0000">Go thy way; and as thou hast believed, so be it done unto thee. And his servant was healed in the selfsame hour.</font>
39:8:20: And Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">The foxes have holes, and the birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay his head.</font>
39:8:22: But Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Follow me; and let the dead bury their dead.</font>
39:8:26: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why are ye fearful, O ye of little faith? Then he arose, and rebuked the winds and the sea; and there was a great calm.</font>
39:8:32: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Go.</font> And when they were come out, they went into the herd of swine: and, behold, the whole herd of swine ran violently down a steep place into the sea, and perished in the waters.
39:9:2: And, behold, they brought to him a man sick of the palsy, lying on a bed: and Jesus seeing their faith said unto the sick of the palsy; <font color="#CC0000">Son, be of good cheer; thy sins be forgiven thee.</font>
39:9:4: And Jesus knowing their thoughts said, <font color="#CC0000">Wherefore think ye evil in your hearts?</font>
39:9:5: <font color="#CC0000">For whether is easier, to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and walk?</font>
39:9:6: <font color="#CC0000">But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins,</font> (then saith he to the sick of the palsy,) <font color="#CC0000">Arise, take up thy bed, and go unto thine house.</font>
39:9:9: And as Jesus passed forth from thence, he saw a man, named Matthew, sitting at the receipt of custom: and he saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Follow me.</font> And he arose, and followed him.
39:9:12: But when Jesus heard that, he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">They that be whole need not a physician, but they that are sick.</font>
39:9:13: <font color="#CC0000">But go ye and learn what that meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice: for I am not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.</font>
39:9:15: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Can the children of the bridechamber mourn, as long as the bridegroom is with them? but the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken from them, and then shall they fast.</font>
39:9:16: <font color="#CC0000">No man putteth a piece of new cloth unto an old garment, for that which is put in to fill it up taketh from the garment, and the rent is made worse.</font>
39:9:17: <font color="#CC0000">Neither do men put new wine into old bottles: else the bottles break, and the wine runneth out, and the bottles perish: but they put new wine into new bottles, and both are preserved.</font>
39:9:22: But Jesus turned him about, and when he saw her, he said, <font color="#CC0000">Daughter, be of good comfort; thy faith hath made thee whole.</font> And the woman was made whole from that hour.
39:9:24: He said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Give place: for the maid is not dead, but sleepeth.</font> And they laughed him to scorn.
39:9:28: And when he was come into the house, the blind men came to him: and Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Believe ye that I am able to do this?</font> They said unto him, Yea, Lord.
39:9:29: Then touched he their eyes, saying, <font color="#CC0000">According to your faith be it unto you.</font>
39:9:30: And their eyes were opened; and Jesus straitly charged them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">See that no man know it.</font>
39:9:37: Then saith he unto his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">The harvest truly is plenteous, but the labourers are few;</font>
39:9:38: <font color="#CC0000">Pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he will send forth labourers into his harvest.</font>
39:10:5: These twelve Jesus sent forth, and commanded them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Go not into the way of the Gentiles, and into any city of the Samaritans enter ye not:</font>
39:10:6: <font color="#CC0000">But go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.</font>
39:10:7: <font color="#CC0000">And as ye go, preach, saying, The kingdom of heaven is at hand.</font>
39:10:8: <font color="#CC0000">Heal the sick, cleanse the lepers, raise the dead, cast out devils: freely ye have received, freely give.</font>
39:10:9: <font color="#CC0000">Provide neither gold, nor silver, nor brass in your purses,</font>
39:10:10: <font color="#CC0000">Nor scrip for your journey, neither two coats, neither shoes, nor yet staves: for the workman is worthy of his meat.</font>
39:10:11: <font color="#CC0000">And into whatsoever city or town ye shall enter, enquire who in it is worthy; and there abide till ye go thence.</font>
39:10:12: <font color="#CC0000">And when ye come into an house, salute it.</font>
39:10:13: <font color="#CC0000">And if the house be worthy, let your peace come upon it: but if it be not worthy, let your peace return to you.</font>
39:10:14: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear your words, when ye depart out of that house or city, shake off the dust of your feet.</font>
39:10:15: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for that city.</font>
39:10:16: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, I send you forth as sheep in the midst of wolves: be ye therefore wise as serpents, and harmless as doves.</font>
39:10:17: <font color="#CC0000">But beware of men: for they will deliver you up to the councils, and they will scourge you in their synagogues;</font>
39:10:18: <font color="#CC0000">And ye shall be brought before governors and kings for my sake, for a testimony against them and the Gentiles.</font>
39:10:19: <font color="#CC0000">But when they deliver you up, take no thought how or what ye shall speak: for it shall be given you in that same hour what ye shall speak.</font>
39:10:20: <font color="#CC0000">For it is not ye that speak, but the Spirit of your Father which speaketh in you.</font>
39:10:21: <font color="#CC0000">And the brother shall deliver up the brother to death, and the father the child: and the children shall rise up against their parents, and cause them to be put to death.</font>
39:10:22: <font color="#CC0000">And ye shall be hated of all men for my name's sake: but he that endureth to the end shall be saved.</font>
39:10:23: <font color="#CC0000">But when they persecute you in this city, flee ye into another: for verily I say unto you, Ye shall not have gone over the cities of Israel, till the Son of man be come.</font>
39:10:24: <font color="#CC0000">The disciple is not above his master, nor the servant above his lord.</font>
39:10:25: <font color="#CC0000">It is enough for the disciple that he be as his master, and the servant as his lord. If they have called the master of the house Beelzebub, how much more shall they call them of his household?</font>
39:10:26: <font color="#CC0000">Fear them not therefore: for there is nothing covered, that shall not be revealed; and hid, that shall not be known.</font>
39:10:27: <font color="#CC0000">What I tell you in darkness, that speak ye in light: and what ye hear in the ear, that preach ye upon the housetops.</font>
39:10:28: <font color="#CC0000">And fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul: but rather fear him which is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.</font>
39:10:29: <font color="#CC0000">Are not two sparrows sold for a farthing? and one of them shall not fall on the ground without your Father.</font>
39:10:30: <font color="#CC0000">But the very hairs of your head are all numbered.</font>
39:10:31: <font color="#CC0000">Fear ye not therefore, ye are of more value than many sparrows.</font>
39:10:32: <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever therefore shall confess me before men, him will I confess also before my Father which is in heaven.</font>
39:10:33: <font color="#CC0000">But whosoever shall deny me before men, him will I also deny before my Father which is in heaven.</font>
39:10:34: <font color="#CC0000">Think not that I am come to send peace on earth: I came not to send peace, but a sword.</font>
39:10:35: <font color="#CC0000">For I am come to set a man at variance against his father, and the daughter against her mother, and the daughter in law against her mother in law.</font>
39:10:36: <font color="#CC0000">And a man's foes shall be they of his own household.</font>
39:10:37: <font color="#CC0000">He that loveth father or mother more than me is not worthy of me: and he that loveth son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me.</font>
39:10:38: <font color="#CC0000">And he that taketh not his cross, and followeth after me, is not worthy of me.</font>
39:10:39: <font color="#CC0000">He that findeth his life shall lose it: and he that loseth his life for my sake shall find it.</font>
39:10:40: <font color="#CC0000">He that receiveth you receiveth me, and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.</font>
39:10:41: <font color="#CC0000">He that receiveth a prophet in the name of a prophet shall receive a prophet's reward; and he that receiveth a righteous man in the name of a righteous man shall receive a righteous man's reward.</font>
39:10:42: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever shall give to drink unto one of these little ones a cup of cold water only in the name of a disciple, verily I say unto you, he shall in no wise lose his reward.</font>
39:11:4: Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Go and shew John again those things which ye do hear and see:</font>
39:11:5: <font color="#CC0000">The blind receive their sight, and the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, and the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have the gospel preached to them.</font>
39:11:6: <font color="#CC0000">And blessed is he, whosoever shall not be offended in me.</font>
39:11:7: And as they departed, Jesus began to say unto the multitudes concerning John, <font color="#CC0000">What went ye out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken with the wind?</font>
39:11:8: <font color="#CC0000">But what went ye out for to see? A man clothed in soft raiment? behold, they that wear soft clothing are in kings' houses.</font>
39:11:9: <font color="#CC0000">But what went ye out for to see? A prophet? yea, I say unto you, and more than a prophet.</font>
39:11:10: <font color="#CC0000">For this is he, of whom it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.</font>
39:11:11: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, Among them that are born of women there hath not risen a greater than John the Baptist: notwithstanding he that is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.</font>
39:11:12: <font color="#CC0000">And from the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven suffereth violence, and the violent take it by force.</font>
39:11:13: <font color="#CC0000">For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John.</font>
39:11:14: <font color="#CC0000">And if ye will receive it, this is Elias, which was for to come.</font>
39:11:15: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.</font>
39:11:16: <font color="#CC0000">But whereunto shall I liken this generation? It is like unto children sitting in the markets, and calling unto their fellows,</font>
39:11:17: <font color="#CC0000">And saying, We have piped unto you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned unto you, and ye have not lamented.</font>
39:11:18: <font color="#CC0000">For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He hath a devil.</font>
39:11:19: <font color="#CC0000">The Son of man came eating and drinking, and they say, Behold a man gluttonous, and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners. But wisdom is justified of her children.</font>
39:11:21: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works, which were done in you, had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.</font>
39:11:22: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the day of judgment, than for you.</font>
39:11:23: <font color="#CC0000">And thou, Capernaum, which art exalted unto heaven, shalt be brought down to hell: for if the mighty works, which have been done in thee, had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until this day.</font>
39:11:24: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, That it shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom in the day of judgment, than for thee.</font>
39:11:25: At that time Jesus answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes.</font>
39:11:26: <font color="#CC0000">Even so, Father: for so it seemed good in thy sight.</font>
39:11:27: <font color="#CC0000">All things are delivered unto me of my Father: and no man knoweth the Son, but the Father; neither knoweth any man the Father, save the Son, and he to whomsoever the Son will reveal him.</font>
39:11:28: <font color="#CC0000">Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.</font>
39:11:29: <font color="#CC0000">Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me; for I am meek and lowly in heart: and ye shall find rest unto your souls.</font>
39:11:30: <font color="#CC0000">For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.</font>
39:12:3: But he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Have ye not read what David did, when he was an hungred, and they that were with him;</font>
39:12:4: <font color="#CC0000">How he entered into the house of God, and did eat the shewbread, which was not lawful for him to eat, neither for them which were with him, but only for the priests?</font>
39:12:5: <font color="#CC0000">Or have ye not read in the law, how that on the sabbath days the priests in the temple profane the sabbath, and are blameless?</font>
39:12:6: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, That in this place is one greater than the temple.</font>
39:12:7: <font color="#CC0000">But if ye had known what this meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice, ye would not have condemned the guiltless.</font>
39:12:8: <font color="#CC0000">For the Son of man is Lord even of the sabbath day.</font>
39:12:11: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">What man shall there be among you, that shall have one sheep, and if it fall into a pit on the sabbath day, will he not lay hold on it, and lift it out?</font>
39:12:12: <font color="#CC0000">How much then is a man better than a sheep? Wherefore it is lawful to do well on the sabbath days.</font>
39:12:13: Then saith he to the man, <font color="#CC0000">Stretch forth thine hand.</font> And he stretched it forth; and it was restored whole, like as the other.
39:12:25: And Jesus knew their thoughts, and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and every city or house divided against itself shall not stand:</font>
39:12:26: <font color="#CC0000">And if Satan cast out Satan, he is divided against himself; how shall then his kingdom stand?</font>
39:12:27: <font color="#CC0000">And if I by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your children cast them out? therefore they shall be your judges.</font>
39:12:28: <font color="#CC0000">But if I cast out devils by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God is come unto you.</font>
39:12:29: <font color="#CC0000">Or else how can one enter into a strong man's house, and spoil his goods, except he first bind the strong man? and then he will spoil his house.</font>
39:12:30: <font color="#CC0000">He that is not with me is against me; and he that gathereth not with me scattereth abroad.</font>
39:12:31: <font color="#CC0000">Wherefore I say unto you, All manner of sin and blasphemy shall be forgiven unto men: but the blasphemy against the Holy Ghost shall not be forgiven unto men.</font>
39:12:32: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever speaketh a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him: but whosoever speaketh against the Holy Ghost, it shall not be forgiven him, neither in this world, neither in the world to come.</font>
39:12:33: <font color="#CC0000">Either make the tree good, and his fruit good; or else make the tree corrupt, and his fruit corrupt: for the tree is known by his fruit.</font>
39:12:34: <font color="#CC0000">O generation of vipers, how can ye, being evil, speak good things? for out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.</font>
39:12:35: <font color="#CC0000">A good man out of the good treasure of the heart bringeth forth good things: and an evil man out of the evil treasure bringeth forth evil things.</font>
39:12:36: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, That every idle word that men shall speak, they shall give account thereof in the day of judgment.</font>
39:12:37: <font color="#CC0000">For by thy words thou shalt be justified, and by thy words thou shalt be condemned.</font>
39:12:39: But he answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">An evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given to it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas:</font>
39:12:40: <font color="#CC0000">For as Jonas was three days and three nights in the whale's belly; so shall the Son of man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.</font>
39:12:41: <font color="#CC0000">The men of Nineveh shall rise in judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: because they repented at the preaching of Jonas; and, behold, a greater than Jonas is here.</font>
39:12:42: <font color="#CC0000">The queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for she came from the uttermost parts of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and, behold, a greater than Solomon is here.</font>
39:12:43: <font color="#CC0000">When the unclean spirit is gone out of a man, he walketh through dry places, seeking rest, and findeth none.</font>
39:12:44: <font color="#CC0000">Then he saith, I will return into my house from whence I came out; and when he is come, he findeth it empty, swept, and garnished.</font>
39:12:45: <font color="#CC0000">Then goeth he, and taketh with himself seven other spirits more wicked than himself, and they enter in and dwell there: and the last state of that man is worse than the first. Even so shall it be also unto this wicked generation.</font>
39:12:48: But he answered and said unto him that told him, <font color="#CC0000">Who is my mother? and who are my brethren?</font>
39:12:49: And he stretched forth his hand toward his disciples, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Behold my mother and my brethren!</font>
39:12:50: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever shall do the will of my Father which is in heaven, the same is my brother, and sister, and mother.</font>
39:13:3: And he spake many things unto them in parables, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Behold, a sower went forth to sow;</font>
39:13:4: <font color="#CC0000">And when he sowed, some seeds fell by the way side, and the fowls came and devoured them up:</font>
39:13:5: <font color="#CC0000">Some fell upon stony places, where they had not much earth: and forthwith they sprung up, because they had no deepness of earth:</font>
39:13:6: <font color="#CC0000">And when the sun was up, they were scorched; and because they had no root, they withered away.</font>
39:13:7: <font color="#CC0000">And some fell among thorns; and the thorns sprung up, and choked them:</font>
39:13:8: <font color="#CC0000">But other fell into good ground, and brought forth fruit, some an hundredfold, some sixtyfold, some thirtyfold.</font>
39:13:9: <font color="#CC0000">Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.</font>
39:13:11: He answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Because it is given unto you to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it is not given.</font>
39:13:12: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever hath, to him shall be given, and he shall have more abundance: but whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken away even that he hath.</font>
39:13:13: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore speak I to them in parables: because they seeing see not; and hearing they hear not, neither do they understand.</font>
39:13:14: <font color="#CC0000">And in them is fulfilled the prophecy of Esaias, which saith, By hearing ye shall hear, and shall not understand; and seeing ye shall see, and shall not perceive:</font>
39:13:15: <font color="#CC0000">For this people's heart is waxed gross, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes they have closed; lest at any time they should see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and should understand with their heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.</font>
39:13:16: <font color="#CC0000">But blessed are your eyes, for they see: and your ears, for they hear.</font>
39:13:17: <font color="#CC0000">For verily I say unto you, That many prophets and righteous men have desired to see those things which ye see, and have not seen them; and to hear those things which ye hear, and have not heard them.</font>
39:13:18: <font color="#CC0000">Hear ye therefore the parable of the sower.</font>
39:13:19: <font color="#CC0000">When any one heareth the word of the kingdom, and understandeth it not, then cometh the wicked one, and catcheth away that which was sown in his heart. This is he which received seed by the way side.</font>
39:13:20: <font color="#CC0000">But he that received the seed into stony places, the same is he that heareth the word, and anon with joy receiveth it;</font>
39:13:21: <font color="#CC0000">Yet hath he not root in himself, but dureth for a while: for when tribulation or persecution ariseth because of the word, by and by he is offended.</font>
39:13:22: <font color="#CC0000">He also that received seed among the thorns is he that heareth the word; and the care of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches, choke the word, and he becometh unfruitful.</font>
39:13:23: <font color="#CC0000">But he that received seed into the good ground is he that heareth the word, and understandeth it; which also beareth fruit, and bringeth forth, some an hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.</font>
39:13:24: Another parable put he forth unto them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">The kingdom of heaven is likened unto a man which sowed good seed in his field:</font>
39:13:25: <font color="#CC0000">But while men slept, his enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat, and went his way.</font>
39:13:26: <font color="#CC0000">But when the blade was sprung up, and brought forth fruit, then appeared the tares also.</font>
39:13:27: <font color="#CC0000">So the servants of the householder came and said unto him, Sir, didst not thou sow good seed in thy field? from whence then hath it tares?</font>
39:13:28: <font color="#CC0000">He said unto them, An enemy hath done this. The servants said unto him, Wilt thou then that we go and gather them up?</font>
39:13:29: <font color="#CC0000">But he said, Nay; lest while ye gather up the tares, ye root up also the wheat with them.</font>
39:13:30: <font color="#CC0000">Let both grow together until the harvest: and in the time of harvest I will say to the reapers, Gather ye together first the tares, and bind them in bundles to burn them: but gather the wheat into my barn.</font>
39:13:31: Another parable put he forth unto them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">The kingdom of heaven is like to a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and sowed in his field:</font>
39:13:32: <font color="#CC0000">Which indeed is the least of all seeds: but when it is grown, it is the greatest among herbs, and becometh a tree, so that the birds of the air come and lodge in the branches thereof.</font>
39:13:33: Another parable spake he unto them; <font color="#CC0000">The kingdom of heaven is like unto leaven, which a woman took, and hid in three measures of meal, till the whole was leavened.</font>
39:13:37: He answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">He that soweth the good seed is the Son of man;</font>
39:13:38: <font color="#CC0000">The field is the world; the good seed are the children of the kingdom; but the tares are the children of the wicked one;</font>
39:13:39: <font color="#CC0000">The enemy that sowed them is the devil; the harvest is the end of the world; and the reapers are the angels.</font>
39:13:40: <font color="#CC0000">As therefore the tares are gathered and burned in the fire; so shall it be in the end of this world.</font>
39:13:41: <font color="#CC0000">The Son of man shall send forth his angels, and they shall gather out of his kingdom all things that offend, and them which do iniquity;</font>
39:13:42: <font color="#CC0000">And shall cast them into a furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.</font>
39:13:43: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father. Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.</font>
39:13:44: <font color="#CC0000">Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto treasure hid in a field; the which when a man hath found, he hideth, and for joy thereof goeth and selleth all that he hath, and buyeth that field.</font>
39:13:45: <font color="#CC0000">Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a merchant man, seeking goodly pearls:</font>
39:13:46: <font color="#CC0000">Who, when he had found one pearl of great price, went and sold all that he had, and bought it.</font>
39:13:47: <font color="#CC0000">Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a net, that was cast into the sea, and gathered of every kind:</font>
39:13:48: <font color="#CC0000">Which, when it was full, they drew to shore, and sat down, and gathered the good into vessels, but cast the bad away.</font>
39:13:49: <font color="#CC0000">So shall it be at the end of the world: the angels shall come forth, and sever the wicked from among the just,</font>
39:13:50: <font color="#CC0000">And shall cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.</font>
39:13:51: Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Have ye understood all these things?</font> They say unto him, Yea, Lord.
39:13:52: Then said he unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Therefore every scribe which is instructed unto the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is an householder, which bringeth forth out of his treasure things new and old.</font>
39:13:57: And they were offended in him. But Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">A prophet is not without honour, save in his own country, and in his own house.</font>
39:14:16: But Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">They need not depart; give ye them to eat.</font>
39:14:18: He said, <font color="#CC0000">Bring them hither to me.</font>
39:14:27: But straightway Jesus spake unto them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Be of good cheer; it is I; be not afraid.</font>
39:14:29: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Come.</font> And when Peter was come down out of the ship, he walked on the water, to go to Jesus.
39:14:31: And immediately Jesus stretched forth his hand, and caught him, and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">O thou of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt?</font>
39:15:3: But he answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why do ye also transgress the commandment of God by your tradition?</font>
39:15:4: <font color="#CC0000">For God commanded, saying, Honour thy father and mother: and, He that curseth father or mother, let him die the death.</font>
39:15:5: <font color="#CC0000">But ye say, Whosoever shall say to his father or his mother, It is a gift, by whatsoever thou mightest be profited by me;</font>
39:15:6: <font color="#CC0000">And honour not his father or his mother, he shall be free. Thus have ye made the commandment of God of none effect by your tradition.</font>
39:15:7: <font color="#CC0000">Ye hypocrites, well did Esaias prophesy of you, saying,</font>
39:15:8: <font color="#CC0000">This people draweth nigh unto me with their mouth, and honoureth me with their lips; but their heart is far from me.</font>
39:15:9: <font color="#CC0000">But in vain they do worship me, teaching for doctrines the commandments of men.</font>
39:15:10: And he called the multitude, and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Hear, and understand:</font>
39:15:11: <font color="#CC0000">Not that which goeth into the mouth defileth a man; but that which cometh out of the mouth, this defileth a man.</font>
39:15:13: But he answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">Every plant, which my heavenly Father hath not planted, shall be rooted up.</font>
39:15:14: <font color="#CC0000">Let them alone: they be blind leaders of the blind. And if the blind lead the blind, both shall fall into the ditch.</font>
39:15:16: And Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">Are ye also yet without understanding?</font>
39:15:17: <font color="#CC0000">Do not ye yet understand, that whatsoever entereth in at the mouth goeth into the belly, and is cast out into the draught?</font>
39:15:18: <font color="#CC0000">But those things which proceed out of the mouth come forth from the heart; and they defile the man.</font>
39:15:19: <font color="#CC0000">For out of the heart proceed evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, fornications, thefts, false witness, blasphemies:</font>
39:15:20: <font color="#CC0000">These are the things which defile a man: but to eat with unwashen hands defileth not a man.</font>
39:15:24: But he answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">I am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel.</font>
39:15:26: But he answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">It is not meet to take the children's bread, and to cast it to dogs.</font>
39:15:28: Then Jesus answered and said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">O woman, great is thy faith: be it unto thee even as thou wilt. And her daughter was made whole from that very hour.</font>
39:15:32: Then Jesus called his disciples unto him, and said, <font color="#CC0000">I have compassion on the multitude, because they continue with me now three days, and have nothing to eat: and I will not send them away fasting, lest they faint in the way.</font>
39:15:34: And Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven, and a few little fishes.</font>
39:16:2: He answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">When it is evening, ye say, It will be fair weather: for the sky is red.</font>
39:16:3: <font color="#CC0000">And in the morning, It will be foul weather to day: for the sky is red and lowring. O ye hypocrites, ye can discern the face of the sky; but can ye not discern the signs of the times?</font>
39:16:4: <font color="#CC0000">A wicked and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given unto it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas.</font> And he left them, and departed.
39:16:6: Then Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Take heed and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees.</font>
39:16:8: Which when Jesus perceived, he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">O ye of little faith, why reason ye among yourselves, because ye have brought no bread?</font>
39:16:9: <font color="#CC0000">Do ye not yet understand, neither remember the five loaves of the five thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?</font>
39:16:10: <font color="#CC0000">Neither the seven loaves of the four thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?</font>
39:16:11: <font color="#CC0000">How is it that ye do not understand that I spake it not to you concerning bread, that ye should beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees?</font>
39:16:13: When Jesus came into the coasts of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Whom do men say that I the Son of man am?</font>
39:16:15: He saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">But whom say ye that I am?</font>
39:16:17: And Jesus answered and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Blessed art thou, Simon Bar–jona: for flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee, but my Father which is in heaven.</font>
39:16:18: <font color="#CC0000">And I say also unto thee, That thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church; and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it.</font>
39:16:19: <font color="#CC0000">And I will give unto thee the keys of the kingdom of heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt bind on earth shall be bound in heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.</font>
39:16:23: But he turned, and said unto Peter, <font color="#CC0000">Get thee behind me, Satan: thou art an offence unto me: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but those that be of men.</font>
39:16:24: Then said Jesus unto his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.</font>
39:16:25: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever will save his life shall lose it: and whosoever will lose his life for my sake shall find it.</font>
39:16:26: <font color="#CC0000">For what is a man profited, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul? or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?</font>
39:16:27: <font color="#CC0000">For the Son of man shall come in the glory of his Father with his angels; and then he shall reward every man according to his works.</font>
39:16:28: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, There be some standing here, which shall not taste of death, till they see the Son of man coming in his kingdom.</font>
39:17:7: And Jesus came and touched them, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Arise, and be not afraid.</font>
39:17:9: And as they came down from the mountain, Jesus charged them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Tell the vision to no man, until the Son of man be risen again from the dead.</font>
39:17:11: And Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Elias truly shall first come, and restore all things.</font>
39:17:12: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, That Elias is come already, and they knew him not, but have done unto him whatsoever they listed. Likewise shall also the Son of man suffer of them.</font>
39:17:17: Then Jesus answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him hither to me.</font>
39:17:20: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Because of your unbelief: for verily I say unto you, If ye have faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye shall say unto this mountain, Remove hence to yonder place; and it shall remove; and nothing shall be impossible unto you.</font>
39:17:21: <font color="#CC0000">Howbeit this kind goeth not out but by prayer and fasting.</font>
39:17:22: And while they abode in Galilee, Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">The Son of man shall be betrayed into the hands of men:</font>
39:17:23: <font color="#CC0000">And they shall kill him, and the third day he shall be raised again.</font> And they were exceeding sorry.
39:17:25: He saith, Yes. And when he was come into the house, Jesus prevented him, saying, <font color="#CC0000">What thinkest thou, Simon? of whom do the kings of the earth take custom or tribute? of their own children, or of strangers?</font>
39:17:26: Peter saith unto him, Of strangers. Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Then are the children free.</font>
39:17:27: <font color="#CC0000">Notwithstanding, lest we should offend them, go thou to the sea, and cast an hook, and take up the fish that first cometh up; and when thou hast opened his mouth, thou shalt find a piece of money: that take, and give unto them for me and thee.</font>
39:18:3: And said, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, Except ye be converted, and become as little children, ye shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven.</font>
39:18:4: <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever therefore shall humble himself as this little child, the same is greatest in the kingdom of heaven.</font>
39:18:5: <font color="#CC0000">And whoso shall receive one such little child in my name receiveth me.</font>
39:18:6: <font color="#CC0000">But whoso shall offend one of these little ones which believe in me, it were better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and that he were drowned in the depth of the sea.</font>
39:18:7: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto the world because of offences! for it must needs be that offences come; but woe to that man by whom the offence cometh!</font>
39:18:8: <font color="#CC0000">Wherefore if thy hand or thy foot offend thee, cut them off, and cast them from thee: it is better for thee to enter into life halt or maimed, rather than having two hands or two feet to be cast into everlasting fire.</font>
39:18:9: <font color="#CC0000">And if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out, and cast it from thee: it is better for thee to enter into life with one eye, rather than having two eyes to be cast into hell fire.</font>
39:18:10: <font color="#CC0000">Take heed that ye despise not one of these little ones; for I say unto you, That in heaven their angels do always behold the face of my Father which is in heaven.</font>
39:18:11: <font color="#CC0000">For the Son of man is come to save that which was lost.</font>
39:18:12: <font color="#CC0000">How think ye? if a man have an hundred sheep, and one of them be gone astray, doth he not leave the ninety and nine, and goeth into the mountains, and seeketh that which is gone astray?</font>
39:18:13: <font color="#CC0000">And if so be that he find it, verily I say unto you, he rejoiceth more of that sheep, than of the ninety and nine which went not astray.</font>
39:18:14: <font color="#CC0000">Even so it is not the will of your Father which is in heaven, that one of these little ones should perish.</font>
39:18:15: <font color="#CC0000">Moreover if thy brother shall trespass against thee, go and tell him his fault between thee and him alone: if he shall hear thee, thou hast gained thy brother.</font>
39:18:16: <font color="#CC0000">But if he will not hear thee, then take with thee one or two more, that in the mouth of two or three witnesses every word may be established.</font>
39:18:17: <font color="#CC0000">And if he shall neglect to hear them, tell it unto the church: but if he neglect to hear the church, let him be unto thee as an heathen man and a publican.</font>
39:18:18: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, Whatsoever ye shall bind on earth shall be bound in heaven: and whatsoever ye shall loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.</font>
39:18:19: <font color="#CC0000">Again I say unto you, That if two of you shall agree on earth as touching any thing that they shall ask, it shall be done for them of my Father which is in heaven.</font>
39:18:20: <font color="#CC0000">For where two or three are gathered together in my name, there am I in the midst of them.</font>
39:18:22: Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">I say not unto thee, Until seven times: but, Until seventy times seven.</font>
39:18:23: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore is the kingdom of heaven likened unto a certain king, which would take account of his servants.</font>
39:18:24: <font color="#CC0000">And when he had begun to reckon, one was brought unto him, which owed him ten thousand talents.</font>
39:18:25: <font color="#CC0000">But forasmuch as he had not to pay, his lord commanded him to be sold, and his wife, and children, and all that he had, and payment to be made.</font>
39:18:26: <font color="#CC0000">The servant therefore fell down, and worshipped him, saying, Lord, have patience with me, and I will pay thee all.</font>
39:18:27: <font color="#CC0000">Then the lord of that servant was moved with compassion, and loosed him, and forgave him the debt.</font>
39:18:28: <font color="#CC0000">But the same servant went out, and found one of his fellowservants, which owed him an hundred pence: and he laid hands on him, and took him by the throat, saying, Pay me that thou owest.</font>
39:18:29: <font color="#CC0000">And his fellowservant fell down at his feet, and besought him, saying, Have patience with me, and I will pay thee all.</font>
39:18:30: <font color="#CC0000">And he would not: but went and cast him into prison, till he should pay the debt.</font>
39:18:31: <font color="#CC0000">So when his fellowservants saw what was done, they were very sorry, and came and told unto their lord all that was done.</font>
39:18:32: <font color="#CC0000">Then his lord, after that he had called him, said unto him, O thou wicked servant, I forgave thee all that debt, because thou desiredst me:</font>
39:18:33: <font color="#CC0000">Shouldest not thou also have had compassion on thy fellowservant, even as I had pity on thee?</font>
39:18:34: <font color="#CC0000">And his lord was wroth, and delivered him to the tormentors, till he should pay all that was due unto him.</font>
39:18:35: <font color="#CC0000">So likewise shall my heavenly Father do also unto you, if ye from your hearts forgive not every one his brother their trespasses.</font>
39:19:4: And he answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Have ye not read, that he which made them at the beginning made them male and female,</font>
39:19:5: <font color="#CC0000">And said, For this cause shall a man leave father and mother, and shall cleave to his wife: and they twain shall be one flesh?</font>
39:19:6: <font color="#CC0000">Wherefore they are no more twain, but one flesh. What therefore God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.</font>
39:19:8: He saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Moses because of the hardness of your hearts suffered you to put away your wives: but from the beginning it was not so.</font>
39:19:9: <font color="#CC0000">And I say unto you, Whosoever shall put away his wife, except it be for fornication, and shall marry another, committeth adultery: and whoso marrieth her which is put away doth commit adultery.</font>
39:19:11: But he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">All men cannot receive this saying, save they to whom it is given.</font>
39:19:12: <font color="#CC0000">For there are some eunuchs, which were so born from their mother's womb: and there are some eunuchs, which were made eunuchs of men: and there be eunuchs, which have made themselves eunuchs for the kingdom of heaven's sake. He that is able to receive it, let him receive it.</font>
39:19:14: But Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">Suffer little children, and forbid them not, to come unto me: for of such is the kingdom of heaven.</font>
39:19:17: And he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Why callest thou me good? there is none good but one, that is, God: but if thou wilt enter into life, keep the commandments.</font>
39:19:19: <font color="#CC0000">Honour thy father and thy mother: and, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.</font>
39:19:21: Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">If thou wilt be perfect, go and sell that thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come and follow me.</font>
39:19:23: Then said Jesus unto his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, That a rich man shall hardly enter into the kingdom of heaven.</font>
39:19:24: <font color="#CC0000">And again I say unto you, It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.</font>
39:19:26: But Jesus beheld them, and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">With men this is impossible; but with God all things are possible.</font>
39:19:28: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, That ye which have followed me, in the regeneration when the Son of man shall sit in the throne of his glory, ye also shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.</font>
39:19:29: <font color="#CC0000">And every one that hath forsaken houses, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my name's sake, shall receive an hundredfold, and shall inherit everlasting life.</font>
39:19:30: <font color="#CC0000">But many that are first shall be last; and the last shall be first.</font>
39:20:1: <font color="#CC0000">For the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is an householder, which went out early in the morning to hire labourers into his vineyard.</font>
39:20:2: <font color="#CC0000">And when he had agreed with the labourers for a penny a day, he sent them into his vineyard.</font>
39:20:3: <font color="#CC0000">And he went out about the third hour, and saw others standing idle in the marketplace,</font>
39:20:4: <font color="#CC0000">And said unto them; Go ye also into the vineyard, and whatsoever is right I will give you. And they went their way.</font>
39:20:5: <font color="#CC0000">Again he went out about the sixth and ninth hour, and did likewise.</font>
39:20:6: <font color="#CC0000">And about the eleventh hour he went out, and found others standing idle, and saith unto them, Why stand ye here all the day idle?</font>
39:20:7: <font color="#CC0000">They say unto him, Because no man hath hired us. He saith unto them, Go ye also into the vineyard; and whatsoever is right, that shall ye receive.</font>
39:20:8: <font color="#CC0000">So when even was come, the lord of the vineyard saith unto his steward, Call the labourers, and give them their hire, beginning from the last unto the first.</font>
39:20:9: <font color="#CC0000">And when they came that were hired about the eleventh hour, they received every man a penny.</font>
39:20:10: <font color="#CC0000">But when the first came, they supposed that they should have received more; and they likewise received every man a penny.</font>
39:20:11: <font color="#CC0000">And when they had received it, they murmured against the goodman of the house,</font>
39:20:12: <font color="#CC0000">Saying, These last have wrought but one hour, and thou hast made them equal unto us, which have borne the burden and heat of the day.</font>
39:20:13: <font color="#CC0000">But he answered one of them, and said, Friend, I do thee no wrong: didst not thou agree with me for a penny?</font>
39:20:14: <font color="#CC0000">Take that thine is, and go thy way: I will give unto this last, even as unto thee.</font>
39:20:15: <font color="#CC0000">Is it not lawful for me to do what I will with mine own? Is thine eye evil, because I am good?</font>
39:20:16: <font color="#CC0000">So the last shall be first, and the first last: for many be called, but few chosen.</font>
39:20:18: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be betrayed unto the chief priests and unto the scribes, and they shall condemn him to death,</font>
39:20:19: <font color="#CC0000">And shall deliver him to the Gentiles to mock, and to scourge, and to crucify him: and the third day he shall rise again.</font>
39:20:21: And he said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">What wilt thou?</font> She saith unto him, Grant that these my two sons may sit, the one on thy right hand, and the other on the left, in thy kingdom.
39:20:22: But Jesus answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">Ye know not what ye ask. Are ye able to drink of the cup that I shall drink of, and to be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?</font> They say unto him, We are able.
39:20:23: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Ye shall drink indeed of my cup, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with: but to sit on my right hand, and on my left, is not mine to give, but it shall be given to them for whom it is prepared of my Father.</font>
39:20:25: But Jesus called them unto him, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Ye know that the princes of the Gentiles exercise dominion over them, and they that are great exercise authority upon them.</font>
39:20:26: <font color="#CC0000">But it shall not be so among you: but whosoever will be great among you, let him be your minister;</font>
39:20:27: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever will be chief among you, let him be your servant:</font>
39:20:28: <font color="#CC0000">Even as the Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom for many.</font>
39:20:32: And Jesus stood still, and called them, and said, <font color="#CC0000">What will ye that I shall do unto you?</font>
39:21:2: Saying unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Go into the village over against you, and straightway ye shall find an ass tied, and a colt with her: loose them, and bring them unto me.</font>
39:21:3: <font color="#CC0000">And if any man say ought unto you, ye shall say, The Lord hath need of them; and straightway he will send them.</font>
39:21:13: And said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">It is written, My house shall be called the house of prayer; but ye have made it a den of thieves.</font>
39:21:16: And said unto him, Hearest thou what these say? And Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Yea; have ye never read, Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings thou hast perfected praise?</font>
39:21:19: And when he saw a fig tree in the way, he came to it, and found nothing thereon, but leaves only, and said unto it, <font color="#CC0000">Let no fruit grow on thee henceforward for ever.</font> And presently the fig tree withered away.
39:21:21: Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, If ye have faith, and doubt not, ye shall not only do this which is done to the fig tree, but also if ye shall say unto this mountain, Be thou removed, and be thou cast into the sea; it shall be done.</font>
39:21:22: <font color="#CC0000">And all things, whatsoever ye shall ask in prayer, believing, ye shall receive.</font>
39:21:24: And Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I also will ask you one thing, which if ye tell me, I in like wise will tell you by what authority I do these things.</font>
39:21:25: <font color="#CC0000">The baptism of John, whence was it? from heaven, or of men?</font> And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say unto us, Why did ye not then believe him?
39:21:27: And they answered Jesus, and said, We cannot tell. And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.</font>
39:21:28: <font color="#CC0000">But what think ye? A certain man had two sons; and he came to the first, and said, Son, go work to day in my vineyard.</font>
39:21:29: <font color="#CC0000">He answered and said, I will not: but afterward he repented, and went.</font>
39:21:30: <font color="#CC0000">And he came to the second, and said likewise. And he answered and said, I go, sir: and went not.</font>
39:21:31: <font color="#CC0000">Whether of them twain did the will of his father?</font> They say unto him, The first. Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, That the publicans and the harlots go into the kingdom of God before you.</font>
39:21:32: <font color="#CC0000">For John came unto you in the way of righteousness, and ye believed him not: but the publicans and the harlots believed him: and ye, when ye had seen it, repented not afterward, that ye might believe him.</font>
39:21:33: <font color="#CC0000">Hear another parable: There was a certain householder, which planted a vineyard, and hedged it round about, and digged a winepress in it, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen, and went into a far country:</font>
39:21:34: <font color="#CC0000">And when the time of the fruit drew near, he sent his servants to the husbandmen, that they might receive the fruits of it.</font>
39:21:35: <font color="#CC0000">And the husbandmen took his servants, and beat one, and killed another, and stoned another.</font>
39:21:36: <font color="#CC0000">Again, he sent other servants more than the first: and they did unto them likewise.</font>
39:21:37: <font color="#CC0000">But last of all he sent unto them his son, saying, They will reverence my son.</font>
39:21:38: <font color="#CC0000">But when the husbandmen saw the son, they said among themselves, This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and let us seize on his inheritance.</font>
39:21:39: <font color="#CC0000">And they caught him, and cast him out of the vineyard, and slew him.</font>
39:21:40: <font color="#CC0000">When the lord therefore of the vineyard cometh, what will he do unto those husbandmen?</font>
39:21:42: Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Did ye never read in the scriptures, The stone which the builders rejected, the same is become the head of the corner: this is the Lord's doing, and it is marvellous in our eyes?</font>
39:21:43: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore say I unto you, The kingdom of God shall be taken from you, and given to a nation bringing forth the fruits thereof.</font>
39:21:44: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever shall fall on this stone shall be broken: but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder.</font>
39:22:2: <font color="#CC0000">The kingdom of heaven is like unto a certain king, which made a marriage for his son,</font>
39:22:3: <font color="#CC0000">And sent forth his servants to call them that were bidden to the wedding: and they would not come.</font>
39:22:4: <font color="#CC0000">Again, he sent forth other servants, saying, Tell them which are bidden, Behold, I have prepared my dinner: my oxen and my fatlings are killed, and all things are ready: come unto the marriage.</font>
39:22:5: <font color="#CC0000">But they made light of it, and went their ways, one to his farm, another to his merchandise:</font>
39:22:6: <font color="#CC0000">And the remnant took his servants, and entreated them spitefully, and slew them.</font>
39:22:7: <font color="#CC0000">But when the king heard thereof, he was wroth: and he sent forth his armies, and destroyed those murderers, and burned up their city.</font>
39:22:8: <font color="#CC0000">Then saith he to his servants, The wedding is ready, but they which were bidden were not worthy.</font>
39:22:9: <font color="#CC0000">Go ye therefore into the highways, and as many as ye shall find, bid to the marriage.</font>
39:22:10: <font color="#CC0000">So those servants went out into the highways, and gathered together all as many as they found, both bad and good: and the wedding was furnished with guests.</font>
39:22:11: <font color="#CC0000">And when the king came in to see the guests, he saw there a man which had not on a wedding garment:</font>
39:22:12: <font color="#CC0000">And he saith unto him, Friend, how camest thou in hither not having a wedding garment? And he was speechless.</font>
39:22:13: <font color="#CC0000">Then said the king to the servants, Bind him hand and foot, and take him away, and cast him into outer darkness; there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.</font>
39:22:14: <font color="#CC0000">For many are called, but few are chosen.</font>
39:22:18: But Jesus perceived their wickedness, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Why tempt ye me, ye hypocrites?</font>
39:22:19: <font color="#CC0000">Shew me the tribute money.</font> And they brought unto him a penny.
39:22:20: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Whose is this image and superscription?</font>
39:22:21: They say unto him, Cæsar's. Then saith he unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Render therefore unto Cæsar the things which are Cæsar's; and unto God the things that are God's.</font>
39:22:29: Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Ye do err, not knowing the scriptures, nor the power of God.</font>
39:22:30: <font color="#CC0000">For in the resurrection they neither marry, nor are given in marriage, but are as the angels of God in heaven.</font>
39:22:31: <font color="#CC0000">But as touching the resurrection of the dead, have ye not read that which was spoken unto you by God, saying,</font>
39:22:32: <font color="#CC0000">I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? God is not the God of the dead, but of the living.</font>
39:22:37: Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind.</font>
39:22:38: <font color="#CC0000">This is the first and great commandment.</font>
39:22:39: <font color="#CC0000">And the second is like unto it, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.</font>
39:22:40: <font color="#CC0000">On these two commandments hang all the law and the prophets.</font>
39:22:42: Saying, <font color="#CC0000">What think ye of Christ? whose son is he? They say unto him, The Son of David.</font>
39:22:43: He saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">How then doth David in spirit call him Lord, saying,</font>
39:22:44: <font color="#CC0000">The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, till I make thine enemies thy footstool?</font>
39:22:45: <font color="#CC0000">If David then call him Lord, how is he his son?</font>
39:23:2: Saying, <font color="#CC0000">The scribes and the Pharisees sit in Moses' seat:</font>
39:23:3: <font color="#CC0000">All therefore whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do; but do not ye after their works: for they say, and do not.</font>
39:23:4: <font color="#CC0000">For they bind heavy burdens and grievous to be borne, and lay them on men's shoulders; but they themselves will not move them with one of their fingers.</font>
39:23:5: <font color="#CC0000">But all their works they do for to be seen of men: they make broad their phylacteries, and enlarge the borders of their garments,</font>
39:23:6: <font color="#CC0000">And love the uppermost rooms at feasts, and the chief seats in the synagogues,</font>
39:23:7: <font color="#CC0000">And greetings in the markets, and to be called of men, Rabbi, Rabbi.</font>
39:23:8: <font color="#CC0000">But be not ye called Rabbi: for one is your Master, even Christ; and all ye are brethren.</font>
39:23:9: <font color="#CC0000">And call no man your father upon the earth: for one is your Father, which is in heaven.</font>
39:23:10: <font color="#CC0000">Neither be ye called masters: for one is your Master, even Christ.</font>
39:23:11: <font color="#CC0000">But he that is greatest among you shall be your servant.</font>
39:23:12: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever shall exalt himself shall be abased; and he that shall humble himself shall be exalted.</font>
39:23:13: <font color="#CC0000">But woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye shut up the kingdom of heaven against men: for ye neither go in yourselves, neither suffer ye them that are entering to go in.</font>
39:23:14: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye devour widows' houses, and for a pretence make long prayer: therefore ye shall receive the greater damnation.</font>
39:23:15: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye compass sea and land to make one proselyte, and when he is made, ye make him twofold more the child of hell than yourselves.</font>
39:23:16: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you, ye blind guides, which say, Whosoever shall swear by the temple, it is nothing; but whosoever shall swear by the gold of the temple, he is a debtor!</font>
39:23:17: <font color="#CC0000">Ye fools and blind: for whether is greater, the gold, or the temple that sanctifieth the gold?</font>
39:23:18: <font color="#CC0000">And, Whosoever shall swear by the altar, it is nothing; but whosoever sweareth by the gift that is upon it, he is guilty.</font>
39:23:19: <font color="#CC0000">Ye fools and blind: for whether is greater, the gift, or the altar that sanctifieth the gift?</font>
39:23:20: <font color="#CC0000">Whoso therefore shall swear by the altar, sweareth by it, and by all things thereon.</font>
39:23:21: <font color="#CC0000">And whoso shall swear by the temple, sweareth by it, and by him that dwelleth therein.</font>
39:23:22: <font color="#CC0000">And he that shall swear by heaven, sweareth by the throne of God, and by him that sitteth thereon.</font>
39:23:23: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye pay tithe of mint and anise and cummin, and have omitted the weightier matters of the law, judgment, mercy, and faith: these ought ye to have done, and not to leave the other undone.</font>
39:23:24: <font color="#CC0000">Ye blind guides, which strain at a gnat, and swallow a camel.</font>
39:23:25: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye make clean the outside of the cup and of the platter, but within they are full of extortion and excess.</font>
39:23:26: <font color="#CC0000">Thou blind Pharisee, cleanse first that which is within the cup and platter, that the outside of them may be clean also.</font>
39:23:27: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye are like unto whited sepulchres, which indeed appear beautiful outward, but are within full of dead men's bones, and of all uncleanness.</font>
