39:23:28: <font color="#CC0000">Even so ye also outwardly appear righteous unto men, but within ye are full of hypocrisy and iniquity.</font>
39:23:29: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! because ye build the tombs of the prophets, and garnish the sepulchres of the righteous,</font>
39:23:30: <font color="#CC0000">And say, If we had been in the days of our fathers, we would not have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.</font>
39:23:31: <font color="#CC0000">Wherefore ye be witnesses unto yourselves, that ye are the children of them which killed the prophets.</font>
39:23:32: <font color="#CC0000">Fill ye up then the measure of your fathers.</font>
39:23:33: <font color="#CC0000">Ye serpents, ye generation of vipers, how can ye escape the damnation of hell?</font>
39:23:34: <font color="#CC0000">Wherefore, behold, I send unto you prophets, and wise men, and scribes: and some of them ye shall kill and crucify; and some of them shall ye scourge in your synagogues, and persecute them from city to city:</font>
39:23:35: <font color="#CC0000">That upon you may come all the righteous blood shed upon the earth, from the blood of righteous Abel unto the blood of Zacharias son of Barachias, whom ye slew between the temple and the altar.</font>
39:23:36: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, All these things shall come upon this generation.</font>
39:23:37: <font color="#CC0000">O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, thou that killest the prophets, and stonest them which are sent unto thee, how often would I have gathered thy children together, even as a hen gathereth her chickens under her wings, and ye would not!</font>
39:23:38: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, your house is left unto you desolate.</font>
39:23:39: <font color="#CC0000">For I say unto you, Ye shall not see me henceforth, till ye shall say, Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.</font>
39:24:2: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">See ye not all these things? verily I say unto you, There shall not be left here one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.</font>
39:24:4: And Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Take heed that no man deceive you.</font>
39:24:5: <font color="#CC0000">For many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and shall deceive many.</font>
39:24:6: <font color="#CC0000">And ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars: see that ye be not troubled: for all these things must come to pass, but the end is not yet.</font>
39:24:7: <font color="#CC0000">For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be famines, and pestilences, and earthquakes, in divers places.</font>
39:24:8: <font color="#CC0000">All these are the beginning of sorrows.</font>
39:24:9: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall they deliver you up to be afflicted, and shall kill you: and ye shall be hated of all nations for my name's sake.</font>
39:24:10: <font color="#CC0000">And then shall many be offended, and shall betray one another, and shall hate one another.</font>
39:24:11: <font color="#CC0000">And many false prophets shall rise, and shall deceive many.</font>
39:24:12: <font color="#CC0000">And because iniquity shall abound, the love of many shall wax cold.</font>
39:24:13: <font color="#CC0000">But he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.</font>
39:24:14: <font color="#CC0000">And this gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in all the world for a witness unto all nations; and then shall the end come.</font>
39:24:15: <font color="#CC0000">When ye therefore shall see the abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, stand in the holy place, (whoso readeth, let him understand:)</font>
39:24:16: <font color="#CC0000">Then let them which be in Judaea flee into the mountains:</font>
39:24:17: <font color="#CC0000">Let him which is on the housetop not come down to take any thing out of his house:</font>
39:24:18: <font color="#CC0000">Neither let him which is in the field return back to take his clothes.</font>
39:24:19: <font color="#CC0000">And woe unto them that are with child, and to them that give suck in those days!</font>
39:24:20: <font color="#CC0000">But pray ye that your flight be not in the winter, neither on the sabbath day:</font>
39:24:21: <font color="#CC0000">For then shall be great tribulation, such as was not since the beginning of the world to this time, no, nor ever shall be.</font>
39:24:22: <font color="#CC0000">And except those days should be shortened, there should no flesh be saved: but for the elect's sake those days shall be shortened.</font>
39:24:23: <font color="#CC0000">Then if any man shall say unto you, Lo, here is Christ, or there; believe it not.</font>
39:24:24: <font color="#CC0000">For there shall arise false Christs, and false prophets, and shall shew great signs and wonders; insomuch that, if it were possible, they shall deceive the very elect.</font>
39:24:25: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, I have told you before.</font>
39:24:26: <font color="#CC0000">Wherefore if they shall say unto you, Behold, he is in the desert; go not forth: behold, he is in the secret chambers; believe it not.</font>
39:24:27: <font color="#CC0000">For as the lightning cometh out of the east, and shineth even unto the west; so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.</font>
39:24:28: <font color="#CC0000">For wheresoever the carcase is, there will the eagles be gathered together.</font>
39:24:29: <font color="#CC0000">Immediately after the tribulation of those days shall the sun be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light, and the stars shall fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens shall be shaken:</font>
39:24:30: <font color="#CC0000">And then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven: and then shall all the tribes of the earth mourn, and they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.</font>
39:24:31: <font color="#CC0000">And he shall send his angels with a great sound of a trumpet, and they shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.</font>
39:24:32: <font color="#CC0000">Now learn a parable of the fig tree; When his branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is nigh:</font>
39:24:33: <font color="#CC0000">So likewise ye, when ye shall see all these things, know that it is near, even at the doors.</font>
39:24:34: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass, till all these things be fulfilled.</font>
39:24:35: <font color="#CC0000">Heaven and earth shall pass away, but my words shall not pass away.</font>
39:24:36: <font color="#CC0000">But of that day and hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels of heaven, but my Father only.</font>
39:24:37: <font color="#CC0000">But as the days of Noe were, so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.</font>
39:24:38: <font color="#CC0000">For as in the days that were before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noe entered into the ark,</font>
39:24:39: <font color="#CC0000">And knew not until the flood came, and took them all away; so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.</font>
39:24:40: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall two be in the field; the one shall be taken, and the other left.</font>
39:24:41: <font color="#CC0000">Two women shall be grinding at the mill; the one shall be taken, and the other left.</font>
39:24:42: <font color="#CC0000">Watch therefore: for ye know not what hour your Lord doth come.</font>
39:24:43: <font color="#CC0000">But know this, that if the goodman of the house had known in what watch the thief would come, he would have watched, and would not have suffered his house to be broken up.</font>
39:24:44: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore be ye also ready: for in such an hour as ye think not the Son of man cometh.</font>
39:24:45: <font color="#CC0000">Who then is a faithful and wise servant, whom his lord hath made ruler over his household, to give them meat in due season?</font>
39:24:46: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed is that servant, whom his lord when he cometh shall find so doing.</font>
39:24:47: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, That he shall make him ruler over all his goods.</font>
39:24:48: <font color="#CC0000">But and if that evil servant shall say in his heart, My lord delayeth his coming;</font>
39:24:49: <font color="#CC0000">And shall begin to smite his fellowservants, and to eat and drink with the drunken;</font>
39:24:50: <font color="#CC0000">The lord of that servant shall come in a day when he looketh not for him, and in an hour that he is not aware of,</font>
39:24:51: <font color="#CC0000">And shall cut him asunder, and appoint him his portion with the hypocrites: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.</font>
39:25:1: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall the kingdom of heaven be likened unto ten virgins, which took their lamps, and went forth to meet the bridegroom.</font>
39:25:2: <font color="#CC0000">And five of them were wise, and five were foolish.</font>
39:25:3: <font color="#CC0000">They that were foolish took their lamps, and took no oil with them:</font>
39:25:4: <font color="#CC0000">But the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps.</font>
39:25:5: <font color="#CC0000">While the bridegroom tarried, they all slumbered and slept.</font>
39:25:6: <font color="#CC0000">And at midnight there was a cry made, Behold, the bridegroom cometh; go ye out to meet him.</font>
39:25:7: <font color="#CC0000">Then all those virgins arose, and trimmed their lamps.</font>
39:25:8: <font color="#CC0000">And the foolish said unto the wise, Give us of your oil; for our lamps are gone out.</font>
39:25:9: <font color="#CC0000">But the wise answered, saying, Not so; lest there be not enough for us and you: but go ye rather to them that sell, and buy for yourselves.</font>
39:25:10: <font color="#CC0000">And while they went to buy, the bridegroom came; and they that were ready went in with him to the marriage: and the door was shut.</font>
39:25:11: <font color="#CC0000">Afterward came also the other virgins, saying, Lord, Lord, open to us.</font>
39:25:12: <font color="#CC0000">But he answered and said, Verily I say unto you, I know you not.</font>
39:25:13: <font color="#CC0000">Watch therefore, for ye know neither the day nor the hour wherein the Son of man cometh.</font>
39:25:14: <font color="#CC0000">For the kingdom of heaven is as a man travelling into a far country, who called his own servants, and delivered unto them his goods.</font>
39:25:15: <font color="#CC0000">And unto one he gave five talents, to another two, and to another one; to every man according to his several ability; and straightway took his journey.</font>
39:25:16: <font color="#CC0000">Then he that had received the five talents went and traded with the same, and made them other five talents.</font>
39:25:17: <font color="#CC0000">And likewise he that had received two, he also gained other two.</font>
39:25:18: <font color="#CC0000">But he that had received one went and digged in the earth, and hid his lord's money.</font>
39:25:19: <font color="#CC0000">After a long time the lord of those servants cometh, and reckoneth with them.</font>
39:25:20: <font color="#CC0000">And so he that had received five talents came and brought other five talents, saying, Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents: behold, I have gained beside them five talents more.</font>
39:25:21: <font color="#CC0000">His lord said unto him, Well done, thou good and faithful servant: thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will make thee ruler over many things: enter thou into the joy of thy lord.</font>
39:25:22: <font color="#CC0000">He also that had received two talents came and said, Lord, thou deliveredst unto me two talents: behold, I have gained two other talents beside them.</font>
39:25:23: <font color="#CC0000">His lord said unto him, Well done, good and faithful servant; thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will make thee ruler over many things: enter thou into the joy of thy lord.</font>
39:25:24: <font color="#CC0000">Then he which had received the one talent came and said, Lord, I knew thee that thou art an hard man, reaping where thou hast not sown, and gathering where thou hast not strawed:</font>
39:25:25: <font color="#CC0000">And I was afraid, and went and hid thy talent in the earth: lo, there thou hast that is thine.</font>
39:25:26: <font color="#CC0000">His lord answered and said unto him, Thou wicked and slothful servant, thou knewest that I reap where I sowed not, and gather where I have not strawed:</font>
39:25:27: <font color="#CC0000">Thou oughtest therefore to have put my money to the exchangers, and then at my coming I should have received mine own with usury.</font>
39:25:28: <font color="#CC0000">Take therefore the talent from him, and give it unto him which hath ten talents.</font>
39:25:29: <font color="#CC0000">For unto every one that hath shall be given, and he shall have abundance: but from him that hath not shall be taken away even that which he hath.</font>
39:25:30: <font color="#CC0000">And cast ye the unprofitable servant into outer darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.</font>
39:25:31: <font color="#CC0000">When the Son of man shall come in his glory, and all the holy angels with him, then shall he sit upon the throne of his glory:</font>
39:25:32: <font color="#CC0000">And before him shall be gathered all nations: and he shall separate them one from another, as a shepherd divideth his sheep from the goats:</font>
39:25:33: <font color="#CC0000">And he shall set the sheep on his right hand, but the goats on the left.</font>
39:25:34: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall the King say unto them on his right hand, Come, ye blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world:</font>
39:25:35: <font color="#CC0000">For I was an hungred, and ye gave me meat: I was thirsty, and ye gave me drink: I was a stranger, and ye took me in:</font>
39:25:36: <font color="#CC0000">Naked, and ye clothed me: I was sick, and ye visited me: I was in prison, and ye came unto me.</font>
39:25:37: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall the righteous answer him, saying, Lord, when saw we thee an hungred, and fed thee? or thirsty, and gave thee drink?</font>
39:25:38: <font color="#CC0000">When saw we thee a stranger, and took thee in? or naked, and clothed thee?</font>
39:25:39: <font color="#CC0000">Or when saw we thee sick, or in prison, and came unto thee?</font>
39:25:40: <font color="#CC0000">And the King shall answer and say unto them, Verily I say unto you, Inasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of these my brethren, ye have done it unto me.</font>
39:25:41: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall he say also unto them on the left hand, Depart from me, ye cursed, into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels:</font>
39:25:42: <font color="#CC0000">For I was an hungred, and ye gave me no meat: I was thirsty, and ye gave me no drink:</font>
39:25:43: <font color="#CC0000">I was a stranger, and ye took me not in: naked, and ye clothed me not: sick, and in prison, and ye visited me not.</font>
39:25:44: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall they also answer him, saying, Lord, when saw we thee an hungred, or athirst, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and did not minister unto thee?</font>
39:25:45: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall he answer them, saying, Verily I say unto you, Inasmuch as ye did it not to one of the least of these, ye did it not to me.</font>
39:25:46: <font color="#CC0000">And these shall go away into everlasting punishment: but the righteous into life eternal.</font>
39:26:2: <font color="#CC0000">Ye know that after two days is the feast of the passover, and the Son of man is betrayed to be crucified.</font>
39:26:10: When Jesus understood it, he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why trouble ye the woman? for she hath wrought a good work upon me.</font>
39:26:11: <font color="#CC0000">For ye have the poor always with you; but me ye have not always.</font>
39:26:12: <font color="#CC0000">For in that she hath poured this ointment on my body, she did it for my burial.</font>
39:26:13: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached in the whole world, there shall also this, that this woman hath done, be told for a memorial of her.</font>
39:26:18: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Go into the city to such a man, and say unto him, The Master saith, My time is at hand; I will keep the passover at thy house with my disciples.</font>
39:26:21: And as they did eat, he said, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.</font>
39:26:23: And he answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">He that dippeth his hand with me in the dish, the same shall betray me.</font>
39:26:24: <font color="#CC0000">The Son of man goeth as it is written of him: but woe unto that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! it had been good for that man if he had not been born.</font>
39:26:25: Then Judas, which betrayed him, answered and said, Master, is it I? He said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou hast said.</font>
39:26:26: And as they were eating, Jesus took bread, and blessed it, and brake it, and gave it to the disciples, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Take, eat; this is my body.</font>
39:26:27: And he took the cup, and gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Drink ye all of it;</font>
39:26:28: <font color="#CC0000">For this is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many for the remission of sins.</font>
39:26:29: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, I will not drink henceforth of this fruit of the vine, until that day when I drink it new with you in my Father's kingdom.</font>
39:26:31: Then saith Jesus unto them, <font color="#CC0000">All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock shall be scattered abroad.</font>
39:26:32: <font color="#CC0000">But after I am risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.</font>
39:26:34: Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto thee, That this night, before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.</font>
39:26:36: Then cometh Jesus with them unto a place called Gethsemane, and saith unto the disciples, <font color="#CC0000">Sit ye here, while I go and pray yonder.</font>
39:26:38: Then saith he unto them, <font color="#CC0000">My soul is exceeding sorrowful, even unto death: tarry ye here, and watch with me.</font>
39:26:39: And he went a little further, and fell on his face, and prayed, saying, <font color="#CC0000">O my Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass from me: nevertheless not as I will, but as thou wilt.</font>
39:26:40: And he cometh unto the disciples, and findeth them asleep, and saith unto Peter, <font color="#CC0000">What, could ye not watch with me one hour?</font>
39:26:41: <font color="#CC0000">Watch and pray, that ye enter not into temptation: the spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.</font>
39:26:42: He went away again the second time, and prayed, saying, <font color="#CC0000">O my Father, if this cup may not pass away from me, except I drink it, thy will be done.</font>
39:26:45: Then cometh he to his disciples, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Sleep on now, and take your rest: behold, the hour is at hand, and the Son of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.</font>
39:26:46: <font color="#CC0000">Rise, let us be going: behold, he is at hand that doth betray me.</font>
39:26:50: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Friend, wherefore art thou come?</font> Then came they, and laid hands on Jesus, and took him.
39:26:52: Then said Jesus unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Put up again thy sword into his place: for all they that take the sword shall perish with the sword.</font>
39:26:53: <font color="#CC0000">Thinkest thou that I cannot now pray to my Father, and he shall presently give me more than twelve legions of angels?</font>
39:26:54: <font color="#CC0000">But how then shall the scriptures be fulfilled, that thus it must be?</font>
39:26:55: In that same hour said Jesus to the multitudes, <font color="#CC0000">Are ye come out as against a thief with swords and staves for to take me? I sat daily with you teaching in the temple, and ye laid no hold on me.</font>
39:26:56: <font color="#CC0000">But all this was done, that the scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled.</font> Then all the disciples forsook him, and fled.
39:26:64: Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou hast said: nevertheless I say unto you, Hereafter shall ye see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of power, and coming in the clouds of heaven.</font>
39:27:11: And Jesus stood before the governor: and the governor asked him, saying, Art thou the King of the Jews? And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou sayest.</font>
39:27:46: And about the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani?</font> that is to say, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?
39:28:9: And as they went to tell his disciples, behold, Jesus met them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">All hail.</font> And they came and held him by the feet, and worshipped him.
39:28:10: Then said Jesus unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Be not afraid: go tell my brethren that they go into Galilee, and there shall they see me.</font>
39:28:18: And Jesus came and spake unto them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth.</font>
39:28:19: <font color="#CC0000">Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost:</font>
39:28:20: <font color="#CC0000">Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you: and, lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world.</font> Amen.
40:1:15: And saying, <font color="#CC0000">The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand: repent ye, and believe the gospel.</font>
40:1:17: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Come ye after me, and I will make you to become fishers of men.</font>
40:1:25: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Hold thy peace, and come out of him.</font>
40:1:38: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Let us go into the next towns, that I may preach there also: for therefore came I forth.</font>
40:1:41: And Jesus, moved with compassion, put forth his hand, and touched him, and saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">I will; be thou clean.</font>
40:1:44: And saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">See thou say nothing to any man: but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing those things which Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.</font>
40:2:5: When Jesus saw their faith, he said unto the sick of the palsy, <font color="#CC0000">Son, thy sins be forgiven thee.</font>
40:2:8: And immediately when Jesus perceived in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why reason ye these things in your hearts?</font>
40:2:9: <font color="#CC0000">Whether is it easier to say to the sick of the palsy, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and take up thy bed, and walk?</font>
40:2:10: <font color="#CC0000">But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins,</font> (he saith to the sick of the palsy,)
40:2:11: <font color="#CC0000">I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy bed, and go thy way into thine house.</font>
40:2:14: And as he passed by, he saw Levi the son of Alphæus sitting at the receipt of custom, and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Follow me.</font> And he arose and followed him.
40:2:17: When Jesus heard it, he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">They that are whole have no need of the physician, but they that are sick: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.</font>
40:2:19: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Can the children of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them? as long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.</font>
40:2:20: <font color="#CC0000">But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.</font>
40:2:21: <font color="#CC0000">No man also seweth a piece of new cloth on an old garment: else the new piece that filled it up taketh away from the old, and the rent is made worse.</font>
40:2:22: <font color="#CC0000">And no man putteth new wine into old bottles: else the new wine doth burst the bottles, and the wine is spilled, and the bottles will be marred: but new wine must be put into new bottles.</font>
40:2:25: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Have ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was an hungred, he, and they that were with him?</font>
40:2:26: <font color="#CC0000">How he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest, and did eat the shewbread, which is not lawful to eat but for the priests, and gave also to them which were with him?</font>
40:2:27: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath:</font>
40:2:28: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.</font>
40:3:3: And he saith unto the man which had the withered hand, <font color="#CC0000">Stand forth.</font>
40:3:4: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath days, or to do evil? to save life, or to kill?</font> But they held their peace.
40:3:5: And when he had looked round about on them with anger, being grieved for the hardness of their hearts, he saith unto the man, <font color="#CC0000">Stretch forth thine hand.</font> And he stretched it out: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
40:3:23: And he called them unto him, and said unto them in parables, <font color="#CC0000">How can Satan cast out Satan?</font>
40:3:24: <font color="#CC0000">And if a kingdom be divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.</font>
40:3:25: <font color="#CC0000">And if a house be divided against itself, that house cannot stand.</font>
40:3:26: <font color="#CC0000">And if Satan rise up against himself, and be divided, he cannot stand, but hath an end.</font>
40:3:27: <font color="#CC0000">No man can enter into a strong man's house, and spoil his goods, except he will first bind the strong man; and then he will spoil his house.</font>
40:3:28: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, All sins shall be forgiven unto the sons of men, and blasphemies wherewith soever they shall blaspheme:</font>
40:3:29: <font color="#CC0000">But he that shall blaspheme against the Holy Ghost hath never forgiveness, but is in danger of eternal damnation:</font>
40:3:33: And he answered them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Who is my mother, or my brethren?</font>
40:3:34: And he looked round about on them which sat about him, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Behold my mother and my brethren!</font>
40:3:35: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever shall do the will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother.</font>
40:4:3: <font color="#CC0000">Hearken; Behold, there went out a sower to sow:</font>
40:4:4: <font color="#CC0000">And it came to pass, as he sowed, some fell by the way side, and the fowls of the air came and devoured it up.</font>
40:4:5: <font color="#CC0000">And some fell on stony ground, where it had not much earth; and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of earth:</font>
40:4:6: <font color="#CC0000">But when the sun was up, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.</font>
40:4:7: <font color="#CC0000">And some fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.</font>
40:4:8: <font color="#CC0000">And other fell on good ground, and did yield fruit that sprang up and increased; and brought forth, some thirty, and some sixty, and some an hundred.</font>
40:4:9: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.</font>
40:4:11: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Unto you it is given to know the mystery of the kingdom of God: but unto them that are without, all these things are done in parables:</font>
40:4:12: <font color="#CC0000">That seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest at any time they should be converted, and their sins should be forgiven them.</font>
40:4:13: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Know ye not this parable? and how then will ye know all parables?</font>
40:4:15: <font color="#CC0000">And these are they by the way side, where the word is sown; but when they have heard, Satan cometh immediately, and taketh away the word that was sown in their hearts.</font>
40:4:16: <font color="#CC0000">And these are they likewise which are sown on stony ground; who, when they have heard the word, immediately receive it with gladness;</font>
40:4:17: <font color="#CC0000">And have no root in themselves, and so endure but for a time: afterward, when affliction or persecution ariseth for the word's sake, immediately they are offended.</font>
40:4:18: <font color="#CC0000">And these are they which are sown among thorns; such as hear the word,</font>
40:4:19: <font color="#CC0000">And the cares of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in, choke the word, and it becometh unfruitful.</font>
40:4:20: <font color="#CC0000">And these are they which are sown on good ground; such as hear the word, and receive it, and bring forth fruit, some thirtyfold, some sixty, and some an hundred.</font>
40:4:21: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Is a candle brought to be put under a bushel, or under a bed? and not to be set on a candlestick?</font>
40:4:22: <font color="#CC0000">For there is nothing hid, which shall not be manifested; neither was any thing kept secret, but that it should come abroad.</font>
40:4:23: <font color="#CC0000">If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.</font>
40:4:24: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Take heed what ye hear: with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you: and unto you that hear shall more be given.</font>
40:4:25: <font color="#CC0000">For he that hath, to him shall be given: and he that hath not, from him shall be taken even that which he hath.</font>
40:4:26: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">So is the kingdom of God, as if a man should cast seed into the ground;</font>
40:4:27: <font color="#CC0000">And should sleep, and rise night and day, and the seed should spring and grow up, he knoweth not how.</font>
40:4:28: <font color="#CC0000">For the earth bringeth forth fruit of herself; first the blade, then the ear, after that the full corn in the ear.</font>
40:4:29: <font color="#CC0000">But when the fruit is brought forth, immediately he putteth in the sickle, because the harvest is come.</font>
40:4:30: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Whereunto shall we liken the kingdom of God? or with what comparison shall we compare it?</font>
40:4:31: <font color="#CC0000">It is like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown in the earth, is less than all the seeds that be in the earth:</font>
40:4:32: <font color="#CC0000">But when it is sown, it groweth up, and becometh greater than all herbs, and shooteth out great branches; so that the fowls of the air may lodge under the shadow of it.</font>
40:4:35: And the same day, when the even was come, he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Let us pass over unto the other side.</font>
40:4:39: And he arose, and rebuked the wind, and said unto the sea, <font color="#CC0000">Peace, be still.</font> And the wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
40:4:40: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why are ye so fearful? how is it that ye have no faith?</font>
40:5:8: For he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Come out of the man, thou unclean spirit.</font>
40:5:9: And he asked him, <font color="#CC0000">What is thy name?</font> And he answered, saying, My name is Legion: for we are many.
40:5:19: Howbeit Jesus suffered him not, but saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Go home to thy friends, and tell them how great things the Lord hath done for thee, and hath had compassion on thee.</font>
40:5:30: And Jesus, immediately knowing in himself that virtue had gone out of him, turned him about in the press, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Who touched my clothes?</font>
40:5:34: And he said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Daughter, thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace, and be whole of thy plague.</font>
40:5:36: As soon as Jesus heard the word that was spoken, he saith unto the ruler of the synagogue, <font color="#CC0000">Be not afraid, only believe.</font>
40:5:39: And when he was come in, he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why make ye this ado, and weep? the damsel is not dead, but sleepeth.</font>
40:5:41: And he took the damsel by the hand, and said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Talitha cumi;</font> which is, being interpreted, Damsel, I say unto thee, arise.
40:6:4: But Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">A prophet is not without honour, but in his own country, and among his own kin, and in his own house.</font>
40:6:10: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">In what place soever ye enter into an house, there abide till ye depart from that place.</font>
40:6:11: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear you, when ye depart thence, shake off the dust under your feet for a testimony against them. Verily I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for that city.</font>
40:6:31: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Come ye yourselves apart into a desert place, and rest a while:</font> for there were many coming and going, and they had no leisure so much as to eat.
40:6:37: He answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Give ye them to eat.</font> And they say unto him, Shall we go and buy two hundred pennyworth of bread, and give them to eat?
40:6:38: He saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">How many loaves have ye? go and see.</font> And when they knew, they say, Five, and two fishes.
40:6:50: For they all saw him, and were troubled. And immediately he talked with them, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Be of good cheer: it is I; be not afraid.</font>
40:7:6: He answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Well hath Esaias prophesied of you hypocrites, as it is written, This people honoureth me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.</font>
40:7:7: <font color="#CC0000">Howbeit in vain do they worship me, teaching for doctrines the commandments of men.</font>
40:7:8: <font color="#CC0000">For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men, as the washing of pots and cups: and many other such like things ye do.</font>
40:7:9: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Full well ye reject the commandment of God, that ye may keep your own tradition.</font>
40:7:10: <font color="#CC0000">For Moses said, Honour thy father and thy mother; and, Whoso curseth father or mother, let him die the death:</font>
40:7:11: <font color="#CC0000">But ye say, If a man shall say to his father or mother, It is Corban, that is to say, a gift, by whatsoever thou mightest be profited by me; he shall be free.</font>
40:7:12: <font color="#CC0000">And ye suffer him no more to do ought for his father or his mother;</font>
40:7:13: <font color="#CC0000">Making the word of God of none effect through your tradition, which ye have delivered: and many such like things do ye.</font>
40:7:14: And when he had called all the people unto him, he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Hearken unto me every one of you, and understand:</font>
40:7:15: <font color="#CC0000">There is nothing from without a man, that entering into him can defile him: but the things which come out of him, those are they that defile the man.</font>
40:7:16: <font color="#CC0000">If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.</font>
40:7:18: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Are ye so without understanding also? Do ye not perceive, that whatsoever thing from without entereth into the man, it cannot defile him;</font>
40:7:19: <font color="#CC0000">Because it entereth not into his heart, but into the belly, and goeth out into the draught, purging all meats?</font>
40:7:20: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">That which cometh out of the man, that defileth the man.</font>
40:7:21: <font color="#CC0000">For from within, out of the heart of men, proceed evil thoughts, adulteries, fornications, murders,</font>
40:7:22: <font color="#CC0000">Thefts, covetousness, wickedness, deceit, lasciviousness, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, foolishness:</font>
40:7:23: <font color="#CC0000">All these evil things come from within, and defile the man.</font>
40:7:27: But Jesus said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Let the children first be filled: for it is not meet to take the children's bread, and to cast it unto the dogs.</font>
40:7:29: And he said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">For this saying go thy way; the devil is gone out of thy daughter.</font>
40:7:34: And looking up to heaven, he sighed, and saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Ephphatha,</font> that is, Be opened.
40:8:2: <font color="#CC0000">I have compassion on the multitude, because they have now been with me three days, and have nothing to eat:</font>
40:8:3: <font color="#CC0000">And if I send them away fasting to their own houses, they will faint by the way: for divers of them came from far.</font>
40:8:5: And he asked them, <font color="#CC0000">How many loaves have ye?</font> And they said, Seven.
40:8:12: And he sighed deeply in his spirit, and saith, <font color="#CC0000">Why doth this generation seek after a sign? verily I say unto you, There shall no sign be given unto this generation.</font>
40:8:15: And he charged them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and of the leaven of Herod.</font>
40:8:17: And when Jesus knew it, he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why reason ye, because ye have no bread? perceive ye not yet, neither understand? have ye your heart yet hardened?</font>
40:8:18: <font color="#CC0000">Having eyes, see ye not? and having ears, hear ye not? and do ye not remember?</font>
40:8:19: <font color="#CC0000">When I brake the five loaves among five thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up?</font> They say unto him, Twelve.
40:8:20: <font color="#CC0000">And when the seven among four thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up?</font> And they said, Seven.
40:8:21: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">How is it that ye do not understand?</font>
40:8:26: And he sent him away to his house, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Neither go into the town, nor tell it to any in the town.</font>
40:8:27: And Jesus went out, and his disciples, into the towns of Cæsarea Philippi: and by the way he asked his disciples, saying unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Whom do men say that I am?</font>
40:8:29: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">But whom say ye that I am?</font> And Peter answereth and saith unto him, Thou art the Christ.
40:8:33: But when he had turned about and looked on his disciples, he rebuked Peter, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Get thee behind me, Satan: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but the things that be of men.</font>
40:8:34: And when he had called the people unto him with his disciples also, he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.</font>
40:8:35: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever will save his life shall lose it; but whosoever shall lose his life for my sake and the gospel's, the same shall save it.</font>
40:8:36: <font color="#CC0000">For what shall it profit a man, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul?</font>
40:8:37: <font color="#CC0000">Or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?</font>
40:8:38: <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever therefore shall be ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation; of him also shall the Son of man be ashamed, when he cometh in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.</font>
40:9:1: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, That there be some of them that stand here, which shall not taste of death, till they have seen the kingdom of God come with power.</font>
40:9:12: And he answered and told them, <font color="#CC0000">Elias verily cometh first, and restoreth all things; and how it is written of the Son of man, that he must suffer many things, and be set at nought.</font>
40:9:13: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, That Elias is indeed come, and they have done unto him whatsoever they listed, as it is written of him.</font>
40:9:16: And he asked the scribes, <font color="#CC0000">What question ye with them?</font>
40:9:19: He answereth him, and saith, <font color="#CC0000">O faithless generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him unto me.</font>
40:9:21: And he asked his father, <font color="#CC0000">How long is it ago since this came unto him?</font> And he said, Of a child.
40:9:23: Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">If thou canst believe, all things are possible to him that believeth.</font>
40:9:25: When Jesus saw that the people came running together, he rebuked the foul spirit, saying unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou dumb and deaf spirit, I charge thee, come out of him, and enter no more into him.</font>
40:9:29: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">This kind can come forth by nothing, but by prayer and fasting.</font>
40:9:31: For he taught his disciples, and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">The Son of man is delivered into the hands of men, and they shall kill him; and after that he is killed, he shall rise the third day.</font>
40:9:33: And he came to Capernaum: and being in the house he asked them, <font color="#CC0000">What was it that ye disputed among yourselves by the way?</font>
40:9:35: And he sat down, and called the twelve, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">If any man desire to be first, the same shall be last of all, and servant of all.</font>
40:9:37: <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever shall receive one of such children in my name, receiveth me: and whosoever shall receive me, receiveth not me, but him that sent me.</font>
40:9:39: But Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">Forbid him not: for there is no man which shall do a miracle in my name, that can lightly speak evil of me.</font>
40:9:40: <font color="#CC0000">For he that is not against us is on our part.</font>
40:9:41: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever shall give you a cup of water to drink in my name, because ye belong to Christ, verily I say unto you, he shall not lose his reward.</font>
40:9:42: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever shall offend one of these little ones that believe in me, it is better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and he were cast into the sea.</font>
40:9:43: <font color="#CC0000">And if thy hand offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter into life maimed, than having two hands to go into hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:</font>
40:9:44: <font color="#CC0000">Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.</font>
40:9:45: <font color="#CC0000">And if thy foot offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter halt into life, than having two feet to be cast into hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:</font>
40:9:46: <font color="#CC0000">Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.</font>
40:9:47: <font color="#CC0000">And if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out: it is better for thee to enter into the kingdom of God with one eye, than having two eyes to be cast into hell fire:</font>
40:9:48: <font color="#CC0000">Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.</font>
40:9:49: <font color="#CC0000">For every one shall be salted with fire, and every sacrifice shall be salted with salt.</font>
40:9:50: <font color="#CC0000">Salt is good: but if the salt have lost his saltness, wherewith will ye season it? Have salt in yourselves, and have peace one with another.</font>
40:10:3: And he answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">What did Moses command you?</font>
40:10:5: And Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">For the hardness of your heart he wrote you this precept.</font>
40:10:6: <font color="#CC0000">But from the beginning of the creation God made them male and female.</font>
40:10:7: <font color="#CC0000">For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and cleave to his wife;</font>
40:10:8: <font color="#CC0000">And they twain shall be one flesh: so then they are no more twain, but one flesh.</font>
40:10:9: <font color="#CC0000">What therefore God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.</font>
40:10:11: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever shall put away his wife, and marry another, committeth adultery against her.</font>
40:10:12: <font color="#CC0000">And if a woman shall put away her husband, and be married to another, she committeth adultery.</font>
40:10:14: But when Jesus saw it, he was much displeased, and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Suffer the little children to come unto me, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdom of God.</font>
40:10:15: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child, he shall not enter therein.</font>
40:10:18: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Why callest thou me good? there is none good but one, that is, God.</font>
40:10:19: <font color="#CC0000">Thou knowest the commandments, Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Defraud not, Honour thy father and mother.</font>
40:10:21: Then Jesus beholding him loved him, and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">One thing thou lackest: go thy way, sell whatsoever thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, take up the cross, and follow me.</font>
40:10:23: And Jesus looked round about, and saith unto his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God!</font>
40:10:24: And the disciples were astonished at his words. But Jesus answereth again, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Children, how hard is it for them that trust in riches to enter into the kingdom of God!</font>
40:10:25: <font color="#CC0000">It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.</font>
40:10:27: And Jesus looking upon them saith, <font color="#CC0000">With men it is impossible, but not with God: for with God all things are possible.</font>
40:10:29: And Jesus answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my sake, and the gospel's,</font>
40:10:30: <font color="#CC0000">But he shall receive an hundredfold now in this time, houses, and brethren, and sisters, and mothers, and children, and lands, with persecutions; and in the world to come eternal life.</font>
40:10:31: <font color="#CC0000">But many that are first shall be last; and the last first.</font>
40:10:33: Saying, <font color="#CC0000">Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be delivered unto the chief priests, and unto the scribes; and they shall condemn him to death, and shall deliver him to the Gentiles:</font>
40:10:34: <font color="#CC0000">And they shall mock him, and shall scourge him, and shall spit upon him, and shall kill him: and the third day he shall rise again.</font>
40:10:36: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">What would ye that I should do for you?</font>
40:10:38: But Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Ye know not what ye ask: can ye drink of the cup that I drink of? and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?</font>
40:10:39: And they said unto him, We can. And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Ye shall indeed drink of the cup that I drink of; and with the baptism that I am baptized withal shall ye be baptized:</font>
40:10:40: <font color="#CC0000">But to sit on my right hand and on my left hand is not mine to give; but it shall be given to them for whom it is prepared.</font>
40:10:42: But Jesus called them to him, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Ye know that they which are accounted to rule over the Gentiles exercise lordship over them; and their great ones exercise authority upon them.</font>
40:10:43: <font color="#CC0000">But so shall it not be among you: but whosoever will be great among you, shall be your minister:</font>
40:10:44: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever of you will be the chiefest, shall be servant of all.</font>
40:10:45: <font color="#CC0000">For even the Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom for many.</font>
40:10:51: And Jesus answered and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">What wilt thou that I should do unto thee?</font> The blind man said unto him, Lord, that I might receive my sight.
40:10:52: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Go thy way; thy faith hath made thee whole.</font> And immediately he received his sight, and followed Jesus in the way.
40:11:2: And saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Go your way into the village over against you: and as soon as ye be entered into it, ye shall find a colt tied, whereon never man sat; loose him, and bring him.</font>
40:11:3: <font color="#CC0000">And if any man say unto you, Why do ye this? say ye that the Lord hath need of him; and straightway he will send him hither.</font>
40:11:14: And Jesus answered and said unto it, <font color="#CC0000">No man eat fruit of thee hereafter for ever.</font> And his disciples heard it.
40:11:17: And he taught, saying unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Is it not written, My house shall be called of all nations the house of prayer? but ye have made it a den of thieves.</font>
40:11:22: And Jesus answering saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Have faith in God.</font>
40:11:23: <font color="#CC0000">For verily I say unto you, That whosoever shall say unto this mountain, Be thou removed, and be thou cast into the sea; and shall not doubt in his heart, but shall believe that those things which he saith shall come to pass; he shall have whatsoever he saith.</font>
40:11:24: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore I say unto you, What things soever ye desire, when ye pray, believe that ye receive them, and ye shall have them.</font>
40:11:25: <font color="#CC0000">And when ye stand praying, forgive, if ye have ought against any: that your Father also which is in heaven may forgive you your trespasses.</font>
40:11:26: <font color="#CC0000">But if ye do not forgive, neither will your Father which is in heaven forgive your trespasses.</font>
40:11:29: And Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I will also ask of you one question, and answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.</font>
40:11:30: <font color="#CC0000">The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men? answer me.</font>
40:11:33: And they answered and said unto Jesus, We cannot tell. And Jesus answering saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.</font>
40:12:1: And he began to speak unto them by parables. <font color="#CC0000">A certain man planted a vineyard, and set an hedge about it, and digged a place for the winefat, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen, and went into a far country.</font>
40:12:2: <font color="#CC0000">And at the season he sent to the husbandmen a servant, that he might receive from the husbandmen of the fruit of the vineyard.</font>
40:12:3: <font color="#CC0000">And they caught him, and beat him, and sent him away empty.</font>
40:12:4: <font color="#CC0000">And again he sent unto them another servant; and at him they cast stones, and wounded him in the head, and sent him away shamefully handled.</font>
40:12:5: <font color="#CC0000">And again he sent another; and him they killed, and many others; beating some, and killing some.</font>
40:12:6: <font color="#CC0000">Having yet therefore one son, his wellbeloved, he sent him also last unto them, saying, They will reverence my son.</font>
40:12:7: <font color="#CC0000">But those husbandmen said among themselves, This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance shall be ours.</font>
40:12:8: <font color="#CC0000">And they took him, and killed him, and cast him out of the vineyard.</font>
40:12:9: <font color="#CC0000">What shall therefore the lord of the vineyard do? he will come and destroy the husbandmen, and will give the vineyard unto others.</font>
40:12:10: <font color="#CC0000">And have ye not read this scripture; The stone which the builders rejected is become the head of the corner:</font>
40:12:11: <font color="#CC0000">This was the Lord's doing, and it is marvellous in our eyes?</font>
40:12:15: Shall we give, or shall we not give? But he, knowing their hypocrisy, said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why tempt ye me? bring me a penny, that I may see it.</font>
40:12:16: And they brought it. And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Whose is this image and superscription?</font> And they said unto him, Cæsar's.
40:12:17: And Jesus answering said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Render to Cæsar the things that are Cæsar's, and to God the things that are God's.</font> And they marvelled at him.
40:12:24: And Jesus answering said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Do ye not therefore err, because ye know not the scriptures, neither the power of God?</font>
40:12:25: <font color="#CC0000">For when they shall rise from the dead, they neither marry, nor are given in marriage; but are as the angels which are in heaven.</font>
40:12:26: <font color="#CC0000">And as touching the dead, that they rise: have ye not read in the book of Moses, how in the bush God spake unto him, saying, I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?</font>
40:12:27: <font color="#CC0000">He is not the God of the dead, but the God of the living: ye therefore do greatly err.</font>
40:12:29: And Jesus answered him, <font color="#CC0000">The first of all the commandments is, Hear, O Israel; The Lord our God is one Lord:</font>
40:12:30: <font color="#CC0000">And thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind, and with all thy strength: this is the first commandment.</font>
40:12:31: <font color="#CC0000">And the second is like, namely this, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself. There is none other commandment greater than these.</font>
40:12:34: And when Jesus saw that he answered discreetly, he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou art not far from the kingdom of God.</font> And no man after that durst ask him any question.
40:12:35: And Jesus answered and said, while he taught in the temple, <font color="#CC0000">How say the scribes that Christ is the Son of David?</font>
40:12:36: <font color="#CC0000">For David himself said by the Holy Ghost, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, till I make thine enemies thy footstool.</font>
40:12:37: <font color="#CC0000">David therefore himself calleth him Lord; and whence is he then his son? And the common people heard him gladly.</font>
40:12:38: And he said unto them in his doctrine, <font color="#CC0000">Beware of the scribes, which love to go in long clothing, and love salutations in the marketplaces,</font>
40:12:39: <font color="#CC0000">And the chief seats in the synagogues, and the uppermost rooms at feasts:</font>
40:12:40: <font color="#CC0000">Which devour widows' houses, and for a pretence make long prayers: these shall receive greater damnation.</font>
40:12:43: And he called unto him his disciples, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, That this poor widow hath cast more in, than all they which have cast into the treasury:</font>
40:12:44: <font color="#CC0000">For all they did cast in of their abundance; but she of her want did cast in all that she had, even all her living.</font>
40:13:2: And Jesus answering said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Seest thou these great buildings? there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.</font>
40:13:5: And Jesus answering them began to say, <font color="#CC0000">Take heed lest any man deceive you:</font>
40:13:6: <font color="#CC0000">For many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and shall deceive many.</font>
40:13:7: <font color="#CC0000">And when ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars, be ye not troubled: for such things must needs be; but the end shall not be yet.</font>
40:13:8: <font color="#CC0000">For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be earthquakes in divers places, and there shall be famines and troubles: these are the beginnings of sorrows.</font>
40:13:9: <font color="#CC0000">But take heed to yourselves: for they shall deliver you up to councils; and in the synagogues ye shall be beaten: and ye shall be brought before rulers and kings for my sake, for a testimony against them.</font>
40:13:10: <font color="#CC0000">And the gospel must first be published among all nations.</font>
40:13:11: <font color="#CC0000">But when they shall lead you, and deliver you up, take no thought beforehand what ye shall speak, neither do ye premeditate: but whatsoever shall be given you in that hour, that speak ye: for it is not ye that speak, but the Holy Ghost.</font>
40:13:12: <font color="#CC0000">Now the brother shall betray the brother to death, and the father the son; and children shall rise up against their parents, and shall cause them to be put to death.</font>
40:13:13: <font color="#CC0000">And ye shall be hated of all men for my name's sake: but he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.</font>
40:13:14: <font color="#CC0000">But when ye shall see the abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing where it ought not, (let him that readeth understand,) then let them that be in Judæa flee to the mountains:</font>
40:13:15: <font color="#CC0000">And let him that is on the housetop not go down into the house, neither enter therein, to take any thing out of his house:</font>
40:13:16: <font color="#CC0000">And let him that is in the field not turn back again for to take up his garment.</font>
40:13:17: <font color="#CC0000">But woe to them that are with child, and to them that give suck in those days!</font>
40:13:18: <font color="#CC0000">And pray ye that your flight be not in the winter.</font>
40:13:19: <font color="#CC0000">For in those days shall be affliction, such as was not from the beginning of the creation which God created unto this time, neither shall be.</font>
40:13:20: <font color="#CC0000">And except that the Lord had shortened those days, no flesh should be saved: but for the elect's sake, whom he hath chosen, he hath shortened the days.</font>
40:13:21: <font color="#CC0000">And then if any man shall say to you, Lo, here is Christ; or, lo, he is there; believe him not:</font>
40:13:22: <font color="#CC0000">For false Christs and false prophets shall rise, and shall shew signs and wonders, to seduce, if it were possible, even the elect.</font>
40:13:23: <font color="#CC0000">But take ye heed: behold, I have foretold you all things.</font>
40:13:24: <font color="#CC0000">But in those days, after that tribulation, the sun shall be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light,</font>
40:13:25: <font color="#CC0000">And the stars of heaven shall fall, and the powers that are in heaven shall be shaken.</font>
40:13:26: <font color="#CC0000">And then shall they see the Son of man coming in the clouds with great power and glory.</font>
40:13:27: <font color="#CC0000">And then shall he send his angels, and shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from the uttermost part of the earth to the uttermost part of heaven.</font>
40:13:28: <font color="#CC0000">Now learn a parable of the fig tree; When her branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is near:</font>
40:13:29: <font color="#CC0000">So ye in like manner, when ye shall see these things come to pass, know that it is nigh, even at the doors.</font>
40:13:30: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, that this generation shall not pass, till all these things be done.</font>
40:13:31: <font color="#CC0000">Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my words shall not pass away.</font>
40:13:32: <font color="#CC0000">But of that day and that hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels which are in heaven, neither the Son, but the Father.</font>
40:13:33: <font color="#CC0000">Take ye heed, watch and pray: for ye know not when the time is.</font>
40:13:34: <font color="#CC0000">For the Son of man is as a man taking a far journey, who left his house, and gave authority to his servants, and to every man his work, and commanded the porter to watch.</font>
40:13:35: <font color="#CC0000">Watch ye therefore: for ye know not when the master of the house cometh, at even, or at midnight, or at the cockcrowing, or in the morning:</font>
40:13:36: <font color="#CC0000">Lest coming suddenly he find you sleeping.</font>
40:13:37: <font color="#CC0000">And what I say unto you I say unto all, Watch.</font>
40:14:6: And Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">Let her alone; why trouble ye her? she hath wrought a good work on me.</font>
40:14:7: <font color="#CC0000">For ye have the poor with you always, and whensoever ye will ye may do them good: but me ye have not always.</font>
40:14:8: <font color="#CC0000">She hath done what she could: she is come aforehand to anoint my body to the burying.</font>
40:14:9: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached throughout the whole world, this also that she hath done shall be spoken of for a memorial of her.</font>
40:14:13: And he sendeth forth two of his disciples, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Go ye into the city, and there shall meet you a man bearing a pitcher of water: follow him.</font>
40:14:14: <font color="#CC0000">And wheresoever he shall go in, say ye to the goodman of the house, The Master saith, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples?</font>
40:14:15: <font color="#CC0000">And he will shew you a large upper room furnished and prepared: there make ready for us.</font>
40:14:18: And as they sat and did eat, Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, One of you which eateth with me shall betray me.</font>
40:14:20: And he answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">It is one of the twelve, that dippeth with me in the dish.</font>
40:14:21: <font color="#CC0000">The Son of man indeed goeth, as it is written of him: but woe to that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! good were it for that man if he had never been born.</font>
40:14:22: And as they did eat, Jesus took bread, and blessed, and brake it, and gave to them, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Take, eat: this is my body.</font>
40:14:24: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">This is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many.</font>
40:14:25: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, I will drink no more of the fruit of the vine, until that day that I drink it new in the kingdom of God.</font>
40:14:27: And Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep shall be scattered.</font>
40:14:28: <font color="#CC0000">But after that I am risen, I will go before you into Galilee.</font>
40:14:30: And Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto thee, That this day, even in this night, before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice.</font>
40:14:32: And they came to a place which was named Gethsemane: and he saith to his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">Sit ye here, while I shall pray.</font>
40:14:34: And saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">My soul is exceeding sorrowful unto death: tarry ye here, and watch.</font>
40:14:36: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Abba, Father, all things are possible unto thee; take away this cup from me: nevertheless not what I will, but what thou wilt.</font>
40:14:37: And he cometh, and findeth them sleeping, and saith unto Peter, <font color="#CC0000">Simon, sleepest thou? couldest not thou watch one hour?</font>
40:14:38: <font color="#CC0000">Watch ye and pray, lest ye enter into temptation. The spirit truly is ready, but the flesh is weak.</font>
40:14:41: And he cometh the third time, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Sleep on now, and take your rest: it is enough, the hour is come; behold, the Son of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.</font>
40:14:42: <font color="#CC0000">Rise up, let us go; lo, he that betrayeth me is at hand.</font>
40:14:48: And Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Are ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and with staves to take me?</font>
40:14:49: <font color="#CC0000">I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and ye took me not: but the scriptures must be fulfilled.</font>
40:14:62: And Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">I am: and ye shall see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of power, and coming in the clouds of heaven.</font>
40:14:72: And the second time the cock crew. And Peter called to mind the word that Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice.</font> And when he thought thereon, he wept.
40:15:2: And Pilate asked him, Art thou the King of the Jews? And he answering said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou sayest it.</font>
40:15:34: And at the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani?</font> which is, being interpreted, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?
40:16:15: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Go ye into all the world, and preach the gospel to every creature.</font>
40:16:16: <font color="#CC0000">He that believeth and is baptized shall be saved; but he that believeth not shall be damned.</font>
40:16:17: <font color="#CC0000">And these signs shall follow them that believe; In my name shall they cast out devils; they shall speak with new tongues;</font>
40:16:18: <font color="#CC0000">They shall take up serpents; and if they drink any deadly thing, it shall not hurt them; they shall lay hands on the sick, and they shall recover.</font>
41:2:49: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">How is it that ye sought me? wist ye not that I must be about my Father's business?</font>
41:4:4: And Jesus answered him, saying, <font color="#CC0000">It is written, That man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.</font>
41:4:8: And Jesus answered and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Get thee behind me, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.</font>
41:4:12: And Jesus answering said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">It is said, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.</font>
41:4:18: <font color="#CC0000">The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he hath anointed me to preach the gospel to the poor; he hath sent me to heal the brokenhearted, to preach deliverance to the captives, and recovering of sight to the blind, to set at liberty them that are bruised,</font>
41:4:19: <font color="#CC0000">To preach the acceptable year of the Lord.</font>
41:4:21: And he began to say unto them, <font color="#CC0000">This day is this scripture fulfilled in your ears.</font>
41:4:23: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Ye will surely say unto me this proverb, Physician, heal thyself: whatsoever we have heard done in Capernaum, do also here in thy country.</font>
41:4:24: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, No prophet is accepted in his own country.</font>
41:4:25: <font color="#CC0000">But I tell you of a truth, many widows were in Israel in the days of Elias, when the heaven was shut up three years and six months, when great famine was throughout all the land;</font>
41:4:26: <font color="#CC0000">But unto none of them was Elias sent, save unto Sarepta, a city of Sidon, unto a woman that was a widow.</font>
41:4:27: <font color="#CC0000">And many lepers were in Israel in the time of Eliseus the prophet; and none of them was cleansed, saving Naaman the Syrian.</font>
41:4:35: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Hold thy peace, and come out of him.</font> And when the devil had thrown him in the midst, he came out of him, and hurt him not.
41:4:43: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I must preach the kingdom of God to other cities also: for therefore am I sent.</font>
41:5:4: Now when he had left speaking, he said unto Simon, <font color="#CC0000">Launch out into the deep, and let down your nets for a draught.</font>
41:5:10: And so was also James, and John, the sons of Zebedee, which were partners with Simon. And Jesus said unto Simon, <font color="#CC0000">Fear not; from henceforth thou shalt catch men.</font>
41:5:13: And he put forth his hand, and touched him, saying, <font color="#CC0000">I will: be thou clean.</font> And immediately the leprosy departed from him.
41:5:14: And he charged him to tell no man: <font color="#CC0000">but go, and shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing, according as Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.</font>
41:5:20: And when he saw their faith, he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Man, thy sins are forgiven thee.</font>
41:5:22: But when Jesus perceived their thoughts, he answering said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">What reason ye in your hearts?</font>
41:5:23: <font color="#CC0000">Whether is easier, to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Rise up and walk?</font>
41:5:24: <font color="#CC0000">But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power upon earth to forgive sins,</font> (he said unto the sick of the palsy,) <font color="#CC0000">I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy couch, and go into thine house.</font>
41:5:27: And after these things he went forth, and saw a publican, named Levi, sitting at the receipt of custom: and he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Follow me.</font>
41:5:31: And Jesus answering said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">They that are whole need not a physician; but they that are sick.</font>
41:5:32: <font color="#CC0000">I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.</font>
41:5:34: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Can ye make the children of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them?</font>
41:5:35: <font color="#CC0000">But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.</font>
41:5:36: And he spake also a parable unto them; <font color="#CC0000">No man putteth a piece of a new garment upon an old; if otherwise, then both the new maketh a rent, and the piece that was taken out of the new agreeth not with the old.</font>
41:5:37: <font color="#CC0000">And no man putteth new wine into old bottles; else the new wine will burst the bottles, and be spilled, and the bottles shall perish.</font>
41:5:38: <font color="#CC0000">But new wine must be put into new bottles; and both are preserved.</font>
41:5:39: <font color="#CC0000">No man also having drunk old wine straightway desireth new: for he saith, The old is better.</font>
41:6:3: And Jesus answering them said, <font color="#CC0000">Have ye not read so much as this, what David did, when himself was an hungred, and they which were with him;</font>
41:6:4: <font color="#CC0000">How he went into the house of God, and did take and eat the shewbread, and gave also to them that were with him; which it is not lawful to eat but for the priests alone?</font>
41:6:5: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">That the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.</font>
41:6:8: But he knew their thoughts, and said to the man which had the withered hand, <font color="#CC0000">Rise up, and stand forth in the midst.</font> And he arose and stood forth.
41:6:9: Then said Jesus unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I will ask you one thing; Is it lawful on the sabbath days to do good, or to do evil? to save life, or to destroy it?</font>
41:6:10: And looking round about upon them all, he said unto the man, <font color="#CC0000">Stretch forth thy hand.</font> And he did so: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
41:6:20: And he lifted up his eyes on his disciples, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Blessed be ye poor: for yours is the kingdom of God.</font>
41:6:21: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are ye that hunger now: for ye shall be filled. Blessed are ye that weep now: for ye shall laugh.</font>
41:6:22: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are ye, when men shall hate you, and when they shall separate you from their company, and shall reproach you, and cast out your name as evil, for the Son of man's sake.</font>
41:6:23: <font color="#CC0000">Rejoice ye in that day, and leap for joy: for, behold, your reward is great in heaven: for in the like manner did their fathers unto the prophets.</font>
41:6:24: <font color="#CC0000">But woe unto you that are rich! for ye have received your consolation.</font>
41:6:25: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you that are full! for ye shall hunger. Woe unto you that laugh now! for ye shall mourn and weep.</font>
41:6:26: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you, when all men shall speak well of you! for so did their fathers to the false prophets.</font>
41:6:27: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you which hear, Love your enemies, do good to them which hate you,</font>
41:6:28: <font color="#CC0000">Bless them that curse you, and pray for them which despitefully use you.</font>
41:6:29: <font color="#CC0000">And unto him that smiteth thee on the one cheek offer also the other; and him that taketh away thy cloke forbid not to take thy coat also.</font>
41:6:30: <font color="#CC0000">Give to every man that asketh of thee; and of him that taketh away thy goods ask them not again.</font>
41:6:31: <font color="#CC0000">And as ye would that men should do to you, do ye also to them likewise.</font>
41:6:32: <font color="#CC0000">For if ye love them which love you, what thank have ye? for sinners also love those that love them.</font>
41:6:33: <font color="#CC0000">And if ye do good to them which do good to you, what thank have ye? for sinners also do even the same.</font>
41:6:34: <font color="#CC0000">And if ye lend to them of whom ye hope to receive, what thank have ye? for sinners also lend to sinners, to receive as much again.</font>
41:6:35: <font color="#CC0000">But love ye your enemies, and do good, and lend, hoping for nothing again; and your reward shall be great, and ye shall be the children of the Highest: for he is kind unto the unthankful and to the evil.</font>
41:6:36: <font color="#CC0000">Be ye therefore merciful, as your Father also is merciful.</font>
41:6:37: <font color="#CC0000">Judge not, and ye shall not be judged: condemn not, and ye shall not be condemned: forgive, and ye shall be forgiven:</font>
41:6:38: <font color="#CC0000">Give, and it shall be given unto you; good measure, pressed down, and shaken together, and running over, shall men give into your bosom. For with the same measure that ye mete withal it shall be measured to you again.</font>
41:6:39: And he spake a parable unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Can the blind lead the blind? shall they not both fall into the ditch?</font>
41:6:40: <font color="#CC0000">The disciple is not above his master: but every one that is perfect shall be as his master.</font>
41:6:41: <font color="#CC0000">And why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brother's eye, but perceivest not the beam that is in thine own eye?</font>
41:6:42: <font color="#CC0000">Either how canst thou say to thy brother, Brother, let me pull out the mote that is in thine eye, when thou thyself beholdest not the beam that is in thine own eye? Thou hypocrite, cast out first the beam out of thine own eye, and then shalt thou see clearly to pull out the mote that is in thy brother's eye.</font>
41:6:43: <font color="#CC0000">For a good tree bringeth not forth corrupt fruit; neither doth a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.</font>
41:6:44: <font color="#CC0000">For every tree is known by his own fruit. For of thorns men do not gather figs, nor of a bramble bush gather they grapes.</font>
41:6:45: <font color="#CC0000">A good man out of the good treasure of his heart bringeth forth that which is good; and an evil man out of the evil treasure of his heart bringeth forth that which is evil: for of the abundance of the heart his mouth speaketh.</font>
41:6:46: <font color="#CC0000">And why call ye me, Lord, Lord, and do not the things which I say?</font>
41:6:47: <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever cometh to me, and heareth my sayings, and doeth them, I will shew you to whom he is like:</font>
41:6:48: <font color="#CC0000">He is like a man which built an house, and digged deep, and laid the foundation on a rock: and when the flood arose, the stream beat vehemently upon that house, and could not shake it: for it was founded upon a rock.</font>
41:6:49: <font color="#CC0000">But he that heareth, and doeth not, is like a man that without a foundation built an house upon the earth; against which the stream did beat vehemently, and immediately it fell; and the ruin of that house was great.</font>
41:7:9: When Jesus heard these things, he marvelled at him, and turned him about, and said unto the people that followed him, <font color="#CC0000">I say unto you, I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel.</font>
41:7:13: And when the Lord saw her, he had compassion on her, and said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Weep not.</font>
41:7:14: And he came and touched the bier: and they that bare him stood still. And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Young man, I say unto thee, Arise.</font>
41:7:22: Then Jesus answering said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Go your way, and tell John what things ye have seen and heard; how that the blind see, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, to the poor the gospel is preached.</font>
41:7:23: <font color="#CC0000">And blessed is he, whosoever shall not be offended in me.</font>
41:7:24: And when the messengers of John were departed, he began to speak unto the people concerning John, <font color="#CC0000">What went ye out into the wilderness for to see? A reed shaken with the wind?</font>
41:7:25: <font color="#CC0000">But what went ye out for to see? A man clothed in soft raiment? Behold, they which are gorgeously apparelled, and live delicately, are in kings' courts.</font>
