41:7:26: <font color="#CC0000">But what went ye out for to see? A prophet? Yea, I say unto you, and much more than a prophet.</font>
41:7:27: <font color="#CC0000">This is he, of whom it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.</font>
41:7:28: <font color="#CC0000">For I say unto you, Among those that are born of women there is not a greater prophet than John the Baptist: but he that is least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.</font>
41:7:31: And the Lord said, <font color="#CC0000">Whereunto then shall I liken the men of this generation? and to what are they like?</font>
41:7:32: <font color="#CC0000">They are like unto children sitting in the marketplace, and calling one to another, and saying, We have piped unto you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned to you, and ye have not wept.</font>
41:7:33: <font color="#CC0000">For John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine; and ye say, He hath a devil.</font>
41:7:34: <font color="#CC0000">The Son of man is come eating and drinking; and ye say, Behold a gluttonous man, and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners!</font>
41:7:35: <font color="#CC0000">But wisdom is justified of all her children.</font>
41:7:40: And Jesus answering said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Simon, I have somewhat to say unto thee.</font> And he saith, Master, say on.
41:7:41: <font color="#CC0000">There was a certain creditor which had two debtors: the one owed five hundred pence, and the other fifty.</font>
41:7:42: <font color="#CC0000">And when they had nothing to pay, he frankly forgave them both. Tell me therefore, which of them will love him most?</font>
41:7:43: Simon answered and said, I suppose that he, to whom he forgave most. And he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou hast rightly judged.</font>
41:7:44: And he turned to the woman, and said unto Simon, <font color="#CC0000">Seest thou this woman? I entered into thine house, thou gavest me no water for my feet: but she hath washed my feet with tears, and wiped them with the hairs of her head.</font>
41:7:45: <font color="#CC0000">Thou gavest me no kiss: but this woman since the time I came in hath not ceased to kiss my feet.</font>
41:7:46: <font color="#CC0000">My head with oil thou didst not anoint: but this woman hath anointed my feet with ointment.</font>
41:7:47: <font color="#CC0000">Wherefore I say unto thee, Her sins, which are many, are forgiven; for she loved much: but to whom little is forgiven, the same loveth little.</font>
41:7:48: And he said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Thy sins are forgiven.</font>
41:7:50: And he said to the woman, <font color="#CC0000">Thy faith hath saved thee; go in peace.</font>
41:8:5: <font color="#CC0000">A sower went out to sow his seed: and as he sowed, some fell by the way side; and it was trodden down, and the fowls of the air devoured it.</font>
41:8:6: <font color="#CC0000">And some fell upon a rock; and as soon as it was sprung up, it withered away, because it lacked moisture.</font>
41:8:7: <font color="#CC0000">And some fell among thorns; and the thorns sprang up with it, and choked it.</font>
41:8:8: <font color="#CC0000">And other fell on good ground, and sprang up, and bare fruit an hundredfold.</font> And when he had said these things, he cried, <font color="#CC0000">He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.</font>
41:8:10: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Unto you it is given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of God: but to others in parables; that seeing they might not see, and hearing they might not understand.</font>
41:8:11: <font color="#CC0000">Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.</font>
41:8:12: <font color="#CC0000">Those by the way side are they that hear; then cometh the devil, and taketh away the word out of their hearts, lest they should believe and be saved.</font>
41:8:13: <font color="#CC0000">They on the rock are they, which, when they hear, receive the word with joy; and these have no root, which for a while believe, and in time of temptation fall away.</font>
41:8:14: <font color="#CC0000">And that which fell among thorns are they, which, when they have heard, go forth, and are choked with cares and riches and pleasures of this life, and bring no fruit to perfection.</font>
41:8:15: <font color="#CC0000">But that on the good ground are they, which in an honest and good heart, having heard the word, keep it, and bring forth fruit with patience.</font>
41:8:16: <font color="#CC0000">No man, when he hath lighted a candle, covereth it with a vessel, or putteth it under a bed; but setteth it on a candlestick, that they which enter in may see the light.</font>
41:8:17: <font color="#CC0000">For nothing is secret, that shall not be made manifest; neither any thing hid, that shall not be known and come abroad.</font>
41:8:18: <font color="#CC0000">Take heed therefore how ye hear: for whosoever hath, to him shall be given; and whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken even that which he seemeth to have.</font>
41:8:21: And he answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">My mother and my brethren are these which hear the word of God, and do it.</font>
41:8:22: Now it came to pass on a certain day, that he went into a ship with his disciples: and he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Let us go over unto the other side of the lake.</font> And they launched forth.
41:8:25: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Where is your faith?</font> And they being afraid wondered, saying one to another, What manner of man is this! for he commandeth even the winds and water, and they obey him.
41:8:30: And Jesus asked him, saying, <font color="#CC0000">What is thy name?</font> And he said, Legion: because many devils were entered into him.
41:8:39: <font color="#CC0000">Return to thine own house, and shew how great things God hath done unto thee.</font> And he went his way, and published throughout the whole city how great things Jesus had done unto him.
41:8:45: And Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">Who touched me?</font> When all denied, Peter and they that were with him said, Master, the multitude throng thee and press thee, and sayest thou, <font color="#CC0000">Who touched me?</font>
41:8:46: And Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">Somebody hath touched me: for I perceive that virtue is gone out of me.</font>
41:8:48: And he said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Daughter, be of good comfort: thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace.</font>
41:8:50: But when Jesus heard it, he answered him, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Fear not: believe only, and she shall be made whole.</font>
41:8:52: And all wept, and bewailed her: but he said, <font color="#CC0000">Weep not; she is not dead, but sleepeth.</font>
41:8:54: And he put them all out, and took her by the hand, and called, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Maid, arise.</font>
41:9:3: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Take nothing for your journey, neither staves, nor scrip, neither bread, neither money; neither have two coats apiece.</font>
41:9:4: <font color="#CC0000">And whatsoever house ye enter into, there abide, and thence depart.</font>
41:9:5: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever will not receive you, when ye go out of that city, shake off the very dust from your feet for a testimony against them.</font>
41:9:13: But he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Give ye them to eat.</font> And they said, We have no more but five loaves and two fishes; except we should go and buy meat for all this people.
41:9:14: For they were about five thousand men. And he said to his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">Make them sit down by fifties in a company.</font>
41:9:18: And it came to pass, as he was alone praying, his disciples were with him: and he asked them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Whom say the people that I am?</font>
41:9:20: He said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">But whom say ye that I am?</font> Peter answering said, The Christ of God.
41:9:22: Saying, <font color="#CC0000">The Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be slain, and be raised the third day.</font>
41:9:23: And he said to them all, <font color="#CC0000">If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross daily, and follow me.</font>
41:9:24: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever will save his life shall lose it: but whosoever will lose his life for my sake, the same shall save it.</font>
41:9:25: <font color="#CC0000">For what is a man advantaged, if he gain the whole world, and lose himself, or be cast away?</font>
41:9:26: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever shall be ashamed of me and of my words, of him shall the Son of man be ashamed, when he shall come in his own glory, and in his Father's, and of the holy angels.</font>
41:9:27: <font color="#CC0000">But I tell you of a truth, there be some standing here, which shall not taste of death, till they see the kingdom of God.</font>
41:9:41: And Jesus answering said, <font color="#CC0000">O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you, and suffer you? Bring thy son hither.</font>
41:9:44: <font color="#CC0000">Let these sayings sink down into your ears: for the Son of man shall be delivered into the hands of men.</font>
41:9:48: And said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever shall receive this child in my name receiveth me: and whosoever shall receive me receiveth him that sent me: for he that is least among you all, the same shall be great.</font>
41:9:50: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Forbid him not: for he that is not against us is for us.</font>
41:9:55: But he turned, and rebuked them, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Ye know not what manner of spirit ye are of.</font>
41:9:56: <font color="#CC0000">For the Son of man is not come to destroy men's lives, but to save them. And they went to another village.</font>
41:9:58: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay his head.</font>
41:9:59: And he said unto another, <font color="#CC0000">Follow me.</font> But he said, Lord, suffer me first to go and bury my father.
41:9:60: Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Let the dead bury their dead: but go thou and preach the kingdom of God.</font>
41:9:62: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">No man, having put his hand to the plough, and looking back, is fit for the kingdom of God.</font>
41:10:2: Therefore said he unto them, <font color="#CC0000">The harvest truly is great, but the labourers are few: pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he would send forth labourers into his harvest.</font>
41:10:3: <font color="#CC0000">Go your ways: behold, I send you forth as lambs among wolves.</font>
41:10:4: <font color="#CC0000">Carry neither purse, nor scrip, nor shoes: and salute no man by the way.</font>
41:10:5: <font color="#CC0000">And into whatsoever house ye enter, first say, Peace be to this house.</font>
41:10:6: <font color="#CC0000">And if the son of peace be there, your peace shall rest upon it: if not, it shall turn to you again.</font>
41:10:7: <font color="#CC0000">And in the same house remain, eating and drinking such things as they give: for the labourer is worthy of his hire. Go not from house to house.</font>
41:10:8: <font color="#CC0000">And into whatsoever city ye enter, and they receive you, eat such things as are set before you:</font>
41:10:9: <font color="#CC0000">And heal the sick that are therein, and say unto them, The kingdom of God is come nigh unto you.</font>
41:10:10: <font color="#CC0000">But into whatsoever city ye enter, and they receive you not, go your ways out into the streets of the same, and say,</font>
41:10:11: <font color="#CC0000">Even the very dust of your city, which cleaveth on us, we do wipe off against you: notwithstanding be ye sure of this, that the kingdom of God is come nigh unto you.</font>
41:10:12: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, that it shall be more tolerable in that day for Sodom, than for that city.</font>
41:10:13: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon, which have been done in you, they had a great while ago repented, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.</font>
41:10:14: <font color="#CC0000">But it shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the judgment, than for you.</font>
41:10:15: <font color="#CC0000">And thou, Capernaum, which art exalted to heaven, shalt be thrust down to hell.</font>
41:10:16: <font color="#CC0000">He that heareth you heareth me; and he that despiseth you despiseth me; and he that despiseth me despiseth him that sent me.</font>
41:10:18: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I beheld Satan as lightning fall from heaven.</font>
41:10:19: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, I give unto you power to tread on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy: and nothing shall by any means hurt you.</font>
41:10:20: <font color="#CC0000">Notwithstanding in this rejoice not, that the spirits are subject unto you; but rather rejoice, because your names are written in heaven.</font>
41:10:21: In that hour Jesus rejoiced in spirit, and said, <font color="#CC0000">I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes: even so, Father; for so it seemed good in thy sight.</font>
41:10:22: <font color="#CC0000">All things are delivered to me of my Father: and no man knoweth who the Son is, but the Father; and who the Father is, but the Son, and he to whom the Son will reveal him.</font>
41:10:23: And he turned him unto his disciples, and said privately, <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are the eyes which see the things that ye see:</font>
41:10:24: <font color="#CC0000">For I tell you, that many prophets and kings have desired to see those things which ye see, and have not seen them; and to hear those things which ye hear, and have not heard them.</font>
41:10:26: He said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">What is written in the law? how readest thou?</font>
41:10:28: And he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou hast answered right: this do, and thou shalt live.</font>
41:10:30: And Jesus answering said, <font color="#CC0000">A certain man went down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and fell among thieves, which stripped him of his raiment, and wounded him, and departed, leaving him half dead.</font>
41:10:31: <font color="#CC0000">And by chance there came down a certain priest that way: and when he saw him, he passed by on the other side.</font>
41:10:32: <font color="#CC0000">And likewise a Levite, when he was at the place, came and looked on him, and passed by on the other side.</font>
41:10:33: <font color="#CC0000">But a certain Samaritan, as he journeyed, came where he was: and when he saw him, he had compassion on him,</font>
41:10:34: <font color="#CC0000">And went to him, and bound up his wounds, pouring in oil and wine, and set him on his own beast, and brought him to an inn, and took care of him.</font>
41:10:35: <font color="#CC0000">And on the morrow when he departed, he took out two pence, and gave them to the host, and said unto him, Take care of him; and whatsoever thou spendest more, when I come again, I will repay thee.</font>
41:10:36: <font color="#CC0000">Which now of these three, thinkest thou, was neighbour unto him that fell among the thieves?</font>
41:10:37: And he said, He that shewed mercy on him. Then said Jesus unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Go, and do thou likewise.</font>
41:10:41: And Jesus answered and said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Martha, Martha, thou art careful and troubled about many things:</font>
41:10:42: <font color="#CC0000">But one thing is needful: and Mary hath chosen that good part, which shall not be taken away from her.</font>
41:11:2: And he said unto them, When ye pray, say, <font color="#CC0000">Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name. Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done, as in heaven, so in earth.</font>
41:11:3: <font color="#CC0000">Give us day by day our daily bread.</font>
41:11:4: <font color="#CC0000">And forgive us our sins; for we also forgive every one that is indebted to us. And lead us not into temptation; but deliver us from evil.</font>
41:11:5: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Which of you shall have a friend, and shall go unto him at midnight, and say unto him, Friend, lend me three loaves;</font>
41:11:6: <font color="#CC0000">For a friend of mine in his journey is come to me, and I have nothing to set before him?</font>
41:11:7: <font color="#CC0000">And he from within shall answer and say, Trouble me not: the door is now shut, and my children are with me in bed; I cannot rise and give thee.</font>
41:11:8: <font color="#CC0000">I say unto you, Though he will not rise and give him, because he is his friend, yet because of his importunity he will rise and give him as many as he needeth.</font>
41:11:9: <font color="#CC0000">And I say unto you, Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.</font>
41:11:10: <font color="#CC0000">For every one that asketh receiveth; and he that seeketh findeth; and to him that knocketh it shall be opened.</font>
41:11:11: <font color="#CC0000">If a son shall ask bread of any of you that is a father, will he give him a stone? or if he ask a fish, will he for a fish give him a serpent?</font>
41:11:12: <font color="#CC0000">Or if he shall ask an egg, will he offer him a scorpion?</font>
41:11:13: <font color="#CC0000">If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children: how much more shall your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to them that ask him?</font>
41:11:17: But he, knowing their thoughts, said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and a house divided against a house falleth.</font>
41:11:18: <font color="#CC0000">If Satan also be divided against himself, how shall his kingdom stand? because ye say that I cast out devils through Beelzebub.</font>
41:11:19: <font color="#CC0000">And if I by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your sons cast them out? therefore shall they be your judges.</font>
41:11:20: <font color="#CC0000">But if I with the finger of God cast out devils, no doubt the kingdom of God is come upon you.</font>
41:11:21: <font color="#CC0000">When a strong man armed keepeth his palace, his goods are in peace:</font>
41:11:22: <font color="#CC0000">But when a stronger than he shall come upon him, and overcome him, he taketh from him all his armour wherein he trusted, and divideth his spoils.</font>
41:11:23: <font color="#CC0000">He that is not with me is against me: and he that gathereth not with me scattereth.</font>
41:11:24: <font color="#CC0000">When the unclean spirit is gone out of a man, he walketh through dry places, seeking rest; and finding none, he saith, I will return unto my house whence I came out.</font>
41:11:25: <font color="#CC0000">And when he cometh, he findeth it swept and garnished.</font>
41:11:26: <font color="#CC0000">Then goeth he, and taketh to him seven other spirits more wicked than himself; and they enter in, and dwell there: and the last state of that man is worse than the first.</font>
41:11:28: But he said, <font color="#CC0000">Yea rather, blessed are they that hear the word of God, and keep it.</font>
41:11:29: And when the people were gathered thick together, he began to say, <font color="#CC0000">This is an evil generation: they seek a sign; and there shall no sign be given it, but the sign of Jonas the prophet.</font>
41:11:30: <font color="#CC0000">For as Jonas was a sign unto the Ninevites, so shall also the Son of man be to this generation.</font>
41:11:31: <font color="#CC0000">The queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with the men of this generation, and condemn them: for she came from the utmost parts of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and, behold, a greater than Solomon is here.</font>
41:11:32: <font color="#CC0000">The men of Nineve shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for they repented at the preaching of Jonas; and, behold, a greater than Jonas is here.</font>
41:11:33: <font color="#CC0000">No man, when he hath lighted a candle, putteth it in a secret place, neither under a bushel, but on a candlestick, that they which come in may see the light.</font>
41:11:34: <font color="#CC0000">The light of the body is the eye: therefore when thine eye is single, thy whole body also is full of light; but when thine eye is evil, thy body also is full of darkness.</font>
41:11:35: <font color="#CC0000">Take heed therefore that the light which is in thee be not darkness.</font>
41:11:36: <font color="#CC0000">If thy whole body therefore be full of light, having no part dark, the whole shall be full of light, as when the bright shining of a candle doth give thee light.</font>
41:11:39: And the Lord said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Now do ye Pharisees make clean the outside of the cup and the platter; but your inward part is full of ravening and wickedness.</font>
41:11:40: <font color="#CC0000">Ye fools, did not he that made that which is without make that which is within also?</font>
41:11:41: <font color="#CC0000">But rather give alms of such things as ye have; and, behold, all things are clean unto you.</font>
41:11:42: <font color="#CC0000">But woe unto you, Pharisees! for ye tithe mint and rue and all manner of herbs, and pass over judgment and the love of God: these ought ye to have done, and not to leave the other undone.</font>
41:11:43: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you, Pharisees! for ye love the uppermost seats in the synagogues, and greetings in the markets.</font>
41:11:44: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye are as graves which appear not, and the men that walk over them are not aware of them.</font>
41:11:46: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you also, ye lawyers! for ye lade men with burdens grievous to be borne, and ye yourselves touch not the burdens with one of your fingers.</font>
41:11:47: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you! for ye build the sepulchres of the prophets, and your fathers killed them.</font>
41:11:48: <font color="#CC0000">Truly ye bear witness that ye allow the deeds of your fathers: for they indeed killed them, and ye build their sepulchres.</font>
41:11:49: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore also said the wisdom of God, I will send them prophets and apostles, and some of them they shall slay and persecute:</font>
41:11:50: <font color="#CC0000">That the blood of all the prophets, which was shed from the foundation of the world, may be required of this generation;</font>
41:11:51: <font color="#CC0000">From the blood of Abel unto the blood of Zacharias, which perished between the altar and the temple: verily I say unto you, It shall be required of this generation.</font>
41:11:52: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you, lawyers! for ye have taken away the key of knowledge: ye entered not in yourselves, and them that were entering in ye hindered.</font>
41:12:1: In the mean time, when there were gathered together an innumerable multitude of people, insomuch that they trode one upon another, he began to say unto his disciples first of all, <font color="#CC0000">Beware ye of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.</font>
41:12:2: <font color="#CC0000">For there is nothing covered, that shall not be revealed; neither hid, that shall not be known.</font>
41:12:3: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore whatsoever ye have spoken in darkness shall be heard in the light; and that which ye have spoken in the ear in closets shall be proclaimed upon the housetops.</font>
41:12:4: <font color="#CC0000">And I say unto you my friends, Be not afraid of them that kill the body, and after that have no more that they can do.</font>
41:12:5: <font color="#CC0000">But I will forewarn you whom ye shall fear: Fear him, which after he hath killed hath power to cast into hell; yea, I say unto you, Fear him.</font>
41:12:6: <font color="#CC0000">Are not five sparrows sold for two farthings, and not one of them is forgotten before God?</font>
41:12:7: <font color="#CC0000">But even the very hairs of your head are all numbered. Fear not therefore: ye are of more value than many sparrows.</font>
41:12:8: <font color="#CC0000">Also I say unto you, Whosoever shall confess me before men, him shall the Son of man also confess before the angels of God:</font>
41:12:9: <font color="#CC0000">But he that denieth me before men shall be denied before the angels of God.</font>
41:12:10: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever shall speak a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him: but unto him that blasphemeth against the Holy Ghost it shall not be forgiven.</font>
41:12:11: <font color="#CC0000">And when they bring you unto the synagogues, and unto magistrates, and powers, take ye no thought how or what thing ye shall answer, or what ye shall say:</font>
41:12:12: <font color="#CC0000">For the Holy Ghost shall teach you in the same hour what ye ought to say.</font>
41:12:14: And he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Man, who made me a judge or a divider over you?</font>
41:12:15: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Take heed, and beware of covetousness: for a man's life consisteth not in the abundance of the things which he possesseth.</font>
41:12:16: And he spake a parable unto them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">The ground of a certain rich man brought forth plentifully:</font>
41:12:17: <font color="#CC0000">And he thought within himself, saying, What shall I do, because I have no room where to bestow my fruits?</font>
41:12:18: <font color="#CC0000">And he said, This will I do: I will pull down my barns, and build greater; and there will I bestow all my fruits and my goods.</font>
41:12:19: <font color="#CC0000">And I will say to my soul, Soul, thou hast much goods laid up for many years; take thine ease, eat, drink, and be merry.</font>
41:12:20: <font color="#CC0000">But God said unto him, Thou fool, this night thy soul shall be required of thee: then whose shall those things be, which thou hast provided?</font>
41:12:21: <font color="#CC0000">So is he that layeth up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God.</font>
41:12:22: And he said unto his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">Therefore I say unto you, Take no thought for your life, what ye shall eat; neither for the body, what ye shall put on.</font>
41:12:23: <font color="#CC0000">The life is more than meat, and the body is more than raiment.</font>
41:12:24: <font color="#CC0000">Consider the ravens: for they neither sow nor reap; which neither have storehouse nor barn; and God feedeth them: how much more are ye better than the fowls?</font>
41:12:25: <font color="#CC0000">And which of you with taking thought can add to his stature one cubit?</font>
41:12:26: <font color="#CC0000">If ye then be not able to do that thing which is least, why take ye thought for the rest?</font>
41:12:27: <font color="#CC0000">Consider the lilies how they grow: they toil not, they spin not; and yet I say unto you, that Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.</font>
41:12:28: <font color="#CC0000">If then God so clothe the grass, which is to day in the field, and to morrow is cast into the oven; how much more will he clothe you, O ye of little faith?</font>
41:12:29: <font color="#CC0000">And seek not ye what ye shall eat, or what ye shall drink, neither be ye of doubtful mind.</font>
41:12:30: <font color="#CC0000">For all these things do the nations of the world seek after: and your Father knoweth that ye have need of these things.</font>
41:12:31: <font color="#CC0000">But rather seek ye the kingdom of God; and all these things shall be added unto you.</font>
41:12:32: <font color="#CC0000">Fear not, little flock; for it is your Father's good pleasure to give you the kingdom.</font>
41:12:33: <font color="#CC0000">Sell that ye have, and give alms; provide yourselves bags which wax not old, a treasure in the heavens that faileth not, where no thief approacheth, neither moth corrupteth.</font>
41:12:34: <font color="#CC0000">For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.</font>
41:12:35: <font color="#CC0000">Let your loins be girded about, and your lights burning;</font>
41:12:36: <font color="#CC0000">And ye yourselves like unto men that wait for their lord, when he will return from the wedding; that when he cometh and knocketh, they may open unto him immediately.</font>
41:12:37: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are those servants, whom the lord when he cometh shall find watching: verily I say unto you, that he shall gird himself, and make them to sit down to meat, and will come forth and serve them.</font>
41:12:38: <font color="#CC0000">And if he shall come in the second watch, or come in the third watch, and find them so, blessed are those servants.</font>
41:12:39: <font color="#CC0000">And this know, that if the goodman of the house had known what hour the thief would come, he would have watched, and not have suffered his house to be broken through.</font>
41:12:40: <font color="#CC0000">Be ye therefore ready also: for the Son of man cometh at an hour when ye think not.</font>
41:12:42: And the Lord said, <font color="#CC0000">Who then is that faithful and wise steward, whom his lord shall make ruler over his household, to give them their portion of meat in due season?</font>
41:12:43: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed is that servant, whom his lord when he cometh shall find so doing.</font>
41:12:44: <font color="#CC0000">Of a truth I say unto you, that he will make him ruler over all that he hath.</font>
41:12:45: <font color="#CC0000">But and if that servant say in his heart, My lord delayeth his coming; and shall begin to beat the menservants and maidens, and to eat and drink, and to be drunken;</font>
41:12:46: <font color="#CC0000">The lord of that servant will come in a day when he looketh not for him, and at an hour when he is not aware, and will cut him in sunder, and will appoint him his portion with the unbelievers.</font>
41:12:47: <font color="#CC0000">And that servant, which knew his lord's will, and prepared not himself, neither did according to his will, shall be beaten with many stripes.</font>
41:12:48: <font color="#CC0000">But he that knew not, and did commit things worthy of stripes, shall be beaten with few stripes. For unto whomsoever much is given, of him shall be much required: and to whom men have committed much, of him they will ask the more.</font>
41:12:49: <font color="#CC0000">I am come to send fire on the earth; and what will I, if it be already kindled?</font>
41:12:50: <font color="#CC0000">But I have a baptism to be baptized with; and how am I straitened till it be accomplished!</font>
41:12:51: <font color="#CC0000">Suppose ye that I am come to give peace on earth? I tell you, Nay; but rather division:</font>
41:12:52: <font color="#CC0000">For from henceforth there shall be five in one house divided, three against two, and two against three.</font>
41:12:53: <font color="#CC0000">The father shall be divided against the son, and the son against the father; the mother against the daughter, and the daughter against the mother; the mother in law against her daughter in law, and the daughter in law against her mother in law.</font>
41:12:54: And he said also to the people, <font color="#CC0000">When ye see a cloud rise out of the west, straightway ye say, There cometh a shower; and so it is.</font>
41:12:55: <font color="#CC0000">And when ye see the south wind blow, ye say, There will be heat; and it cometh to pass.</font>
41:12:56: <font color="#CC0000">Ye hypocrites, ye can discern the face of the sky and of the earth; but how is it that ye do not discern this time?</font>
41:12:57: <font color="#CC0000">Yea, and why even of yourselves judge ye not what is right?</font>
41:12:58: <font color="#CC0000">When thou goest with thine adversary to the magistrate, as thou art in the way, give diligence that thou mayest be delivered from him; lest he hale thee to the judge, and the judge deliver thee to the officer, and the officer cast thee into prison.</font>
41:12:59: <font color="#CC0000">I tell thee, thou shalt not depart thence, till thou hast paid the very last mite.</font>
41:13:2: And Jesus answering said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Suppose ye that these Galilæans were sinners above all the Galilæans, because they suffered such things?</font>
41:13:3: <font color="#CC0000">I tell you, Nay: but, except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish.</font>
41:13:4: <font color="#CC0000">Or those eighteen, upon whom the tower in Siloam fell, and slew them, think ye that they were sinners above all men that dwelt in Jerusalem?</font>
41:13:5: <font color="#CC0000">I tell you, Nay: but, except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish.</font>
41:13:6: He spake also this parable; <font color="#CC0000">A certain man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard; and he came and sought fruit thereon, and found none.</font>
41:13:7: <font color="#CC0000">Then said he unto the dresser of his vineyard, Behold, these three years I come seeking fruit on this fig tree, and find none: cut it down; why cumbereth it the ground?</font>
41:13:8: <font color="#CC0000">And he answering said unto him, Lord, let it alone this year also, till I shall dig about it, and dung it:</font>
41:13:9: <font color="#CC0000">And if it bear fruit, well: and if not, then after that thou shalt cut it down.</font>
41:13:12: And when Jesus saw her, he called her to him, and said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Woman, thou art loosed from thine infirmity.</font>
41:13:15: The Lord then answered him, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Thou hypocrite, doth not each one of you on the sabbath loose his ox or his ass from the stall, and lead him away to watering?</font>
41:13:16: <font color="#CC0000">And ought not this woman, being a daughter of Abraham, whom Satan hath bound, lo, these eighteen years, be loosed from this bond on the sabbath day?</font>
41:13:18: Then said he, <font color="#CC0000">Unto what is the kingdom of God like? and whereunto shall I resemble it?</font>
41:13:19: <font color="#CC0000">It is like a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and cast into his garden; and it grew, and waxed a great tree; and the fowls of the air lodged in the branches of it.</font>
41:13:20: And again he said, <font color="#CC0000">Whereunto shall I liken the kingdom of God?</font>
41:13:21: <font color="#CC0000">It is like leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal, till the whole was leavened.</font>
41:13:24: <font color="#CC0000">Strive to enter in at the strait gate: for many, I say unto you, will seek to enter in, and shall not be able.</font>
41:13:25: <font color="#CC0000">When once the master of the house is risen up, and hath shut to the door, and ye begin to stand without, and to knock at the door, saying, Lord, Lord, open unto us; and he shall answer and say unto you, I know you not whence ye are:</font>
41:13:26: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall ye begin to say, We have eaten and drunk in thy presence, and thou hast taught in our streets.</font>
41:13:27: <font color="#CC0000">But he shall say, I tell you, I know you not whence ye are; depart from me, all ye workers of iniquity.</font>
41:13:28: <font color="#CC0000">There shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth, when ye shall see Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, and all the prophets, in the kingdom of God, and you yourselves thrust out.</font>
41:13:29: <font color="#CC0000">And they shall come from the east, and from the west, and from the north, and from the south, and shall sit down in the kingdom of God.</font>
41:13:30: <font color="#CC0000">And, behold, there are last which shall be first, and there are first which shall be last.</font>
41:13:32: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Go ye, and tell that fox, Behold, I cast out devils, and I do cures to day and to morrow, and the third day I shall be perfected.</font>
41:13:33: <font color="#CC0000">Nevertheless I must walk to day, and to morrow, and the day following: for it cannot be that a prophet perish out of Jerusalem.</font>
41:13:34: <font color="#CC0000">O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, which killest the prophets, and stonest them that are sent unto thee; how often would I have gathered thy children together, as a hen doth gather her brood under her wings, and ye would not!</font>
41:13:35: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, your house is left unto you desolate: and verily I say unto you, Ye shall not see me, until the time come when ye shall say, Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.</font>
41:14:3: And Jesus answering spake unto the lawyers and Pharisees, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath day?</font>
41:14:5: And answered them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Which of you shall have an ass or an ox fallen into a pit, and will not straightway pull him out on the sabbath day?</font>
41:14:8: <font color="#CC0000">When thou art bidden of any man to a wedding, sit not down in the highest room; lest a more honourable man than thou be bidden of him;</font>
41:14:9: <font color="#CC0000">And he that bade thee and him come and say to thee, Give this man place; and thou begin with shame to take the lowest room.</font>
41:14:10: <font color="#CC0000">But when thou art bidden, go and sit down in the lowest room; that when he that bade thee cometh, he may say unto thee, Friend, go up higher: then shalt thou have worship in the presence of them that sit at meat with thee.</font>
41:14:11: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever exalteth himself shall be abased; and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.</font>
41:14:12: Then said he also to him that bade him, <font color="#CC0000">When thou makest a dinner or a supper, call not thy friends, nor thy brethren, neither thy kinsmen, nor thy rich neighbours; lest they also bid thee again, and a recompence be made thee.</font>
41:14:13: <font color="#CC0000">But when thou makest a feast, call the poor, the maimed, the lame, the blind:</font>
41:14:14: <font color="#CC0000">And thou shalt be blessed; for they cannot recompense thee: for thou shalt be recompensed at the resurrection of the just.</font>
41:14:16: Then said he unto him, <font color="#CC0000">A certain man made a great supper, and bade many:</font>
41:14:17: <font color="#CC0000">And sent his servant at supper time to say to them that were bidden, Come; for all things are now ready.</font>
41:14:18: <font color="#CC0000">And they all with one consent began to make excuse. The first said unto him, I have bought a piece of ground, and I must needs go and see it: I pray thee have me excused.</font>
41:14:19: <font color="#CC0000">And another said, I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I go to prove them: I pray thee have me excused.</font>
41:14:20: <font color="#CC0000">And another said, I have married a wife, and therefore I cannot come.</font>
41:14:21: <font color="#CC0000">So that servant came, and shewed his lord these things. Then the master of the house being angry said to his servant, Go out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city, and bring in hither the poor, and the maimed, and the halt, and the blind.</font>
41:14:22: <font color="#CC0000">And the servant said, Lord, it is done as thou hast commanded, and yet there is room.</font>
41:14:23: <font color="#CC0000">And the lord said unto the servant, Go out into the highways and hedges, and compel them to come in, that my house may be filled.</font>
41:14:24: <font color="#CC0000">For I say unto you, That none of those men which were bidden shall taste of my supper.</font>
41:14:26: <font color="#CC0000">If any man come to me, and hate not his father, and mother, and wife, and children, and brethren, and sisters, yea, and his own life also, he cannot be my disciple.</font>
41:14:27: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever doth not bear his cross, and come after me, cannot be my disciple.</font>
41:14:28: <font color="#CC0000">For which of you, intending to build a tower, sitteth not down first, and counteth the cost, whether he have sufficient to finish it?</font>
41:14:29: <font color="#CC0000">Lest haply, after he hath laid the foundation, and is not able to finish it, all that behold it begin to mock him,</font>
41:14:30: <font color="#CC0000">Saying, This man began to build, and was not able to finish.</font>
41:14:31: <font color="#CC0000">Or what king, going to make war against another king, sitteth not down first, and consulteth whether he be able with ten thousand to meet him that cometh against him with twenty thousand?</font>
41:14:32: <font color="#CC0000">Or else, while the other is yet a great way off, he sendeth an ambassage, and desireth conditions of peace.</font>
41:14:33: <font color="#CC0000">So likewise, whosoever he be of you that forsaketh not all that he hath, he cannot be my disciple.</font>
41:14:34: <font color="#CC0000">Salt is good: but if the salt have lost his savour, wherewith shall it be seasoned?</font>
41:14:35: <font color="#CC0000">It is neither fit for the land, nor yet for the dunghill; but men cast it out. He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.</font>
41:15:4: <font color="#CC0000">What man of you, having an hundred sheep, if he lose one of them, doth not leave the ninety and nine in the wilderness, and go after that which is lost, until he find it?</font>
41:15:5: <font color="#CC0000">And when he hath found it, he layeth it on his shoulders, rejoicing.</font>
41:15:6: <font color="#CC0000">And when he cometh home, he calleth together his friends and neighbours, saying unto them, Rejoice with me; for I have found my sheep which was lost.</font>
41:15:7: <font color="#CC0000">I say unto you, that likewise joy shall be in heaven over one sinner that repenteth, more than over ninety and nine just persons, which need no repentance.</font>
41:15:8: <font color="#CC0000">Either what woman having ten pieces of silver, if she lose one piece, doth not light a candle, and sweep the house, and seek diligently till she find it?</font>
41:15:9: <font color="#CC0000">And when she hath found it, she calleth her friends and her neighbours together, saying, Rejoice with me; for I have found the piece which I had lost.</font>
41:15:10: <font color="#CC0000">Likewise, I say unto you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner that repenteth.</font>
41:15:11: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">A certain man had two sons:</font>
41:15:12: <font color="#CC0000">And the younger of them said to his father, Father, give me the portion of goods that falleth to me. And he divided unto them his living.</font>
41:15:13: <font color="#CC0000">And not many days after the younger son gathered all together, and took his journey into a far country, and there wasted his substance with riotous living.</font>
41:15:14: <font color="#CC0000">And when he had spent all, there arose a mighty famine in that land; and he began to be in want.</font>
41:15:15: <font color="#CC0000">And he went and joined himself to a citizen of that country; and he sent him into his fields to feed swine.</font>
41:15:16: <font color="#CC0000">And he would fain have filled his belly with the husks that the swine did eat: and no man gave unto him.</font>
41:15:17: <font color="#CC0000">And when he came to himself, he said, How many hired servants of my father's have bread enough and to spare, and I perish with hunger!</font>
41:15:18: <font color="#CC0000">I will arise and go to my father, and will say unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and before thee,</font>
41:15:19: <font color="#CC0000">And am no more worthy to be called thy son: make me as one of thy hired servants.</font>
41:15:20: <font color="#CC0000">And he arose, and came to his father. But when he was yet a great way off, his father saw him, and had compassion, and ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him.</font>
41:15:21: <font color="#CC0000">And the son said unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in thy sight, and am no more worthy to be called thy son.</font>
41:15:22: <font color="#CC0000">But the father said to his servants, Bring forth the best robe, and put it on him; and put a ring on his hand, and shoes on his feet:</font>
41:15:23: <font color="#CC0000">And bring hither the fatted calf, and kill it; and let us eat, and be merry:</font>
41:15:24: <font color="#CC0000">For this my son was dead, and is alive again; he was lost, and is found. And they began to be merry.</font>
41:15:25: <font color="#CC0000">Now his elder son was in the field: and as he came and drew nigh to the house, he heard musick and dancing.</font>
41:15:26: <font color="#CC0000">And he called one of the servants, and asked what these things meant.</font>
41:15:27: <font color="#CC0000">And he said unto him, Thy brother is come; and thy father hath killed the fatted calf, because he hath received him safe and sound.</font>
41:15:28: <font color="#CC0000">And he was angry, and would not go in: therefore came his father out, and intreated him.</font>
41:15:29: <font color="#CC0000">And he answering said to his father, Lo, these many years do I serve thee, neither transgressed I at any time thy commandment: and yet thou never gavest me a kid, that I might make merry with my friends:</font>
41:15:30: <font color="#CC0000">But as soon as this thy son was come, which hath devoured thy living with harlots, thou hast killed for him the fatted calf.</font>
41:15:31: <font color="#CC0000">And he said unto him, Son, thou art ever with me, and all that I have is thine.</font>
41:15:32: <font color="#CC0000">It was meet that we should make merry, and be glad: for this thy brother was dead, and is alive again; and was lost, and is found.</font>
41:16:1: And he said also unto his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">There was a certain rich man, which had a steward; and the same was accused unto him that he had wasted his goods.</font>
41:16:2: <font color="#CC0000">And he called him, and said unto him, How is it that I hear this of thee? give an account of thy stewardship; for thou mayest be no longer steward.</font>
41:16:3: <font color="#CC0000">Then the steward said within himself, What shall I do? for my lord taketh away from me the stewardship: I cannot dig; to beg I am ashamed.</font>
41:16:4: <font color="#CC0000">I am resolved what to do, that, when I am put out of the stewardship, they may receive me into their houses.</font>
41:16:5: <font color="#CC0000">So he called every one of his lord's debtors unto him, and said unto the first, How much owest thou unto my lord?</font>
41:16:6: <font color="#CC0000">And he said, An hundred measures of oil. And he said unto him, Take thy bill, and sit down quickly, and write fifty.</font>
41:16:7: <font color="#CC0000">Then said he to another, And how much owest thou? And he said, An hundred measures of wheat. And he said unto him, Take thy bill, and write fourscore.</font>
41:16:8: <font color="#CC0000">And the lord commended the unjust steward, because he had done wisely: for the children of this world are in their generation wiser than the children of light.</font>
41:16:9: <font color="#CC0000">And I say unto you, Make to yourselves friends of the mammon of unrighteousness; that, when ye fail, they may receive you into everlasting habitations.</font>
41:16:10: <font color="#CC0000">He that is faithful in that which is least is faithful also in much: and he that is unjust in the least is unjust also in much.</font>
41:16:11: <font color="#CC0000">If therefore ye have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon, who will commit to your trust the true riches?</font>
41:16:12: <font color="#CC0000">And if ye have not been faithful in that which is another man's, who shall give you that which is your own?</font>
41:16:13: <font color="#CC0000">No servant can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and mammon.</font>
41:16:15: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Ye are they which justify yourselves before men; but God knoweth your hearts: for that which is highly esteemed among men is abomination in the sight of God.</font>
41:16:16: <font color="#CC0000">The law and the prophets were until John: since that time the kingdom of God is preached, and every man presseth into it.</font>
41:16:17: <font color="#CC0000">And it is easier for heaven and earth to pass, than one tittle of the law to fail.</font>
41:16:18: <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever putteth away his wife, and marrieth another, committeth adultery: and whosoever marrieth her that is put away from her husband committeth adultery.</font>
41:16:19: <font color="#CC0000">There was a certain rich man, which was clothed in purple and fine linen, and fared sumptuously every day:</font>
41:16:20: <font color="#CC0000">And there was a certain beggar named Lazarus, which was laid at his gate, full of sores,</font>
41:16:21: <font color="#CC0000">And desiring to be fed with the crumbs which fell from the rich man's table: moreover the dogs came and licked his sores.</font>
41:16:22: <font color="#CC0000">And it came to pass, that the beggar died, and was carried by the angels into Abraham's bosom: the rich man also died, and was buried;</font>
41:16:23: <font color="#CC0000">And in hell he lift up his eyes, being in torments, and seeth Abraham afar off, and Lazarus in his bosom.</font>
41:16:24: <font color="#CC0000">And he cried and said, Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water, and cool my tongue; for I am tormented in this flame.</font>
41:16:25: <font color="#CC0000">But Abraham said, Son, remember that thou in thy lifetime receivedst thy good things, and likewise Lazarus evil things: but now he is comforted, and thou art tormented.</font>
41:16:26: <font color="#CC0000">And beside all this, between us and you there is a great gulf fixed: so that they which would pass from hence to you cannot; neither can they pass to us, that would come from thence.</font>
41:16:27: <font color="#CC0000">Then he said, I pray thee therefore, father, that thou wouldest send him to my father's house:</font>
41:16:28: <font color="#CC0000">For I have five brethren; that he may testify unto them, lest they also come into this place of torment.</font>
41:16:29: <font color="#CC0000">Abraham saith unto him, They have Moses and the prophets; let them hear them.</font>
41:16:30: <font color="#CC0000">And he said, Nay, father Abraham: but if one went unto them from the dead, they will repent.</font>
41:16:31: <font color="#CC0000">And he said unto him, If they hear not Moses and the prophets, neither will they be persuaded, though one rose from the dead.</font>
41:17:1: Then said he unto the disciples, <font color="#CC0000">It is impossible but that offences will come: but woe unto him, through whom they come!</font>
41:17:2: <font color="#CC0000">It were better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and he cast into the sea, than that he should offend one of these little ones.</font>
41:17:3: <font color="#CC0000">Take heed to yourselves: If thy brother trespass against thee, rebuke him; and if he repent, forgive him.</font>
41:17:4: <font color="#CC0000">And if he trespass against thee seven times in a day, and seven times in a day turn again to thee, saying, I repent; thou shalt forgive him.</font>
41:17:6: And the Lord said, <font color="#CC0000">If ye had faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye might say unto this sycamine tree, Be thou plucked up by the root, and be thou planted in the sea; and it should obey you.</font>
41:17:7: <font color="#CC0000">But which of you, having a servant plowing or feeding cattle, will say unto him by and by, when he is come from the field, Go and sit down to meat?</font>
41:17:8: <font color="#CC0000">And will not rather say unto him, Make ready wherewith I may sup, and gird thyself, and serve me, till I have eaten and drunken; and afterward thou shalt eat and drink?</font>
41:17:9: <font color="#CC0000">Doth he thank that servant because he did the things that were commanded him? I trow not.</font>
41:17:10: <font color="#CC0000">So likewise ye, when ye shall have done all those things which are commanded you, say, We are unprofitable servants: we have done that which was our duty to do.</font>
41:17:14: And when he saw them, he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Go shew yourselves unto the priests.</font> And it came to pass, that, as they went, they were cleansed.
41:17:17: And Jesus answering said, <font color="#CC0000">Were there not ten cleansed? but where are the nine?</font>
41:17:18: <font color="#CC0000">There are not found that returned to give glory to God, save this stranger.</font>
41:17:19: And he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Arise, go thy way: thy faith hath made thee whole.</font>
41:17:20: And when he was demanded of the Pharisees, when the kingdom of God should come, he answered them and said, <font color="#CC0000">The kingdom of God cometh not with observation:</font>
41:17:21: <font color="#CC0000">Neither shall they say, Lo here! or, lo there! for, behold, the kingdom of God is within you.</font>
41:17:22: And he said unto the disciples, <font color="#CC0000">The days will come, when ye shall desire to see one of the days of the Son of man, and ye shall not see it.</font>
41:17:23: <font color="#CC0000">And they shall say to you, See here; or, see there: go not after them, nor follow them.</font>
41:17:24: <font color="#CC0000">For as the lightning, that lighteneth out of the one part under heaven, shineth unto the other part under heaven; so shall also the Son of man be in his day.</font>
41:17:25: <font color="#CC0000">But first must he suffer many things, and be rejected of this generation.</font>
41:17:26: <font color="#CC0000">And as it was in the days of Noe, so shall it be also in the days of the Son of man.</font>
41:17:27: <font color="#CC0000">They did eat, they drank, they married wives, they were given in marriage, until the day that Noe entered into the ark, and the flood came, and destroyed them all.</font>
41:17:28: <font color="#CC0000">Likewise also as it was in the days of Lot; they did eat, they drank, they bought, they sold, they planted, they builded;</font>
41:17:29: <font color="#CC0000">But the same day that Lot went out of Sodom it rained fire and brimstone from heaven, and destroyed them all.</font>
41:17:30: <font color="#CC0000">Even thus shall it be in the day when the Son of man is revealed.</font>
41:17:31: <font color="#CC0000">In that day, he which shall be upon the housetop, and his stuff in the house, let him not come down to take it away: and he that is in the field, let him likewise not return back.</font>
41:17:33: <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever shall seek to save his life shall lose it; and whosoever shall lose his life shall preserve it.</font>
41:17:34: <font color="#CC0000">I tell you, in that night there shall be two men in one bed; the one shall be taken, and the other shall be left.</font>
41:17:35: <font color="#CC0000">Two women shall be grinding together; the one shall be taken, and the other left.</font>
41:17:36: <font color="#CC0000">Two men shall be in the field; the one shall be taken, and the other left.</font>
41:17:37: And they answered and said unto him, Where, Lord? And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Wheresoever the body is, thither will the eagles be gathered together.</font>
41:18:2: Saying, <font color="#CC0000">There was in a city a judge, which feared not God, neither regarded man:</font>
41:18:3: <font color="#CC0000">And there was a widow in that city; and she came unto him, saying, Avenge me of mine adversary.</font>
41:18:4: <font color="#CC0000">And he would not for a while: but afterward he said within himself, Though I fear not God, nor regard man;</font>
41:18:5: <font color="#CC0000">Yet because this widow troubleth me, I will avenge her, lest by her continual coming she weary me.</font>
41:18:6: And the Lord said, <font color="#CC0000">Hear what the unjust judge saith.</font>
41:18:7: <font color="#CC0000">And shall not God avenge his own elect, which cry day and night unto him, though he bear long with them?</font>
41:18:8: <font color="#CC0000">I tell you that he will avenge them speedily. Nevertheless when the Son of man cometh, shall he find faith on the earth?</font>
41:18:10: <font color="#CC0000">Two men went up into the temple to pray; the one a Pharisee, and the other a publican.</font>
41:18:11: <font color="#CC0000">The Pharisee stood and prayed thus with himself, God, I thank thee, that I am not as other men are, extortioners, unjust, adulterers, or even as this publican.</font>
41:18:12: <font color="#CC0000">I fast twice in the week, I give tithes of all that I possess.</font>
41:18:13: <font color="#CC0000">And the publican, standing afar off, would not lift up so much as his eyes unto heaven, but smote upon his breast, saying, God be merciful to me a sinner.</font>
41:18:14: <font color="#CC0000">I tell you, this man went down to his house justified rather than the other: for every one that exalteth himself shall be abased; and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.</font>
41:18:16: But Jesus called them unto him, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Suffer little children to come unto me, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdom of God.</font>
41:18:17: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child shall in no wise enter therein.</font>
41:18:19: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Why callest thou me good? none is good, save one, that is, God.</font>
41:18:20: <font color="#CC0000">Thou knowest the commandments, Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Honour thy father and thy mother.</font>
41:18:22: Now when Jesus heard these things, he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Yet lackest thou one thing: sell all that thou hast, and distribute unto the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, follow me.</font>
41:18:24: And when Jesus saw that he was very sorrowful, he said, <font color="#CC0000">How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God!</font>
41:18:25: <font color="#CC0000">For it is easier for a camel to go through a needle's eye, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.</font>
41:18:27: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">The things which are impossible with men are possible with God.</font>
41:18:29: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or parents, or brethren, or wife, or children, for the kingdom of God's sake,</font>
41:18:30: <font color="#CC0000">Who shall not receive manifold more in this present time, and in the world to come life everlasting.</font>
41:18:31: Then he took unto him the twelve, and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Behold, we go up to Jerusalem, and all things that are written by the prophets concerning the Son of man shall be accomplished.</font>
41:18:32: <font color="#CC0000">For he shall be delivered unto the Gentiles, and shall be mocked, and spitefully entreated, and spitted on:</font>
41:18:33: <font color="#CC0000">And they shall scourge him, and put him to death: and the third day he shall rise again.</font>
41:18:41: Saying, <font color="#CC0000">What wilt thou that I shall do unto thee?</font> And he said, Lord, that I may receive my sight.
41:18:42: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Receive thy sight: thy faith hath saved thee.</font>
41:19:5: And when Jesus came to the place, he looked up, and saw him, and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Zacchæus, make haste, and come down; for to day I must abide at thy house.</font>
41:19:9: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">This day is salvation come to this house, forsomuch as he also is a son of Abraham.</font>
41:19:10: <font color="#CC0000">For the Son of man is come to seek and to save that which was lost.</font>
41:19:12: He said therefore, <font color="#CC0000">A certain nobleman went into a far country to receive for himself a kingdom, and to return.</font>
41:19:13: <font color="#CC0000">And he called his ten servants, and delivered them ten pounds, and said unto them, Occupy till I come.</font>
41:19:14: <font color="#CC0000">But his citizens hated him, and sent a message after him, saying, We will not have this man to reign over us.</font>
41:19:15: <font color="#CC0000">And it came to pass, that when he was returned, having received the kingdom, then he commanded these servants to be called unto him, to whom he had given the money, that he might know how much every man had gained by trading.</font>
41:19:16: <font color="#CC0000">Then came the first, saying, Lord, thy pound hath gained ten pounds.</font>
41:19:17: <font color="#CC0000">And he said unto him, Well, thou good servant: because thou hast been faithful in a very little, have thou authority over ten cities.</font>
41:19:18: <font color="#CC0000">And the second came, saying, Lord, thy pound hath gained five pounds.</font>
41:19:19: <font color="#CC0000">And he said likewise to him, Be thou also over five cities.</font>
41:19:20: <font color="#CC0000">And another came, saying, Lord, behold, here is thy pound, which I have kept laid up in a napkin:</font>
41:19:21: <font color="#CC0000">For I feared thee, because thou art an austere man: thou takest up that thou layedst not down, and reapest that thou didst not sow.</font>
41:19:22: <font color="#CC0000">And he saith unto him, Out of thine own mouth will I judge thee, thou wicked servant. Thou knewest that I was an austere man, taking up that I laid not down, and reaping that I did not sow:</font>
41:19:23: <font color="#CC0000">Wherefore then gavest not thou my money into the bank, that at my coming I might have required mine own with usury?</font>
41:19:24: <font color="#CC0000">And he said unto them that stood by, Take from him the pound, and give it to him that hath ten pounds.</font>
41:19:25: <font color="#CC0000">(And they said unto him, Lord, he hath ten pounds.)</font>
41:19:26: <font color="#CC0000">For I say unto you, That unto every one which hath shall be given; and from him that hath not, even that he hath shall be taken away from him.</font>
41:19:27: <font color="#CC0000">But those mine enemies, which would not that I should reign over them, bring hither, and slay them before me.</font>
41:19:30: Saying, <font color="#CC0000">Go ye into the village over against you; in the which at your entering ye shall find a colt tied, whereon yet never man sat: loose him, and bring him hither.</font>
41:19:31: <font color="#CC0000">And if any man ask you, Why do ye loose him? thus shall ye say unto him, Because the Lord hath need of him.</font>
41:19:40: And he answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I tell you that, if these should hold their peace, the stones would immediately cry out.</font>
41:19:42: Saying, <font color="#CC0000">If thou hadst known, even thou, at least in this thy day, the things which belong unto thy peace! but now they are hid from thine eyes.</font>
41:19:43: <font color="#CC0000">For the days shall come upon thee, that thine enemies shall cast a trench about thee, and compass thee round, and keep thee in on every side,</font>
41:19:44: <font color="#CC0000">And shall lay thee even with the ground, and thy children within thee; and they shall not leave in thee one stone upon another; because thou knewest not the time of thy visitation.</font>
41:19:46: Saying unto them, <font color="#CC0000">It is written, My house is the house of prayer: but ye have made it a den of thieves.</font>
41:20:3: And he answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I will also ask you one thing; and answer me:</font>
41:20:4: <font color="#CC0000">The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men?</font>
41:20:8: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.</font>
41:20:9: Then began he to speak to the people this parable; <font color="#CC0000">A certain man planted a vineyard, and let it forth to husbandmen, and went into a far country for a long time.</font>
41:20:10: <font color="#CC0000">And at the season he sent a servant to the husbandmen, that they should give him of the fruit of the vineyard: but the husbandmen beat him, and sent him away empty.</font>
41:20:11: <font color="#CC0000">And again he sent another servant: and they beat him also, and entreated him shamefully, and sent him away empty.</font>
41:20:12: <font color="#CC0000">And again he sent a third: and they wounded him also, and cast him out.</font>
41:20:13: <font color="#CC0000">Then said the lord of the vineyard, What shall I do? I will send my beloved son: it may be they will reverence him when they see him.</font>
41:20:14: <font color="#CC0000">But when the husbandmen saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, This is the heir: come, let us kill him, that the inheritance may be ours.</font>
41:20:15: <font color="#CC0000">So they cast him out of the vineyard, and killed him. What therefore shall the lord of the vineyard do unto them?</font>
41:20:16: <font color="#CC0000">He shall come and destroy these husbandmen, and shall give the vineyard to others.</font> And when they heard it, they said, God forbid.
41:20:17: And he beheld them, and said, <font color="#CC0000">What is this then that is written, The stone which the builders rejected, the same is become the head of the corner?</font>
41:20:18: <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever shall fall upon that stone shall be broken; but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder.</font>
41:20:23: But he perceived their craftiness, and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why tempt ye me?</font>
41:20:24: <font color="#CC0000">Shew me a penny. Whose image and superscription hath it?</font> They answered and said, Cæsar's.
41:20:25: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Render therefore unto Cæsar the things which be Cæsar's, and unto God the things which be God's.</font>
41:20:34: And Jesus answering said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">The children of this world marry, and are given in marriage:</font>
41:20:35: <font color="#CC0000">But they which shall be accounted worthy to obtain that world, and the resurrection from the dead, neither marry, nor are given in marriage:</font>
41:20:36: <font color="#CC0000">Neither can they die any more: for they are equal unto the angels; and are the children of God, being the children of the resurrection.</font>
41:20:37: <font color="#CC0000">Now that the dead are raised, even Moses shewed at the bush, when he calleth the Lord the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.</font>
41:20:38: <font color="#CC0000">For he is not a God of the dead, but of the living: for all live unto him.</font>
41:20:41: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">How say they that Christ is David's son?</font>
41:20:42: <font color="#CC0000">And David himself saith in the book of Psalms, The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand,</font>
41:20:43: <font color="#CC0000">Till I make thine enemies thy footstool.</font>
41:20:44: <font color="#CC0000">David therefore calleth him Lord, how is he then his son?</font>
41:20:46: <font color="#CC0000">Beware of the scribes, which desire to walk in long robes, and love greetings in the markets, and the highest seats in the synagogues, and the chief rooms at feasts;</font>
41:20:47: <font color="#CC0000">Which devour widows' houses, and for a shew make long prayers: the same shall receive greater damnation.</font>
41:21:3: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Of a truth I say unto you, that this poor widow hath cast in more than they all:</font>
41:21:4: <font color="#CC0000">For all these have of their abundance cast in unto the offerings of God: but she of her penury hath cast in all the living that she had.</font>
41:21:6: <font color="#CC0000">As for these things which ye behold, the days will come, in the which there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.</font>
41:21:8: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Take heed that ye be not deceived: for many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and the time draweth near: go ye not therefore after them.</font>
41:21:9: <font color="#CC0000">But when ye shall hear of wars and commotions, be not terrified: for these things must first come to pass; but the end is not by and by.</font>
41:21:10: Then said he unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom:</font>
41:21:11: <font color="#CC0000">And great earthquakes shall be in divers places, and famines, and pestilences; and fearful sights and great signs shall there be from heaven.</font>
41:21:12: <font color="#CC0000">But before all these, they shall lay their hands on you, and persecute you, delivering you up to the synagogues, and into prisons, being brought before kings and rulers for my name's sake.</font>
41:21:13: <font color="#CC0000">And it shall turn to you for a testimony.</font>
41:21:14: <font color="#CC0000">Settle it therefore in your hearts, not to meditate before what ye shall answer:</font>
41:21:15: <font color="#CC0000">For I will give you a mouth and wisdom, which all your adversaries shall not be able to gainsay nor resist.</font>
41:21:16: <font color="#CC0000">And ye shall be betrayed both by parents, and brethren, and kinsfolks, and friends; and some of you shall they cause to be put to death.</font>
41:21:17: <font color="#CC0000">And ye shall be hated of all men for my name's sake.</font>
41:21:18: <font color="#CC0000">But there shall not an hair of your head perish.</font>
41:21:19: <font color="#CC0000">In your patience possess ye your souls.</font>
41:21:20: <font color="#CC0000">And when ye shall see Jerusalem compassed with armies, then know that the desolation thereof is nigh.</font>
41:21:21: <font color="#CC0000">Then let them which are in Judæa flee to the mountains; and let them which are in the midst of it depart out; and let not them that are in the countries enter thereinto.</font>
41:21:22: <font color="#CC0000">For these be the days of vengeance, that all things which are written may be fulfilled.</font>
41:21:23: <font color="#CC0000">But woe unto them that are with child, and to them that give suck, in those days! for there shall be great distress in the land, and wrath upon this people.</font>
41:21:24: <font color="#CC0000">And they shall fall by the edge of the sword, and shall be led away captive into all nations: and Jerusalem shall be trodden down of the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles be fulfilled.</font>
41:21:25: <font color="#CC0000">And there shall be signs in the sun, and in the moon, and in the stars; and upon the earth distress of nations, with perplexity; the sea and the waves roaring;</font>
41:21:26: <font color="#CC0000">Men's hearts failing them for fear, and for looking after those things which are coming on the earth: for the powers of heaven shall be shaken.</font>
41:21:27: <font color="#CC0000">And then shall they see the Son of man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.</font>
41:21:28: <font color="#CC0000">And when these things begin to come to pass, then look up, and lift up your heads; for your redemption draweth nigh.</font>
41:21:29: And he spake to them a parable; <font color="#CC0000">Behold the fig tree, and all the trees;</font>
41:21:30: <font color="#CC0000">When they now shoot forth, ye see and know of your own selves that summer is now nigh at hand.</font>
41:21:31: <font color="#CC0000">So likewise ye, when ye see these things come to pass, know ye that the kingdom of God is nigh at hand.</font>
41:21:32: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass away, till all be fulfilled.</font>
41:21:33: <font color="#CC0000">Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my words shall not pass away.</font>
41:21:34: <font color="#CC0000">And take heed to yourselves, lest at any time your hearts be overcharged with surfeiting, and drunkenness, and cares of this life, and so that day come upon you unawares.</font>
41:21:35: <font color="#CC0000">For as a snare shall it come on all them that dwell on the face of the whole earth.</font>
41:21:36: <font color="#CC0000">Watch ye therefore, and pray always, that ye may be accounted worthy to escape all these things that shall come to pass, and to stand before the Son of man.</font>
41:22:8: And he sent Peter and John, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Go and prepare us the passover, that we may eat.</font>
41:22:10: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Behold, when ye are entered into the city, there shall a man meet you, bearing a pitcher of water; follow him into the house where he entereth in.</font>
41:22:11: <font color="#CC0000">And ye shall say unto the goodman of the house, The Master saith unto thee, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples?</font>
41:22:12: <font color="#CC0000">And he shall shew you a large upper room furnished: there make ready.</font>
41:22:15: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">With desire I have desired to eat this passover with you before I suffer:</font>
41:22:16: <font color="#CC0000">For I say unto you, I will not any more eat thereof, until it be fulfilled in the kingdom of God.</font>
41:22:17: And he took the cup, and gave thanks, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Take this, and divide it among yourselves:</font>
41:22:18: <font color="#CC0000">For I say unto you, I will not drink of the fruit of the vine, until the kingdom of God shall come.</font>
41:22:19: And he took bread, and gave thanks, and brake it, and gave unto them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">This is my body which is given for you: this do in remembrance of me.</font>
41:22:20: Likewise also the cup after supper, saying, <font color="#CC0000">This cup is the new testament in my blood, which is shed for you.</font>
41:22:21: <font color="#CC0000">But, behold, the hand of him that betrayeth me is with me on the table.</font>
41:22:22: <font color="#CC0000">And truly the Son of man goeth, as it was determined: but woe unto that man by whom he is betrayed!</font>
41:22:25: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">The kings of the Gentiles exercise lordship over them; and they that exercise authority upon them are called benefactors.</font>
41:22:26: <font color="#CC0000">But ye shall not be so: but he that is greatest among you, let him be as the younger; and he that is chief, as he that doth serve.</font>
41:22:27: <font color="#CC0000">For whether is greater, he that sitteth at meat, or he that serveth? is not he that sitteth at meat? but I am among you as he that serveth.</font>
41:22:28: <font color="#CC0000">Ye are they which have continued with me in my temptations.</font>
41:22:29: <font color="#CC0000">And I appoint unto you a kingdom, as my Father hath appointed unto me;</font>
41:22:30: <font color="#CC0000">That ye may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.</font>
41:22:31: And the Lord said, <font color="#CC0000">Simon, Simon, behold, Satan hath desired to have you, that he may sift you as wheat:</font>
41:22:32: <font color="#CC0000">But I have prayed for thee, that thy faith fail not: and when thou art converted, strengthen thy brethren.</font>
41:22:34: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">I tell thee, Peter, the cock shall not crow this day, before that thou shalt thrice deny that thou knowest me.</font>
41:22:35: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">When I sent you without purse, and scrip, and shoes, lacked ye any thing?</font> And they said, Nothing.
41:22:36: Then said he unto them, <font color="#CC0000">But now, he that hath a purse, let him take it, and likewise his scrip: and he that hath no sword, let him sell his garment, and buy one.</font>
41:22:37: <font color="#CC0000">For I say unto you, that this that is written must yet be accomplished in me, And he was reckoned among the transgressors: for the things concerning me have an end.</font>
41:22:38: And they said, Lord, behold, here are two swords. And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">It is enough.</font>
41:22:40: And when he was at the place, he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Pray that ye enter not into temptation.</font>
41:22:42: Saying, <font color="#CC0000">Father, if thou be willing, remove this cup from me: nevertheless not my will, but thine, be done.</font>
41:22:46: And said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why sleep ye? rise and pray, lest ye enter into temptation.</font>
41:22:48: But Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Judas, betrayest thou the Son of man with a kiss?</font>
41:22:51: And Jesus answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">Suffer ye thus far.</font> And he touched his ear, and healed him.
41:22:52: Then Jesus said unto the chief priests, and captains of the temple, and the elders, which were come to him, <font color="#CC0000">Be ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and staves?</font>
41:22:53: <font color="#CC0000">When I was daily with you in the temple, ye stretched forth no hands against me: but this is your hour, and the power of darkness.</font>
41:22:67: Art thou the Christ? tell us. And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">If I tell you, ye will not believe:</font>
41:22:68: <font color="#CC0000">And if I also ask you, ye will not answer me, nor let me go.</font>
41:22:69: <font color="#CC0000">Hereafter shall the Son of man sit on the right hand of the power of God.</font>
41:22:70: Then said they all, Art thou then the Son of God? And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Ye say that I am.</font>
41:23:3: And Pilate asked him, saying, Art thou the King of the Jews? And he answered him and said, <font color="#CC0000">Thou sayest it.</font>
41:23:28: But Jesus turning unto them said, <font color="#CC0000">Daughters of Jerusalem, weep not for me, but weep for yourselves, and for your children.</font>
41:23:29: <font color="#CC0000">For, behold, the days are coming, in the which they shall say, Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that never bare, and the paps which never gave suck.</font>
41:23:30: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall they begin to say to the mountains, Fall on us; and to the hills, Cover us.</font>
41:23:31: <font color="#CC0000">For if they do these things in a green tree, what shall be done in the dry?</font>
41:23:34: Then said Jesus, <font color="#CC0000">Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do.</font> And they parted his raiment, and cast lots.
41:23:43: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto thee, To day shalt thou be with me in paradise.</font>
41:23:46: And when Jesus had cried with a loud voice, he said, <font color="#CC0000">Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit:</font> and having said thus, he gave up the ghost.
41:24:17: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">What manner of communications are these that ye have one to another, as ye walk, and are sad?</font>
