41:24:19: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">What things?</font> And they said unto him, Concerning Jesus of Nazareth, which was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people:
41:24:25: Then he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">O fools, and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken:</font>
41:24:26: <font color="#CC0000">Ought not Christ to have suffered these things, and to enter into his glory?</font>
41:24:36: And as they thus spake, Jesus himself stood in the midst of them, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Peace be unto you.</font>
41:24:38: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why are ye troubled? and why do thoughts arise in your hearts?</font>
41:24:39: <font color="#CC0000">Behold my hands and my feet, that it is I myself: handle me, and see; for a spirit hath not flesh and bones, as ye see me have.</font>
41:24:41: And while they yet believed not for joy, and wondered, he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Have ye here any meat?</font>
41:24:44: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">These are the words which I spake unto you, while I was yet with you, that all things must be fulfilled, which were written in the law of Moses, and in the prophets, and in the psalms, concerning me.</font>
41:24:46: And said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Thus it is written, and thus it behoved Christ to suffer, and to rise from the dead the third day:</font>
41:24:47: <font color="#CC0000">And that repentance and remission of sins should be preached in his name among all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.</font>
41:24:48: <font color="#CC0000">And ye are witnesses of these things.</font>
41:24:49: <font color="#CC0000">And, behold, I send the promise of my Father upon you: but tarry ye in the city of Jerusalem, until ye be endued with power from on high.</font>
42:1:38: Then Jesus turned, and saw them following, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">What seek ye?</font> They said unto him, Rabbi, (which is to say, being interpreted, Master,) where dwellest thou?
42:1:39: He saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Come and see.</font> They came and saw where he dwelt, and abode with him that day: for it was about the tenth hour.
42:1:42: And he brought him to Jesus. And when Jesus beheld him, he said, <font color="#CC0000">Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas,</font> which is by interpretation, A stone.
42:1:43: The day following Jesus would go forth into Galilee, and findeth Philip, and saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Follow me.</font>
42:1:47: Jesus saw Nathanael coming to him, and saith of him, <font color="#CC0000">Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile!</font>
42:1:48: Nathanael saith unto him, Whence knowest thou me? Jesus answered and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Before that Philip called thee, when thou wast under the fig tree, I saw thee.</font>
42:1:50: Jesus answered and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Because I said unto thee, I saw thee under the fig tree, believest thou? thou shalt see greater things than these.</font>
42:1:51: And he saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, Hereafter ye shall see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of man.</font>
42:2:4: Jesus saith unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Woman, what have I to do with thee? mine hour is not yet come.</font>
42:2:7: Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Fill the waterpots with water.</font> And they filled them up to the brim.
42:2:8: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Draw out now, and bear unto the governor of the feast.</font> And they bare it.
42:2:16: And said unto them that sold doves, <font color="#CC0000">Take these things hence; make not my Father's house an house of merchandise.</font>
42:2:19: Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.</font>
42:3:3: Jesus answered and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.</font>
42:3:5: Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.</font>
42:3:6: <font color="#CC0000">That which is born of the flesh is flesh; and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.</font>
42:3:7: <font color="#CC0000">Marvel not that I said unto thee, Ye must be born again.</font>
42:3:8: <font color="#CC0000">The wind bloweth where it listeth, and thou hearest the sound thereof, but canst not tell whence it cometh, and whither it goeth: so is every one that is born of the Spirit.</font>
42:3:10: Jesus answered and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Art thou a master of Israel, and knowest not these things?</font>
42:3:11: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto thee, We speak that we do know, and testify that we have seen; and ye receive not our witness.</font>
42:3:12: <font color="#CC0000">If I have told you earthly things, and ye believe not, how shall ye believe, if I tell you of heavenly things?</font>
42:3:13: <font color="#CC0000">And no man hath ascended up to heaven, but he that came down from heaven, even the Son of man which is in heaven.</font>
42:3:14: <font color="#CC0000">And as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of man be lifted up:</font>
42:3:15: <font color="#CC0000">That whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have eternal life.</font>
42:3:16: <font color="#CC0000">For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.</font>
42:3:17: <font color="#CC0000">For God sent not his Son into the world to condemn the world; but that the world through him might be saved.</font>
42:3:18: <font color="#CC0000">He that believeth on him is not condemned: but he that believeth not is condemned already, because he hath not believed in the name of the only begotten Son of God.</font>
42:3:19: <font color="#CC0000">And this is the condemnation, that light is come into the world, and men loved darkness rather than light, because their deeds were evil.</font>
42:3:20: <font color="#CC0000">For every one that doeth evil hateth the light, neither cometh to the light, lest his deeds should be reproved.</font>
42:3:21: <font color="#CC0000">But he that doeth truth cometh to the light, that his deeds may be made manifest, that they are wrought in God.</font>
42:4:7: There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water: Jesus saith unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Give me to drink.</font>
42:4:10: Jesus answered and said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">If thou knewest the gift of God, and who it is that saith to thee, Give me to drink; thou wouldest have asked of him, and he would have given thee living water.</font>
42:4:13: Jesus answered and said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever drinketh of this water shall thirst again:</font>
42:4:14: <font color="#CC0000">But whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst; but the water that I shall give him shall be in him a well of water springing up into everlasting life.</font>
42:4:16: Jesus saith unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Go, call thy husband, and come hither.</font>
42:4:17: The woman answered and said, I have no husband. Jesus said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Thou hast well said, I have no husband:</font>
42:4:18: <font color="#CC0000">For thou hast had five husbands; and he whom thou now hast is not thy husband: in that saidst thou truly.</font>
42:4:21: Jesus saith unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Woman, believe me, the hour cometh, when ye shall neither in this mountain, nor yet at Jerusalem, worship the Father.</font>
42:4:22: <font color="#CC0000">Ye worship ye know not what: we know what we worship: for salvation is of the Jews.</font>
42:4:23: <font color="#CC0000">But the hour cometh, and now is, when the true worshippers shall worship the Father in spirit and in truth: for the Father seeketh such to worship him.</font>
42:4:24: <font color="#CC0000">God is a Spirit: and they that worship him must worship him in spirit and in truth.</font>
42:4:26: Jesus saith unto her, <font color="#CC0000">I that speak unto thee am he.</font>
42:4:32: But he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I have meat to eat that ye know not of.</font>
42:4:34: Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">My meat is to do the will of him that sent me, and to finish his work.</font>
42:4:35: <font color="#CC0000">Say not ye, There are yet four months, and then cometh harvest? behold, I say unto you, Lift up your eyes, and look on the fields; for they are white already to harvest.</font>
42:4:36: <font color="#CC0000">And he that reapeth receiveth wages, and gathereth fruit unto life eternal: that both he that soweth and he that reapeth may rejoice together.</font>
42:4:37: <font color="#CC0000">And herein is that saying true, One soweth, and another reapeth.</font>
42:4:38: <font color="#CC0000">I sent you to reap that whereon ye bestowed no labour: other men laboured, and ye are entered into their labours.</font>
42:4:48: Then said Jesus unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Except ye see signs and wonders, ye will not believe.</font>
42:4:50: Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Go thy way; thy son liveth.</font> And the man believed the word that Jesus had spoken unto him, and he went his way.
42:5:6: When Jesus saw him lie, and knew that he had been now a long time in that case, he saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Wilt thou be made whole?</font>
42:5:8: Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Rise, take up thy bed, and walk.</font>
42:5:14: Afterward Jesus findeth him in the temple, and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Behold, thou art made whole: sin no more, lest a worse thing come unto thee.</font>
42:5:17: But Jesus answered them, <font color="#CC0000">My Father worketh hitherto, and I work.</font>
42:5:19: Then answered Jesus and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, The Son can do nothing of himself, but what he seeth the Father do: for what things soever he doeth, these also doeth the Son likewise.</font>
42:5:20: <font color="#CC0000">For the Father loveth the Son, and sheweth him all things that himself doeth: and he will shew him greater works than these, that ye may marvel.</font>
42:5:21: <font color="#CC0000">For as the Father raiseth up the dead, and quickeneth them; even so the Son quickeneth whom he will.</font>
42:5:22: <font color="#CC0000">For the Father judgeth no man, but hath committed all judgment unto the Son:</font>
42:5:23: <font color="#CC0000">That all men should honour the Son, even as they honour the Father. He that honoureth not the Son honoureth not the Father which hath sent him.</font>
42:5:24: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that heareth my word, and believeth on him that sent me, hath everlasting life, and shall not come into condemnation; but is passed from death unto life.</font>
42:5:25: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, The hour is coming, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God: and they that hear shall live.</font>
42:5:26: <font color="#CC0000">For as the Father hath life in himself; so hath he given to the Son to have life in himself;</font>
42:5:27: <font color="#CC0000">And hath given him authority to execute judgment also, because he is the Son of man.</font>
42:5:28: <font color="#CC0000">Marvel not at this: for the hour is coming, in the which all that are in the graves shall hear his voice,</font>
42:5:29: <font color="#CC0000">And shall come forth; they that have done good, unto the resurrection of life; and they that have done evil, unto the resurrection of damnation.</font>
42:5:30: <font color="#CC0000">I can of mine own self do nothing: as I hear, I judge: and my judgment is just; because I seek not mine own will, but the will of the Father which hath sent me.</font>
42:5:31: <font color="#CC0000">If I bear witness of myself, my witness is not true.</font>
42:5:32: <font color="#CC0000">There is another that beareth witness of me; and I know that the witness which he witnesseth of me is true.</font>
42:5:33: <font color="#CC0000">Ye sent unto John, and he bare witness unto the truth.</font>
42:5:34: <font color="#CC0000">But I receive not testimony from man: but these things I say, that ye might be saved.</font>
42:5:35: <font color="#CC0000">He was a burning and a shining light: and ye were willing for a season to rejoice in his light.</font>
42:5:36: <font color="#CC0000">But I have greater witness than that of John: for the works which the Father hath given me to finish, the same works that I do, bear witness of me, that the Father hath sent me.</font>
42:5:37: <font color="#CC0000">And the Father himself, which hath sent me, hath borne witness of me. Ye have neither heard his voice at any time, nor seen his shape.</font>
42:5:38: <font color="#CC0000">And ye have not his word abiding in you: for whom he hath sent, him ye believe not.</font>
42:5:39: <font color="#CC0000">Search the scriptures; for in them ye think ye have eternal life: and they are they which testify of me.</font>
42:5:40: <font color="#CC0000">And ye will not come to me, that ye might have life.</font>
42:5:41: <font color="#CC0000">I receive not honour from men.</font>
42:5:42: <font color="#CC0000">But I know you, that ye have not the love of God in you.</font>
42:5:43: <font color="#CC0000">I am come in my Father's name, and ye receive me not: if another shall come in his own name, him ye will receive.</font>
42:5:44: <font color="#CC0000">How can ye believe, which receive honour one of another, and seek not the honour that cometh from God only?</font>
42:5:45: <font color="#CC0000">Do not think that I will accuse you to the Father: there is one that accuseth you, even Moses, in whom ye trust.</font>
42:5:46: <font color="#CC0000">For had ye believed Moses, ye would have believed me: for he wrote of me.</font>
42:5:47: <font color="#CC0000">But if ye believe not his writings, how shall ye believe my words?</font>
42:6:5: When Jesus then lifted up his eyes, and saw a great company come unto him, he saith unto Philip, <font color="#CC0000">Whence shall we buy bread, that these may eat?</font>
42:6:10: And Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">Make the men sit down.</font> Now there was much grass in the place. So the men sat down, in number about five thousand.
42:6:12: When they were filled, he said unto his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">Gather up the fragments that remain, that nothing be lost.</font>
42:6:20: But he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">It is I; be not afraid.</font>
42:6:26: Jesus answered them and said, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, Ye seek me, not because ye saw the miracles, but because ye did eat of the loaves, and were filled.</font>
42:6:27: <font color="#CC0000">Labour not for the meat which perisheth, but for that meat which endureth unto everlasting life, which the Son of man shall give unto you: for him hath God the Father sealed.</font>
42:6:29: Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">This is the work of God, that ye believe on him whom he hath sent.</font>
42:6:32: Then Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, Moses gave you not that bread from heaven; but my Father giveth you the true bread from heaven.</font>
42:6:33: <font color="#CC0000">For the bread of God is he which cometh down from heaven, and giveth life unto the world.</font>
42:6:35: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I am the bread of life: he that cometh to me shall never hunger; and he that believeth on me shall never thirst.</font>
42:6:36: <font color="#CC0000">But I said unto you, That ye also have seen me, and believe not.</font>
42:6:37: <font color="#CC0000">All that the Father giveth me shall come to me; and him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out.</font>
42:6:38: <font color="#CC0000">For I came down from heaven, not to do mine own will, but the will of him that sent me.</font>
42:6:39: <font color="#CC0000">And this is the Father's will which hath sent me, that of all which he hath given me I should lose nothing, but should raise it up again at the last day.</font>
42:6:40: <font color="#CC0000">And this is the will of him that sent me, that every one which seeth the Son, and believeth on him, may have everlasting life: and I will raise him up at the last day.</font>
42:6:43: Jesus therefore answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Murmur not among yourselves.</font>
42:6:44: <font color="#CC0000">No man can come to me, except the Father which hath sent me draw him: and I will raise him up at the last day.</font>
42:6:45: <font color="#CC0000">It is written in the prophets, And they shall be all taught of God. Every man therefore that hath heard, and hath learned of the Father, cometh unto me.</font>
42:6:46: <font color="#CC0000">Not that any man hath seen the Father, save he which is of God, he hath seen the Father.</font>
42:6:47: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth on me hath everlasting life.</font>
42:6:48: <font color="#CC0000">I am that bread of life.</font>
42:6:49: <font color="#CC0000">Your fathers did eat manna in the wilderness, and are dead.</font>
42:6:50: <font color="#CC0000">This is the bread which cometh down from heaven, that a man may eat thereof, and not die.</font>
42:6:51: <font color="#CC0000">I am the living bread which came down from heaven: if any man eat of this bread, he shall live for ever: and the bread that I will give is my flesh, which I will give for the life of the world.</font>
42:6:53: Then Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except ye eat the flesh of the Son of man, and drink his blood, ye have no life in you.</font>
42:6:54: <font color="#CC0000">Whoso eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, hath eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day.</font>
42:6:55: <font color="#CC0000">For my flesh is meat indeed, and my blood is drink indeed.</font>
42:6:56: <font color="#CC0000">He that eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, dwelleth in me, and I in him.</font>
42:6:57: <font color="#CC0000">As the living Father hath sent me, and I live by the Father: so he that eateth me, even he shall live by me.</font>
42:6:58: <font color="#CC0000">This is that bread which came down from heaven: not as your fathers did eat manna, and are dead: he that eateth of this bread shall live for ever.</font>
42:6:61: When Jesus knew in himself that his disciples murmured at it, he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Doth this offend you?</font>
42:6:62: <font color="#CC0000">What and if ye shall see the Son of man ascend up where he was before?</font>
42:6:63: <font color="#CC0000">It is the spirit that quickeneth; the flesh profiteth nothing: the words that I speak unto you, they are spirit, and they are life.</font>
42:6:64: <font color="#CC0000">But there are some of you that believe not.</font> For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were that believed not, and who should betray him.
42:6:65: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Therefore said I unto you, that no man can come unto me, except it were given unto him of my Father.</font>
42:6:67: Then said Jesus unto the twelve, <font color="#CC0000">Will ye also go away?</font>
42:6:70: Jesus answered them, <font color="#CC0000">Have not I chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil?</font>
42:7:6: Then Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">My time is not yet come: but your time is alway ready.</font>
42:7:7: <font color="#CC0000">The world cannot hate you; but me it hateth, because I testify of it, that the works thereof are evil.</font>
42:7:8: <font color="#CC0000">Go ye up unto this feast: I go not up yet unto this feast; for my time is not yet full come.</font>
42:7:16: Jesus answered them, and said, <font color="#CC0000">My doctrine is not mine, but his that sent me.</font>
42:7:17: <font color="#CC0000">If any man will do his will, he shall know of the doctrine, whether it be of God, or whether I speak of myself.</font>
42:7:18: <font color="#CC0000">He that speaketh of himself seeketh his own glory: but he that seeketh his glory that sent him, the same is true, and no unrighteousness is in him.</font>
42:7:19: <font color="#CC0000">Did not Moses give you the law, and yet none of you keepeth the law? Why go ye about to kill me?</font>
42:7:21: Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I have done one work, and ye all marvel.</font>
42:7:22: <font color="#CC0000">Moses therefore gave unto you circumcision; (not because it is of Moses, but of the fathers;) and ye on the sabbath day circumcise a man.</font>
42:7:23: <font color="#CC0000">If a man on the sabbath day receive circumcision, that the law of Moses should not be broken; are ye angry at me, because I have made a man every whit whole on the sabbath day?</font>
42:7:24: <font color="#CC0000">Judge not according to the appearance, but judge righteous judgment.</font>
42:7:28: Then cried Jesus in the temple as he taught, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Ye both know me, and ye know whence I am: and I am not come of myself, but he that sent me is true, whom ye know not.</font>
42:7:29: <font color="#CC0000">But I know him: for I am from him, and he hath sent me.</font>
42:7:33: Then said Jesus unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Yet a little while am I with you, and then I go unto him that sent me.</font>
42:7:34: <font color="#CC0000">Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come.</font>
42:7:37: In the last day, that great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried, saying, <font color="#CC0000">If any man thirst, let him come unto me, and drink.</font>
42:7:38: <font color="#CC0000">He that believeth on me, as the scripture hath said, out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.</font>
42:8:7: So when they continued asking him, he lifted up himself, and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">He that is without sin among you, let him first cast a stone at her.</font>
42:8:10: When Jesus had lifted up himself, and saw none but the woman, he said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Woman, where are those thine accusers? hath no man condemned thee?</font>
42:8:11: She said, No man, Lord. And Jesus said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Neither do I condemn thee: go, and sin no more.</font>
42:8:12: Then spake Jesus again unto them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall not walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life.</font>
42:8:14: Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Though I bear record of myself, yet my record is true: for I know whence I came, and whither I go; but ye cannot tell whence I come, and whither I go.</font>
42:8:15: <font color="#CC0000">Ye judge after the flesh; I judge no man.</font>
42:8:16: <font color="#CC0000">And yet if I judge, my judgment is true: for I am not alone, but I and the Father that sent me.</font>
42:8:17: <font color="#CC0000">It is also written in your law, that the testimony of two men is true.</font>
42:8:18: <font color="#CC0000">I am one that bear witness of myself, and the Father that sent me beareth witness of me.</font>
42:8:19: Then said they unto him, Where is thy Father? Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">Ye neither know me, nor my Father: if ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also.</font>
42:8:21: Then said Jesus again unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I go my way, and ye shall seek me, and shall die in your sins: whither I go, ye cannot come.</font>
42:8:23: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Ye are from beneath; I am from above: ye are of this world; I am not of this world.</font>
42:8:24: <font color="#CC0000">I said therefore unto you, that ye shall die in your sins: for if ye believe not that I am he, ye shall die in your sins.</font>
42:8:25: Then said they unto him, Who art thou? And Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Even the same that I said unto you from the beginning.</font>
42:8:26: <font color="#CC0000">I have many things to say and to judge of you: but he that sent me is true; and I speak to the world those things which I have heard of him.</font>
42:8:28: Then said Jesus unto them, <font color="#CC0000">When ye have lifted up the Son of man, then shall ye know that I am he, and that I do nothing of myself; but as my Father hath taught me, I speak these things.</font>
42:8:29: <font color="#CC0000">And he that sent me is with me: the Father hath not left me alone; for I do always those things that please him.</font>
42:8:31: Then said Jesus to those Jews which believed on him, <font color="#CC0000">If ye continue in my word, then are ye my disciples indeed;</font>
42:8:32: <font color="#CC0000">And ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.</font>
42:8:34: Jesus answered them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whosoever committeth sin is the servant of sin.</font>
42:8:35: <font color="#CC0000">And the servant abideth not in the house for ever: but the Son abideth ever.</font>
42:8:36: <font color="#CC0000">If the Son therefore shall make you free, ye shall be free indeed.</font>
42:8:37: <font color="#CC0000">I know that ye are Abraham's seed; but ye seek to kill me, because my word hath no place in you.</font>
42:8:38: <font color="#CC0000">I speak that which I have seen with my Father: and ye do that which ye have seen with your father.</font>
42:8:39: They answered and said unto him, Abraham is our father. Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">If ye were Abraham's children, ye would do the works of Abraham.</font>
42:8:40: <font color="#CC0000">But now ye seek to kill me, a man that hath told you the truth, which I have heard of God: this did not Abraham.</font>
42:8:41: <font color="#CC0000">Ye do the deeds of your father.</font> Then said they to him, We be not born of fornication; we have one Father, even God.
42:8:42: Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">If God were your Father, ye would love me: for I proceeded forth and came from God; neither came I of myself, but he sent me.</font>
42:8:43: <font color="#CC0000">Why do ye not understand my speech? even because ye cannot hear my word.</font>
42:8:44: <font color="#CC0000">Ye are of your father the devil, and the lusts of your father ye will do. He was a murderer from the beginning, and abode not in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaketh a lie, he speaketh of his own: for he is a liar, and the father of it.</font>
42:8:45: <font color="#CC0000">And because I tell you the truth, ye believe me not.</font>
42:8:46: <font color="#CC0000">Which of you convinceth me of sin? And if I say the truth, why do ye not believe me?</font>
42:8:47: <font color="#CC0000">He that is of God heareth God's words: ye therefore hear them not, because ye are not of God.</font>
42:8:49: Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">I have not a devil; but I honour my Father, and ye do dishonour me.</font>
42:8:50: <font color="#CC0000">And I seek not mine own glory: there is one that seeketh and judgeth.</font>
42:8:51: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, If a man keep my saying, he shall never see death.</font>
42:8:54: Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">If I honour myself, my honour is nothing: it is my Father that honoureth me; of whom ye say, that he is your God:</font>
42:8:55: <font color="#CC0000">Yet ye have not known him; but I know him: and if I should say, I know him not, I shall be a liar like unto you: but I know him, and keep his saying.</font>
42:8:56: <font color="#CC0000">Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day: and he saw it, and was glad.</font>
42:8:58: Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, Before Abraham was, I am.</font>
42:9:3: Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">Neither hath this man sinned, nor his parents: but that the works of God should be made manifest in him.</font>
42:9:4: <font color="#CC0000">I must work the works of him that sent me, while it is day: the night cometh, when no man can work.</font>
42:9:5: <font color="#CC0000">As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.</font>
42:9:7: And said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Go, wash in the pool of Siloam,</font> (which is by interpretation, Sent.) He went his way therefore, and washed, and came seeing.
42:9:35: Jesus heard that they had cast him out; and when he had found him, he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Dost thou believe on the Son of God?</font>
42:9:37: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou hast both seen him, and it is he that talketh with thee.</font>
42:9:39: And Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">For judgment I am come into this world, that they which see not might see; and that they which see might be made blind.</font>
42:9:41: Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">If ye were blind, ye should have no sin: but now ye say, We see; therefore your sin remaineth.</font>
42:10:1: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that entereth not by the door into the sheepfold, but climbeth up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber.</font>
42:10:2: <font color="#CC0000">But he that entereth in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.</font>
42:10:3: <font color="#CC0000">To him the porter openeth; and the sheep hear his voice: and he calleth his own sheep by name, and leadeth them out.</font>
42:10:4: <font color="#CC0000">And when he putteth forth his own sheep, he goeth before them, and the sheep follow him: for they know his voice.</font>
42:10:5: <font color="#CC0000">And a stranger will they not follow, but will flee from him: for they know not the voice of strangers.</font>
42:10:7: Then said Jesus unto them again, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, I am the door of the sheep.</font>
42:10:8: <font color="#CC0000">All that ever came before me are thieves and robbers: but the sheep did not hear them.</font>
42:10:9: <font color="#CC0000">I am the door: by me if any man enter in, he shall be saved, and shall go in and out, and find pasture.</font>
42:10:10: <font color="#CC0000">The thief cometh not, but for to steal, and to kill, and to destroy: I am come that they might have life, and that they might have it more abundantly.</font>
42:10:11: <font color="#CC0000">I am the good shepherd: the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep.</font>
42:10:12: <font color="#CC0000">But he that is an hireling, and not the shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, seeth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep, and fleeth: and the wolf catcheth them, and scattereth the sheep.</font>
42:10:13: <font color="#CC0000">The hireling fleeth, because he is an hireling, and careth not for the sheep.</font>
42:10:14: <font color="#CC0000">I am the good shepherd, and know my sheep, and am known of mine.</font>
42:10:15: <font color="#CC0000">As the Father knoweth me, even so know I the Father: and I lay down my life for the sheep.</font>
42:10:16: <font color="#CC0000">And other sheep I have, which are not of this fold: them also I must bring, and they shall hear my voice; and there shall be one fold, and one shepherd.</font>
42:10:17: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore doth my Father love me, because I lay down my life, that I might take it again.</font>
42:10:18: <font color="#CC0000">No man taketh it from me, but I lay it down of myself. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again. This commandment have I received of my Father.</font>
42:10:25: Jesus answered them, <font color="#CC0000">I told you, and ye believed not: the works that I do in my Father's name, they bear witness of me.</font>
42:10:26: <font color="#CC0000">But ye believe not, because ye are not of my sheep, as I said unto you.</font>
42:10:27: <font color="#CC0000">My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me:</font>
42:10:28: <font color="#CC0000">And I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish, neither shall any man pluck them out of my hand.</font>
42:10:29: <font color="#CC0000">My Father, which gave them me, is greater than all; and no man is able to pluck them out of my Father's hand.</font>
42:10:30: <font color="#CC0000">I and my Father are one.</font>
42:10:32: Jesus answered them, <font color="#CC0000">Many good works have I shewed you from my Father; for which of those works do ye stone me?</font>
42:10:34: Jesus answered them, <font color="#CC0000">Is it not written in your law, I said, Ye are gods?</font>
42:10:35: <font color="#CC0000">If he called them gods, unto whom the word of God came, and the scripture cannot be broken;</font>
42:10:36: <font color="#CC0000">Say ye of him, whom the Father hath sanctified, and sent into the world, Thou blasphemest; because I said, I am the Son of God?</font>
42:10:37: <font color="#CC0000">If I do not the works of my Father, believe me not.</font>
42:10:38: <font color="#CC0000">But if I do, though ye believe not me, believe the works: that ye may know, and believe, that the Father is in me, and I in him.</font>
42:11:4: When Jesus heard that, he said, <font color="#CC0000">This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God might be glorified thereby.</font>
42:11:7: Then after that saith he to his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">Let us go into Judæa again.</font>
42:11:9: Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">Are there not twelve hours in the day? If any man walk in the day, he stumbleth not, because he seeth the light of this world.</font>
42:11:10: <font color="#CC0000">But if a man walk in the night, he stumbleth, because there is no light in him.</font>
42:11:11: These things said he: and after that he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Our friend Lazarus sleepeth; but I go, that I may awake him out of sleep.</font>
42:11:14: Then said Jesus unto them plainly, <font color="#CC0000">Lazarus is dead.</font>
42:11:15: <font color="#CC0000">And I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, to the intent ye may believe; nevertheless let us go unto him.</font>
42:11:23: Jesus saith unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Thy brother shall rise again.</font>
42:11:25: Jesus said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live:</font>
42:11:26: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die. Believest thou this?</font>
42:11:34: And said, <font color="#CC0000">Where have ye laid him?</font> They said unto him, Lord, come and see.
42:11:39: Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">Take ye away the stone.</font> Martha, the sister of him that was dead, saith unto him, Lord, by this time he stinketh: for he hath been dead four days.
42:11:40: Jesus saith unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Said I not unto thee, that, if thou wouldest believe, thou shouldest see the glory of God?</font>
42:11:41: Then they took away the stone from the place where the dead was laid. And Jesus lifted up his eyes, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Father, I thank thee that thou hast heard me.</font>
42:11:42: <font color="#CC0000">And I knew that thou hearest me always: but because of the people which stand by I said it, that they may believe that thou hast sent me.</font>
42:11:43: And when he thus had spoken, he cried with a loud voice, <font color="#CC0000">Lazarus, come forth.</font>
42:11:44: And he that was dead came forth, bound hand and foot with graveclothes: and his face was bound about with a napkin. Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Loose him, and let him go.</font>
42:12:7: Then said Jesus, <font color="#CC0000">Let her alone: against the day of my burying hath she kept this.</font>
42:12:8: <font color="#CC0000">For the poor always ye have with you; but me ye have not always.</font>
42:12:23: And Jesus answered them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">The hour is come, that the Son of man should be glorified.</font>
42:12:24: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except a corn of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone: but if it die, it bringeth forth much fruit.</font>
42:12:25: <font color="#CC0000">He that loveth his life shall lose it; and he that hateth his life in this world shall keep it unto life eternal.</font>
42:12:26: <font color="#CC0000">If any man serve me, let him follow me; and where I am, there shall also my servant be: if any man serve me, him will my Father honour.</font>
42:12:27: <font color="#CC0000">Now is my soul troubled; and what shall I say? Father, save me from this hour: but for this cause came I unto this hour.</font>
42:12:28: <font color="#CC0000">Father, glorify thy name.</font> Then came there a voice from heaven, saying, I have both glorified it, and will glorify it again.
42:12:30: Jesus answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">This voice came not because of me, but for your sakes.</font>
42:12:31: <font color="#CC0000">Now is the judgment of this world: now shall the prince of this world be cast out.</font>
42:12:32: <font color="#CC0000">And I, if I be lifted up from the earth, will draw all men unto me.</font>
42:12:35: Then Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Yet a little while is the light with you. Walk while ye have the light, lest darkness come upon you: for he that walketh in darkness knoweth not whither he goeth.</font>
42:12:36: <font color="#CC0000">While ye have light, believe in the light, that ye may be the children of light.</font> These things spake Jesus, and departed, and did hide himself from them.
42:12:44: Jesus cried and said, <font color="#CC0000">He that believeth on me, believeth not on me, but on him that sent me.</font>
42:12:45: <font color="#CC0000">And he that seeth me seeth him that sent me.</font>
42:12:46: <font color="#CC0000">I am come a light into the world, that whosoever believeth on me should not abide in darkness.</font>
42:12:47: <font color="#CC0000">And if any man hear my words, and believe not, I judge him not: for I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.</font>
42:12:48: <font color="#CC0000">He that rejecteth me, and receiveth not my words, hath one that judgeth him: the word that I have spoken, the same shall judge him in the last day.</font>
42:12:49: <font color="#CC0000">For I have not spoken of myself; but the Father which sent me, he gave me a commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak.</font>
42:12:50: <font color="#CC0000">And I know that his commandment is life everlasting: whatsoever I speak therefore, even as the Father said unto me, so I speak.</font>
42:13:7: Jesus answered and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">What I do thou knowest not now; but thou shalt know hereafter.</font>
42:13:8: Peter saith unto him, Thou shalt never wash my feet. Jesus answered him, <font color="#CC0000">If I wash thee not, thou hast no part with me.</font>
42:13:10: Jesus saith to him, <font color="#CC0000">He that is washed needeth not save to wash his feet, but is clean every whit: and ye are clean, but not all.</font>
42:13:12: So after he had washed their feet, and had taken his garments, and was set down again, he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Know ye what I have done to you?</font>
42:13:13: <font color="#CC0000">Ye call me Master and Lord: and ye say well; for so I am.</font>
42:13:14: <font color="#CC0000">If I then, your Lord and Master, have washed your feet; ye also ought to wash one another's feet.</font>
42:13:15: <font color="#CC0000">For I have given you an example, that ye should do as I have done to you.</font>
42:13:16: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord; neither he that is sent greater than he that sent him.</font>
42:13:17: <font color="#CC0000">If ye know these things, happy are ye if ye do them.</font>
42:13:18: <font color="#CC0000">I speak not of you all: I know whom I have chosen: but that the scripture may be fulfilled, He that eateth bread with me hath lifted up his heel against me.</font>
42:13:19: <font color="#CC0000">Now I tell you before it come, that, when it is come to pass, ye may believe that I am he.</font>
42:13:20: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that receiveth whomsoever I send receiveth me; and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.</font>
42:13:21: When Jesus had thus said, he was troubled in spirit, and testified, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.</font>
42:13:26: Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">He it is, to whom I shall give a sop, when I have dipped it.</font> And when he had dipped the sop, he gave it to Judas Iscariot, the son of Simon.
42:13:27: And after the sop Satan entered into him. Then said Jesus unto him, <font color="#CC0000">That thou doest, do quickly.</font>
42:13:31: Therefore, when he was gone out, Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">Now is the Son of man glorified, and God is glorified in him.</font>
42:13:32: <font color="#CC0000">If God be glorified in him, God shall also glorify him in himself, and shall straightway glorify him.</font>
42:13:33: <font color="#CC0000">Little children, yet a little while I am with you. Ye shall seek me: and as I said unto the Jews, Whither I go, ye cannot come; so now I say to you.</font>
42:13:34: <font color="#CC0000">A new commandment I give unto you, That ye love one another; as I have loved you, that ye also love one another.</font>
42:13:35: <font color="#CC0000">By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples, if ye have love one to another.</font>
42:13:36: Simon Peter said unto him, Lord, whither goest thou? Jesus answered him, <font color="#CC0000">Whither I go, thou canst not follow me now; but thou shalt follow me afterwards.</font>
42:13:38: Jesus answered him, <font color="#CC0000">Wilt thou lay down thy life for my sake? Verily, verily, I say unto thee, The cock shall not crow, till thou hast denied me thrice.</font>
42:14:1: <font color="#CC0000">Let not your heart be troubled: ye believe in God, believe also in me.</font>
42:14:2: <font color="#CC0000">In my Father's house are many mansions: if it were not so, I would have told you. I go to prepare a place for you.</font>
42:14:3: <font color="#CC0000">And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and receive you unto myself; that where I am, there ye may be also.</font>
42:14:4: <font color="#CC0000">And whither I go ye know, and the way ye know.</font>
42:14:6: Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.</font>
42:14:7: <font color="#CC0000">If ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also: and from henceforth ye know him, and have seen him.</font>
42:14:9: Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Have I been so long time with you, and yet hast thou not known me, Philip? he that hath seen me hath seen the Father; and how sayest thou then, Shew us the Father?</font>
42:14:10: <font color="#CC0000">Believest thou not that I am in the Father, and the Father in me? the words that I speak unto you I speak not of myself: but the Father that dwelleth in me, he doeth the works.</font>
42:14:11: <font color="#CC0000">Believe me that I am in the Father, and the Father in me: or else believe me for the very works' sake.</font>
42:14:12: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth on me, the works that I do shall he do also; and greater works than these shall he do; because I go unto my Father.</font>
42:14:13: <font color="#CC0000">And whatsoever ye shall ask in my name, that will I do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.</font>
42:14:14: <font color="#CC0000">If ye shall ask any thing in my name, I will do it.</font>
42:14:15: <font color="#CC0000">If ye love me, keep my commandments.</font>
42:14:16: <font color="#CC0000">And I will pray the Father, and he shall give you another Comforter, that he may abide with you for ever;</font>
42:14:17: <font color="#CC0000">Even the Spirit of truth; whom the world cannot receive, because it seeth him not, neither knoweth him: but ye know him; for he dwelleth with you, and shall be in you.</font>
42:14:18: <font color="#CC0000">I will not leave you comfortless: I will come to you.</font>
42:14:19: <font color="#CC0000">Yet a little while, and the world seeth me no more; but ye see me: because I live, ye shall live also.</font>
42:14:20: <font color="#CC0000">At that day ye shall know that I am in my Father, and ye in me, and I in you.</font>
42:14:21: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath my commandments, and keepeth them, he it is that loveth me: and he that loveth me shall be loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will manifest myself to him.</font>
42:14:23: Jesus answered and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">If a man love me, he will keep my words: and my Father will love him, and we will come unto him, and make our abode with him.</font>
42:14:24: <font color="#CC0000">He that loveth me not keepeth not my sayings: and the word which ye hear is not mine, but the Father's which sent me.</font>
42:14:25: <font color="#CC0000">These things have I spoken unto you, being yet present with you.</font>
42:14:26: <font color="#CC0000">But the Comforter, which is the Holy Ghost, whom the Father will send in my name, he shall teach you all things, and bring all things to your remembrance, whatsoever I have said unto you.</font>
42:14:27: <font color="#CC0000">Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.</font>
42:14:28: <font color="#CC0000">Ye have heard how I said unto you, I go away, and come again unto you. If ye loved me, ye would rejoice, because I said, I go unto the Father: for my Father is greater than I.</font>
42:14:29: <font color="#CC0000">And now I have told you before it come to pass, that, when it is come to pass, ye might believe.</font>
42:14:30: <font color="#CC0000">Hereafter I will not talk much with you: for the prince of this world cometh, and hath nothing in me.</font>
42:14:31: <font color="#CC0000">But that the world may know that I love the Father; and as the Father gave me commandment, even so I do. Arise, let us go hence.</font>
42:15:1: <font color="#CC0000">I am the true vine, and my Father is the husbandman.</font>
42:15:2: <font color="#CC0000">Every branch in me that beareth not fruit he taketh away: and every branch that beareth fruit, he purgeth it, that it may bring forth more fruit.</font>
42:15:3: <font color="#CC0000">Now ye are clean through the word which I have spoken unto you.</font>
42:15:4: <font color="#CC0000">Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; no more can ye, except ye abide in me.</font>
42:15:5: <font color="#CC0000">I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do nothing.</font>
42:15:6: <font color="#CC0000">If a man abide not in me, he is cast forth as a branch, and is withered; and men gather them, and cast them into the fire, and they are burned.</font>
42:15:7: <font color="#CC0000">If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you.</font>
42:15:8: <font color="#CC0000">Herein is my Father glorified, that ye bear much fruit; so shall ye be my disciples.</font>
42:15:9: <font color="#CC0000">As the Father hath loved me, so have I loved you: continue ye in my love.</font>
42:15:10: <font color="#CC0000">If ye keep my commandments, ye shall abide in my love; even as I have kept my Father's commandments, and abide in his love.</font>
42:15:11: <font color="#CC0000">These things have I spoken unto you, that my joy might remain in you, and that your joy might be full.</font>
42:15:12: <font color="#CC0000">This is my commandment, That ye love one another, as I have loved you.</font>
42:15:13: <font color="#CC0000">Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends.</font>
42:15:14: <font color="#CC0000">Ye are my friends, if ye do whatsoever I command you.</font>
42:15:15: <font color="#CC0000">Henceforth I call you not servants; for the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth: but I have called you friends; for all things that I have heard of my Father I have made known unto you.</font>
42:15:16: <font color="#CC0000">Ye have not chosen me, but I have chosen you, and ordained you, that ye should go and bring forth fruit, and that your fruit should remain: that whatsoever ye shall ask of the Father in my name, he may give it you.</font>
42:15:17: <font color="#CC0000">These things I command you, that ye love one another.</font>
42:15:18: <font color="#CC0000">If the world hate you, ye know that it hated me before it hated you.</font>
42:15:19: <font color="#CC0000">If ye were of the world, the world would love his own: but because ye are not of the world, but I have chosen you out of the world, therefore the world hateth you.</font>
42:15:20: <font color="#CC0000">Remember the word that I said unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord. If they have persecuted me, they will also persecute you; if they have kept my saying, they will keep yours also.</font>
42:15:21: <font color="#CC0000">But all these things will they do unto you for my name's sake, because they know not him that sent me.</font>
42:15:22: <font color="#CC0000">If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had sin: but now they have no cloke for their sin.</font>
42:15:23: <font color="#CC0000">He that hateth me hateth my Father also.</font>
42:15:24: <font color="#CC0000">If I had not done among them the works which none other man did, they had not had sin: but now have they both seen and hated both me and my Father.</font>
42:15:25: <font color="#CC0000">But this cometh to pass, that the word might be fulfilled that is written in their law, They hated me without a cause.</font>
42:15:26: <font color="#CC0000">But when the Comforter is come, whom I will send unto you from the Father, even the Spirit of truth, which proceedeth from the Father, he shall testify of me:</font>
42:15:27: <font color="#CC0000">And ye also shall bear witness, because ye have been with me from the beginning.</font>
42:16:1: <font color="#CC0000">These things have I spoken unto you, that ye should not be offended.</font>
42:16:2: <font color="#CC0000">They shall put you out of the synagogues: yea, the time cometh, that whosoever killeth you will think that he doeth God service.</font>
42:16:3: <font color="#CC0000">And these things will they do unto you, because they have not known the Father, nor me.</font>
42:16:4: <font color="#CC0000">But these things have I told you, that when the time shall come, ye may remember that I told you of them. And these things I said not unto you at the beginning, because I was with you.</font>
42:16:5: <font color="#CC0000">But now I go my way to him that sent me; and none of you asketh me, Whither goest thou?</font>
42:16:6: <font color="#CC0000">But because I have said these things unto you, sorrow hath filled your heart.</font>
42:16:7: <font color="#CC0000">Nevertheless I tell you the truth; It is expedient for you that I go away: for if I go not away, the Comforter will not come unto you; but if I depart, I will send him unto you.</font>
42:16:8: <font color="#CC0000">And when he is come, he will reprove the world of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment:</font>
42:16:9: <font color="#CC0000">Of sin, because they believe not on me;</font>
42:16:10: <font color="#CC0000">Of righteousness, because I go to my Father, and ye see me no more;</font>
42:16:11: <font color="#CC0000">Of judgment, because the prince of this world is judged.</font>
42:16:12: <font color="#CC0000">I have yet many things to say unto you, but ye cannot bear them now.</font>
42:16:13: <font color="#CC0000">Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you into all truth: for he shall not speak of himself; but whatsoever he shall hear, that shall he speak: and he will shew you things to come.</font>
42:16:14: <font color="#CC0000">He shall glorify me: for he shall receive of mine, and shall shew it unto you.</font>
42:16:15: <font color="#CC0000">All things that the Father hath are mine: therefore said I, that he shall take of mine, and shall shew it unto you.</font>
42:16:16: <font color="#CC0000">A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me, because I go to the Father.</font>
42:16:19: Now Jesus knew that they were desirous to ask him, and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Do ye enquire among yourselves of that I said, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me?</font>
42:16:20: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, That ye shall weep and lament, but the world shall rejoice: and ye shall be sorrowful, but your sorrow shall be turned into joy.</font>
42:16:21: <font color="#CC0000">A woman when she is in travail hath sorrow, because her hour is come: but as soon as she is delivered of the child, she remembereth no more the anguish, for joy that a man is born into the world.</font>
42:16:22: <font color="#CC0000">And ye now therefore have sorrow: but I will see you again, and your heart shall rejoice, and your joy no man taketh from you.</font>
42:16:23: <font color="#CC0000">And in that day ye shall ask me nothing. Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whatsoever ye shall ask the Father in my name, he will give it you.</font>
42:16:24: <font color="#CC0000">Hitherto have ye asked nothing in my name: ask, and ye shall receive, that your joy may be full.</font>
42:16:25: <font color="#CC0000">These things have I spoken unto you in proverbs: but the time cometh, when I shall no more speak unto you in proverbs, but I shall shew you plainly of the Father.</font>
42:16:26: <font color="#CC0000">At that day ye shall ask in my name: and I say not unto you, that I will pray the Father for you:</font>
42:16:27: <font color="#CC0000">For the Father himself loveth you, because ye have loved me, and have believed that I came out from God.</font>
42:16:28: <font color="#CC0000">I came forth from the Father, and am come into the world: again, I leave the world, and go to the Father.</font>
42:16:31: Jesus answered them, <font color="#CC0000">Do ye now believe?</font>
42:16:32: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, the hour cometh, yea, is now come, that ye shall be scattered, every man to his own, and shall leave me alone: and yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.</font>
42:16:33: <font color="#CC0000">These things I have spoken unto you, that in me ye might have peace. In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.</font>
42:17:1: These words spake Jesus, and lifted up his eyes to heaven, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Father, the hour is come; glorify thy Son, that thy Son also may glorify thee:</font>
42:17:2: <font color="#CC0000">As thou hast given him power over all flesh, that he should give eternal life to as many as thou hast given him.</font>
42:17:3: <font color="#CC0000">And this is life eternal, that they might know thee the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom thou hast sent.</font>
42:17:4: <font color="#CC0000">I have glorified thee on the earth: I have finished the work which thou gavest me to do.</font>
42:17:5: <font color="#CC0000">And now, O Father, glorify thou me with thine own self with the glory which I had with thee before the world was.</font>
42:17:6: <font color="#CC0000">I have manifested thy name unto the men which thou gavest me out of the world: thine they were, and thou gavest them me; and they have kept thy word.</font>
42:17:7: <font color="#CC0000">Now they have known that all things whatsoever thou hast given me are of thee.</font>
42:17:8: <font color="#CC0000">For I have given unto them the words which thou gavest me; and they have received them, and have known surely that I came out from thee, and they have believed that thou didst send me.</font>
42:17:9: <font color="#CC0000">I pray for them: I pray not for the world, but for them which thou hast given me; for they are thine.</font>
42:17:10: <font color="#CC0000">And all mine are thine, and thine are mine; and I am glorified in them.</font>
42:17:11: <font color="#CC0000">And now I am no more in the world, but these are in the world, and I come to thee. Holy Father, keep through thine own name those whom thou hast given me, that they may be one, as we are.</font>
42:17:12: <font color="#CC0000">While I was with them in the world, I kept them in thy name: those that thou gavest me I have kept, and none of them is lost, but the son of perdition; that the scripture might be fulfilled.</font>
42:17:13: <font color="#CC0000">And now come I to thee; and these things I speak in the world, that they might have my joy fulfilled in themselves.</font>
42:17:14: <font color="#CC0000">I have given them thy word; and the world hath hated them, because they are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.</font>
42:17:15: <font color="#CC0000">I pray not that thou shouldest take them out of the world, but that thou shouldest keep them from the evil.</font>
42:17:16: <font color="#CC0000">They are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.</font>
42:17:17: <font color="#CC0000">Sanctify them through thy truth: thy word is truth.</font>
42:17:18: <font color="#CC0000">As thou hast sent me into the world, even so have I also sent them into the world.</font>
42:17:19: <font color="#CC0000">And for their sakes I sanctify myself, that they also might be sanctified through the truth.</font>
42:17:20: <font color="#CC0000">Neither pray I for these alone, but for them also which shall believe on me through their word;</font>
42:17:21: <font color="#CC0000">That they all may be one; as thou, Father, art in me, and I in thee, that they also may be one in us: that the world may believe that thou hast sent me.</font>
42:17:22: <font color="#CC0000">And the glory which thou gavest me I have given them; that they may be one, even as we are one:</font>
42:17:23: <font color="#CC0000">I in them, and thou in me, that they may be made perfect in one; and that the world may know that thou hast sent me, and hast loved them, as thou hast loved me.</font>
42:17:24: <font color="#CC0000">Father, I will that they also, whom thou hast given me, be with me where I am; that they may behold my glory, which thou hast given me: for thou lovedst me before the foundation of the world.</font>
42:17:25: <font color="#CC0000">O righteous Father, the world hath not known thee: but I have known thee, and these have known that thou hast sent me.</font>
42:17:26: <font color="#CC0000">And I have declared unto them thy name, and will declare it: that the love wherewith thou hast loved me may be in them, and I in them.</font>
42:18:4: Jesus therefore, knowing all things that should come upon him, went forth, and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Whom seek ye?</font>
42:18:5: They answered him, Jesus of Nazareth. Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I am he.</font> And Judas also, which betrayed him, stood with them.
42:18:7: Then asked he them again, <font color="#CC0000">Whom seek ye?</font> And they said, Jesus of Nazareth.
42:18:8: Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">I have told you that I am he: if therefore ye seek me, let these go their way:</font>
42:18:11: Then said Jesus unto Peter, <font color="#CC0000">Put up thy sword into the sheath: the cup which my Father hath given me, shall I not drink it?</font>
42:18:20: Jesus answered him, <font color="#CC0000">I spake openly to the world; I ever taught in the synagogue, and in the temple, whither the Jews always resort; and in secret have I said nothing.</font>
42:18:21: <font color="#CC0000">Why askest thou me? ask them which heard me, what I have said unto them: behold, they know what I said.</font>
42:18:23: Jesus answered him, <font color="#CC0000">If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the evil: but if well, why smitest thou me?</font>
42:18:34: Jesus answered him, <font color="#CC0000">Sayest thou this thing of thyself, or did others tell it thee of me?</font>
42:18:36: Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">My kingdom is not of this world: if my kingdom were of this world, then would my servants fight, that I should not be delivered to the Jews: but now is my kingdom not from hence.</font>
42:18:37: Pilate therefore said unto him, Art thou a king then? Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">Thou sayest that I am a king. To this end was I born, and for this cause came I into the world, that I should bear witness unto the truth. Every one that is of the truth heareth my voice.</font>
42:19:11: Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">Thou couldest have no power at all against me, except it were given thee from above: therefore he that delivered me unto thee hath the greater sin.</font>
42:19:26: When Jesus therefore saw his mother, and the disciple standing by, whom he loved, he saith unto his mother, <font color="#CC0000">Woman, behold thy son!</font>
42:19:27: Then saith he to the disciple, <font color="#CC0000">Behold thy mother!</font> And from that hour that disciple took her unto his own home.
42:19:28: After this, Jesus knowing that all things were now accomplished, that the scripture might be fulfilled, saith, <font color="#CC0000">I thirst.</font>
42:19:30: When Jesus therefore had received the vinegar, he said, <font color="#CC0000">It is finished:</font> and he bowed his head, and gave up the ghost.
42:20:15: Jesus saith unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Woman, why weepest thou? whom seekest thou?</font> She, supposing him to be the gardener, saith unto him, Sir, if thou have borne him hence, tell me where thou hast laid him, and I will take him away.
42:20:16: Jesus saith unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Mary.</font> She turned herself, and saith unto him, Rabboni; which is to say, Master.
42:20:17: Jesus saith unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Touch me not; for I am not yet ascended to my Father: but go to my brethren, and say unto them, I ascend unto my Father, and your Father; and to my God, and your God.</font>
42:20:19: Then the same day at evening, being the first day of the week, when the doors were shut where the disciples were assembled for fear of the Jews, came Jesus and stood in the midst, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Peace be unto you.</font>
42:20:21: Then said Jesus to them again, <font color="#CC0000">Peace be unto you: as my Father hath sent me, even so send I you.</font>
42:20:22: And when he had said this, he breathed on them, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Receive ye the Holy Ghost:</font>
42:20:23: <font color="#CC0000">Whose soever sins ye remit, they are remitted unto them; and whose soever sins ye retain, they are retained.</font>
42:20:26: And after eight days again his disciples were within, and Thomas with them: then came Jesus, the doors being shut, and stood in the midst, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Peace be unto you.</font>
42:20:27: Then saith he to Thomas, <font color="#CC0000">Reach hither thy finger, and behold my hands; and reach hither thy hand, and thrust it into my side: and be not faithless, but believing.</font>
42:20:29: Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thomas, because thou hast seen me, thou hast believed: blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed.</font>
42:21:5: Then Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Children, have ye any meat?</font> They answered him, No.
42:21:6: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Cast the net on the right side of the ship, and ye shall find.</font> They cast therefore, and now they were not able to draw it for the multitude of fishes.
42:21:10: Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Bring of the fish which ye have now caught.</font>
42:21:12: Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Come and dine.</font> And none of the disciples durst ask him, Who art thou? knowing that it was the Lord.
42:21:15: So when they had dined, Jesus saith to Simon Peter, <font color="#CC0000">Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me more than these?</font> He saith unto him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I love thee. He saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Feed my lambs.</font>
42:21:16: He saith to him again the second time, <font color="#CC0000">Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me?</font> He saith unto him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I love thee. He saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Feed my sheep.</font>
42:21:17: He saith unto him the third time, <font color="#CC0000">Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me?</font> Peter was grieved because he said unto him the third time, Lovest thou me? And he said unto him, Lord, thou knowest all things; thou knowest that I love thee. Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Feed my sheep.</font>
42:21:18: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto thee, When thou wast young, thou girdedst thyself, and walkedst whither thou wouldest: but when thou shalt be old, thou shalt stretch forth thy hands, and another shall gird thee, and carry thee whither thou wouldest not.</font>
42:21:19: This spake he, signifying by what death he should glorify God. And when he had spoken this, he saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Follow me.</font>
42:21:22: Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">If I will that he tarry till I come, what is that to thee? follow thou me.</font>
43:1:4: And, being assembled together with them, commanded them that they should not depart from Jerusalem, <font color="#CC0000">but wait for the promise of the Father, which,</font> saith he, <font color="#CC0000">ye have heard of me.</font>
43:1:5: <font color="#CC0000">For John truly baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost not many days hence.</font>
43:1:7: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">It is not for you to know the times or the seasons, which the Father hath put in his own power.</font>
43:1:8: <font color="#CC0000">But ye shall receive power, after that the Holy Ghost is come upon you: and ye shall be witnesses unto me both in Jerusalem, and in all Judæa, and in Samaria, and unto the uttermost part of the earth.</font>
43:9:4: And he fell to the earth, and heard a voice saying unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?</font>
43:9:5: And he said, Who art thou, Lord? And the Lord said, <font color="#CC0000">I am Jesus whom thou persecutest: it is hard for thee to kick against the pricks.</font>
43:9:6: And he trembling and astonished said, Lord, what wilt thou have me to do? And the Lord said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Arise, and go into the city, and it shall be told thee what thou must do.</font>
43:9:10: And there was a certain disciple at Damascus, named Ananias; and to him said the Lord in a vision, <font color="#CC0000">Ananias.</font> And he said, Behold, I am here, Lord.
43:9:11: And the Lord said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Arise, and go into the street which is called Straight, and enquire in the house of Judas for one called Saul, of Tarsus: for, behold, he prayeth,</font>
43:9:12: <font color="#CC0000">And hath seen in a vision a man named Ananias coming in, and putting his hand on him, that he might receive his sight.</font>
43:9:15: But the Lord said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Go thy way: for he is a chosen vessel unto me, to bear my name before the Gentiles, and kings, and the children of Israel:</font>
43:9:16: <font color="#CC0000">For I will shew him how great things he must suffer for my name's sake.</font>
43:11:16: Then remembered I the word of the Lord, how that he said, <font color="#CC0000">John indeed baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost.</font>
43:18:9: Then spake the Lord to Paul in the night by a vision, <font color="#CC0000">Be not afraid, but speak, and hold not thy peace:</font>
43:18:10: <font color="#CC0000">For I am with thee, and no man shall set on thee to hurt thee: for I have much people in this city.</font>
43:20:35: I have shewed you all things, how that so labouring ye ought to support the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, how he said, <font color="#CC0000">It is more blessed to give than to receive.</font>
43:22:7: And I fell unto the ground, and heard a voice saying unto me, <font color="#CC0000">Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?</font>
43:22:8: And I answered, Who art thou, Lord? And he said unto me, <font color="#CC0000">I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom thou persecutest.</font>
43:22:10: And I said, What shall I do, Lord? And the Lord said unto me, <font color="#CC0000">Arise, and go into Damascus; and there it shall be told thee of all things which are appointed for thee to do.</font>
43:22:18: And saw him saying unto me, <font color="#CC0000">Make haste, and get thee quickly out of Jerusalem: for they will not receive thy testimony concerning me.</font>
43:22:21: And he said unto me, <font color="#CC0000">Depart: for I will send thee far hence unto the Gentiles.</font>
43:23:11: And the night following the Lord stood by him, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Be of good cheer, Paul: for as thou hast testified of me in Jerusalem, so must thou bear witness also at Rome.</font>
43:26:14: And when we were all fallen to the earth, I heard a voice speaking unto me, and saying in the Hebrew tongue, <font color="#CC0000">Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me? it is hard for thee to kick against the pricks.</font>
43:26:15: And I said, Who art thou, Lord? And he said, <font color="#CC0000">I am Jesus whom thou persecutest.</font>
43:26:16: <font color="#CC0000">But rise, and stand upon thy feet: for I have appeared unto thee for this purpose, to make thee a minister and a witness both of these things which thou hast seen, and of those things in the which I will appear unto thee;</font>
43:26:17: <font color="#CC0000">Delivering thee from the people, and from the Gentiles, unto whom now I send thee,</font>
43:26:18: <font color="#CC0000">To open their eyes, and to turn them from darkness to light, and from the power of Satan unto God, that they may receive forgiveness of sins, and inheritance among them which are sanctified by faith that is in me.</font>
45:11:24: And when he had given thanks, he brake it, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Take, eat: this is my body, which is broken for you: this do in remembrance of me.</font>
45:11:25: After the same manner also he took the cup, when he had supped, saying, <font color="#CC0000">This cup is the new testament in my blood: this do ye, as oft as ye drink it, in remembrance of me.</font>
46:12:9: And he said unto me, <font color="#CC0000">My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness.</font> Most gladly therefore will I rather glory in my infirmities, that the power of Christ may rest upon me.
65:1:8: <font color="#CC0000">I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the ending,</font> saith the Lord, which is, and which was, and which is to come, the Almighty.
65:1:11: Saying, <font color="#CC0000">I am Alpha and Omega, the first and the last:</font> and, <font color="#CC0000">What thou seest, write in a book, and send it unto the seven churches which are in Asia; unto Ephesus, and unto Smyrna, and unto Pergamos, and unto Thyatira, and unto Sardis, and unto Philadelphia, and unto Laodicea.</font>
65:1:17: And when I saw him, I fell at his feet as dead. And he laid his right hand upon me, saying unto me, <font color="#CC0000">Fear not; I am the first and the last:</font>
65:1:18: <font color="#CC0000">I am he that liveth, and was dead; and, behold, I am alive for evermore, Amen; and have the keys of hell and of death.</font>
65:1:19: <font color="#CC0000">Write the things which thou hast seen, and the things which are, and the things which shall be hereafter;</font>
65:1:20: <font color="#CC0000">The mystery of the seven stars which thou sawest in my right hand, and the seven golden candlesticks. The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches: and the seven candlesticks which thou sawest are the seven churches.</font>
65:2:1: <font color="#CC0000">Unto the angel of the church of Ephesus write; These things saith he that holdeth the seven stars in his right hand, who walketh in the midst of the seven golden candlesticks;</font>
65:2:2: <font color="#CC0000">I know thy works, and thy labour, and thy patience, and how thou canst not bear them which are evil: and thou hast tried them which say they are apostles, and are not, and hast found them liars:</font>
65:2:3: <font color="#CC0000">And hast borne, and hast patience, and for my name's sake hast laboured, and hast not fainted.</font>
65:2:4: <font color="#CC0000">Nevertheless I have somewhat against thee, because thou hast left thy first love.</font>
65:2:5: <font color="#CC0000">Remember therefore from whence thou art fallen, and repent, and do the first works; or else I will come unto thee quickly, and will remove thy candlestick out of his place, except thou repent.</font>
65:2:6: <font color="#CC0000">But this thou hast, that thou hatest the deeds of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate.</font>
65:2:7: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the churches; To him that overcometh will I give to eat of the tree of life, which is in the midst of the paradise of God.</font>
65:2:8: <font color="#CC0000">And unto the angel of the church in Smyrna write; These things saith the first and the last, which was dead, and is alive;</font>
65:2:9: <font color="#CC0000">I know thy works, and tribulation, and poverty, (but thou art rich) and I know the blasphemy of them which say they are Jews, and are not, but are the synagogue of Satan.</font>
65:2:10: <font color="#CC0000">Fear none of those things which thou shalt suffer: behold, the devil shall cast some of you into prison, that ye may be tried; and ye shall have tribulation ten days: be thou faithful unto death, and I will give thee a crown of life.</font>
65:2:11: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the churches; He that overcometh shall not be hurt of the second death.</font>
65:2:12: <font color="#CC0000">And to the angel of the church in Pergamos write; These things saith he which hath the sharp sword with two edges;</font>
65:2:13: <font color="#CC0000">I know thy works, and where thou dwellest, even where Satan's seat is: and thou holdest fast my name, and hast not denied my faith, even in those days wherein Antipas was my faithful martyr, who was slain among you, where Satan dwelleth.</font>
65:2:14: <font color="#CC0000">But I have a few things against thee, because thou hast there them that hold the doctrine of Balaam, who taught Balac to cast a stumblingblock before the children of Israel, to eat things sacrificed unto idols, and to commit fornication.</font>
65:2:15: <font color="#CC0000">So hast thou also them that hold the doctrine of the Nicolaitans, which thing I hate.</font>
65:2:16: <font color="#CC0000">Repent; or else I will come unto thee quickly, and will fight against them with the sword of my mouth.</font>
65:2:17: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the churches; To him that overcometh will I give to eat of the hidden manna, and will give him a white stone, and in the stone a new name written, which no man knoweth saving he that receiveth it.</font>
65:2:18: <font color="#CC0000">And unto the angel of the church in Thyatira write; These things saith the Son of God, who hath his eyes like unto a flame of fire, and his feet are like fine brass;</font>
65:2:19: <font color="#CC0000">I know thy works, and charity, and service, and faith, and thy patience, and thy works; and the last to be more than the first.</font>
65:2:20: <font color="#CC0000">Notwithstanding I have a few things against thee, because thou sufferest that woman Jezebel, which calleth herself a prophetess, to teach and to seduce my servants to commit fornication, and to eat things sacrificed unto idols.</font>
65:2:21: <font color="#CC0000">And I gave her space to repent of her fornication; and she repented not.</font>
65:2:22: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, I will cast her into a bed, and them that commit adultery with her into great tribulation, except they repent of their deeds.</font>
65:2:23: <font color="#CC0000">And I will kill her children with death; and all the churches shall know that I am he which searcheth the reins and hearts: and I will give unto every one of you according to your works.</font>
65:2:24: <font color="#CC0000">But unto you I say, and unto the rest in Thyatira, as many as have not this doctrine, and which have not known the depths of Satan, as they speak; I will put upon you none other burden.</font>
65:2:25: <font color="#CC0000">But that which ye have already hold fast till I come.</font>
65:2:26: <font color="#CC0000">And he that overcometh, and keepeth my works unto the end, to him will I give power over the nations:</font>
65:2:27: <font color="#CC0000">And he shall rule them with a rod of iron; as the vessels of a potter shall they be broken to shivers: even as I received of my Father.</font>
65:2:28: <font color="#CC0000">And I will give him the morning star.</font>
65:2:29: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the churches.</font>
65:3:1: <font color="#CC0000">And unto the angel of the church in Sardis write; These things saith he that hath the seven Spirits of God, and the seven stars; I know thy works, that thou hast a name that thou livest, and art dead.</font>
65:3:2: <font color="#CC0000">Be watchful, and strengthen the things which remain, that are ready to die: for I have not found thy works perfect before God.</font>
65:3:3: <font color="#CC0000">Remember therefore how thou hast received and heard, and hold fast, and repent. If therefore thou shalt not watch, I will come on thee as a thief, and thou shalt not know what hour I will come upon thee.</font>
65:3:4: <font color="#CC0000">Thou hast a few names even in Sardis which have not defiled their garments; and they shall walk with me in white: for they are worthy.</font>
65:3:5: <font color="#CC0000">He that overcometh, the same shall be clothed in white raiment; and I will not blot out his name out of the book of life, but I will confess his name before my Father, and before his angels.</font>
65:3:6: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the churches.</font>
65:3:7: <font color="#CC0000">And to the angel of the church in Philadelphia write; These things saith he that is holy, he that is true, he that hath the key of David, he that openeth, and no man shutteth; and shutteth, and no man openeth;</font>
65:3:8: <font color="#CC0000">I know thy works: behold, I have set before thee an open door, and no man can shut it: for thou hast a little strength, and hast kept my word, and hast not denied my name.</font>
65:3:9: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, I will make them of the synagogue of Satan, which say they are Jews, and are not, but do lie; behold, I will make them to come and worship before thy feet, and to know that I have loved thee.</font>
65:3:10: <font color="#CC0000">Because thou hast kept the word of my patience, I also will keep thee from the hour of temptation, which shall come upon all the world, to try them that dwell upon the earth.</font>
65:3:11: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, I come quickly: hold that fast which thou hast, that no man take thy crown.</font>
65:3:12: <font color="#CC0000">Him that overcometh will I make a pillar in the temple of my God, and he shall go no more out: and I will write upon him the name of my God, and the name of the city of my God, which is new Jerusalem, which cometh down out of heaven from my God: and I will write upon him my new name.</font>
65:3:13: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the churches.</font>
65:3:14: <font color="#CC0000">And unto the angel of the church of the Laodiceans write; These things saith the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the beginning of the creation of God;</font>
65:3:15: <font color="#CC0000">I know thy works, that thou art neither cold nor hot: I would thou wert cold or hot.</font>
65:3:16: <font color="#CC0000">So then because thou art lukewarm, and neither cold nor hot, I will spue thee out of my mouth.</font>
65:3:17: <font color="#CC0000">Because thou sayest, I am rich, and increased with goods, and have need of nothing; and knowest not that thou art wretched, and miserable, and poor, and blind, and naked:</font>
65:3:18: <font color="#CC0000">I counsel thee to buy of me gold tried in the fire, that thou mayest be rich; and white raiment, that thou mayest be clothed, and that the shame of thy nakedness do not appear; and anoint thine eyes with eyesalve, that thou mayest see.</font>
65:3:19: <font color="#CC0000">As many as I love, I rebuke and chasten: be zealous therefore, and repent.</font>
65:3:20: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, I stand at the door, and knock: if any man hear my voice, and open the door, I will come in to him, and will sup with him, and he with me.</font>
65:3:21: <font color="#CC0000">To him that overcometh will I grant to sit with me in my throne, even as I also overcame, and am set down with my Father in his throne.</font>
65:3:22: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the churches.</font>
65:22:7: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, I come quickly: blessed is he that keepeth the sayings of the prophecy of this book.</font>
65:22:12: <font color="#CC0000">And, behold, I come quickly; and my reward is with me, to give every man according as his work shall be.</font>
65:22:13: <font color="#CC0000">I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end, the first and the last.</font>
65:22:16: <font color="#CC0000">I Jesus have sent mine angel to testify unto you these things in the churches. I am the root and the offspring of David, and the bright and morning star.</font>
65:22:20: He which testifieth these things saith, <font color="#CC0000">Surely I come quickly.</font> Amen. Even so, come, Lord Jesus.
